title: Fix raw .to_json leaks — Project in HAML, User in Devise views
description:
Title: Fix raw .to_json leaks — Project in HAML, User in Devise views

Description:
@component.project.to_json appears in 3 HAML templates, leaking all Project columns into the DOM. current_user.to_json in Devise profile/password pages leaks all User columns including encrypted_password. Both must use Blueprints. Security issue today, architecture issue for migration.
Design doc: docs/research/2026-06-05-api-completeness-analysis.md §15

Files:
- Modify: app/views/components/show.html.haml (use ProjectBlueprint)
- Modify: app/views/components/triage.html.haml (use ProjectBlueprint)
- Modify: app/views/components/settings.html.haml (use ProjectBlueprint)
- Modify: app/views/devise/registrations/edit.html.haml (use UserBlueprint)
- Modify: app/views/devise/registrations/edit_password.html.haml (use UserBlueprint)
- Test: spec/system/component_data_leaks_spec.rb

First failing test:
expect(page.body).not_to include(project.to_json) when visiting component show page

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] No .to_json or .as_json calls in any HAML template
- [ ] Project data in HAML uses ProjectBlueprint render
- [ ] User data in Devise views uses UserBlueprint render
- [ ] DOM does not contain encrypted_password, reset_password_token, or database credentials
- [ ] Grep confirms zero .to_json calls in app/views/
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
grep -r '\.to_json\|\.as_json' app/views/ | grep -v node_modules && bundle exec rspec spec/system/component_data_leaks_spec.rb

Decision points:
- none — this is a security fix

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT just add field filtering to .to_json — use Blueprint
- Do NOT add rubocop:disable to work around warnings

NOT in scope:
- Converting HAML data injection to API calls (that's Vue Router migration)
- JavaScript .to_json calls (different concern)

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC checkbox — verify with evidence
- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated
- [ ] Run the exact Verification command — paste output
- [ ] git diff shows ONLY files listed in Files section

Story points: sp:3
Estimate: 12 min

title: Fix UserBlueprint admin view — replace raw .as_json in UsersController
description:
Title: Fix UserBlueprint admin view — replace raw .as_json in UsersController

Description:
UsersController renders ALL user JSON via raw .as_json with a hand-built USER_JSON_FIELDS list. This bypasses Blueprinter entirely and risks leaking sensitive columns (encrypted_password, reset_password_token) if the field list drifts. Create a UserBlueprint :admin view and use it everywhere UsersController renders JSON.
Design doc: docs/research/2026-06-05-api-completeness-analysis.md §15

Files:
- Modify: app/blueprints/user_blueprint.rb (add :admin view)
- Modify: app/controllers/users_controller.rb (replace .as_json with Blueprint)
- Modify: app/controllers/application_controller.rb (locked_users uses Blueprint)
- Test: spec/requests/users_spec.rb (verify response shape)

First failing test:
expect(UserBlueprint.render_as_hash(user, view: :admin)).to include(:locked_at, :failed_attempts)

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] UserBlueprint :admin view includes all fields from USER_JSON_FIELDS
- [ ] UsersController#index uses UserBlueprint :admin view
- [ ] UsersController#update uses UserBlueprint :admin view
- [ ] ApplicationController locked_users uses UserBlueprint
- [ ] Response does NOT include encrypted_password, reset_password_token, or other Devise internals
- [ ] USER_JSON_FIELDS constant removed (Blueprint is source of truth)
- [ ] OpenAPI UserAdminResponse schema matches Blueprint output
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
bundle exec rspec spec/requests/users_spec.rb && yarn openapi:bundle && yarn openapi:lint

Decision points:
- none

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT use .as_json or .to_json anywhere — Blueprint only
- Do NOT add rubocop:disable to work around warnings

NOT in scope:
- Admin users namespace routing (v2-btu.28)
- User profile/registration endpoints

title: Add effective_permissions to project/component JSON responses
description:
Title: Add effective_permissions to project/component JSON responses

Description:
The effective_permissions string ('admin', 'author', 'viewer', nil) is injected via HAML on 7 pages and controls all authorization UI (edit buttons, review actions, admin overrides). Add it to ProjectBlueprint and ComponentBlueprint so API responses are self-contained. Requires current_user context in Blueprint rendering.
Design doc: docs/research/2026-06-05-api-completeness-analysis.md §3

Files:
- Modify: app/blueprints/project_blueprint.rb (add effective_permissions field)
- Modify: app/blueprints/component_blueprint.rb (add effective_permissions field)
- Modify: app/controllers/projects_controller.rb (pass current_user to Blueprint options)
- Modify: app/controllers/components_controller.rb (pass current_user to Blueprint options)
- Test: spec/requests/projects_spec.rb (verify permissions in response)
- Test: spec/requests/components_show_spec.rb (verify permissions in response)

First failing test:
expect(JSON.parse(response.body)['effective_permissions']).to eq('admin') when user is project admin

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] GET /projects/:id JSON includes effective_permissions for current user
- [ ] GET /components/:id JSON includes effective_permissions for current user
- [ ] effective_permissions is nil when user is not a project member
- [ ] effective_permissions reflects actual role: admin, reviewer, author, viewer
- [ ] Blueprint uses options[:current_user] — no global state
- [ ] OpenAPI schemas updated with effective_permissions field
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
bundle exec rspec spec/requests/projects_spec.rb spec/requests/components_show_spec.rb && yarn openapi:bundle && yarn openapi:lint

Decision points:
- Include in all views or only :show/:editor? (Recommendation: :show and :editor only — index doesn't need per-item permissions)

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT use Thread.current or global state for current_user — pass via Blueprint options
- Do NOT add rubocop:disable to work around warnings

NOT in scope:
- Per-action permission matrix (that's role-based auth, not API data)
- Removing HAML-injected permissions (that's a Vue Router migration card)

title: Add backup/restore integration tests for merge engine — round-trip + format compatibility
description:
Title: Add backup/restore integration tests for merge engine — round-trip + format compatibility

Description:
The merge engine reads from backup archives (BackupSerializer) and writes through the import
pipeline (RuleBuilder, ReviewBuilder, SatisfactionBuilder). Expert review found 3 integration
gaps that could cause silent data loss or corruption during merge: satisfactions.json dropped
from flat archives, inspec_control_file handled inconsistently (imported verbatim but regenerated
on round-trip), and EXCLUDED_RULE_COLUMNS has no structural link to MERGEABLE_FIELDS.
The existing backup_round_trip_spec must be extended to cover the merge path.
Design doc: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-24-component-sync-merge.md §4.3, §18

Files:
- Modify: spec/services/import/integration/backup_round_trip_spec.rb (extend with merge path)
- Modify: app/services/export/serializers/backup_serializer.rb (cross-reference MERGEABLE_FIELDS)
- Modify: app/services/import/json_archive/satisfaction_builder.rb (handle flat + nested archive)
- Create: spec/services/import/integration/merge_round_trip_spec.rb (export → merge-analyze → compare)
- Test: spec/services/import/integration/merge_round_trip_spec.rb

First failing test:
"export → merge-analyze → compare produces zero conflicts for identical components"

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] MergeInput.from_json_archive parses satisfactions.json from both nested and flat archives
- [ ] inspec_control_file excluded from merge diffing (derived column — regenerated after apply)
- [ ] BackupSerializer.EXCLUDED_RULE_COLUMNS has inline cross-reference to Rule::MERGEABLE_FIELDS
- [ ] merge_round_trip_spec: export component A → export component A again → analyze diff = zero conflicts
- [ ] merge_round_trip_spec: export component A → modify 3 fields → export again → analyze diff = 3 changes
- [ ] merge_round_trip_spec: export with reviews → analyze → review match count equals original count
- [ ] merge_round_trip_spec: export with satisfactions → analyze → satisfaction match count equals original
- [ ] Existing backup_round_trip_spec still passes unchanged
- [ ] All work via TDD
- [ ] No regressions

Verification:
bundle exec rspec spec/services/import/integration/backup_round_trip_spec.rb spec/services/import/integration/merge_round_trip_spec.rb

Decision points:
- Should merge_round_trip_spec use the real Analyzer or test MergeInput parsing separately?
  Real Analyzer — the integration test must prove the full pipeline works end-to-end.
- Should inspec_control_file be in a DERIVED_COLUMNS constant?
  Yes — extract Rule::DERIVED_COLUMNS = %w[inspec_control_file] alongside MERGEABLE_FIELDS.

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT test merge input parsing in isolation only — the round-trip IS the integration test
- Do NOT silently drop satisfactions.json — raise or warn if the archive structure is unexpected
- Do NOT add rubocop:disable/eslint-disable to work around warnings — fix the root cause
- Do NOT assume the archive format is stable — validate manifest version before parsing

NOT in scope:
- MergeApplier write path (card .8)
- Archive format changes (separate card if needed)
- Changing BackupSerializer output format

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC checkbox — verify with evidence
- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated
- [ ] Run the exact Verification command — paste output
- [ ] git diff shows ONLY files listed in Files section
- [ ] Zero new linter disable comments in the diff

Story points: sp:5
Estimate: 20 min

title: Add error handling — PreconditionError rescue + rollback strategy + rake exit codes
description:
Title: Add error handling — PreconditionError rescue + rollback strategy + rake exit codes

Description:
3 findings: (1) CRITICAL: PreconditionError produces 500 for UI callers — need rescue_from in
controller returning structured error. (2) CRITICAL: MergeApplier mid-write rollback incomplete
for quarantine path — need transaction wrapping with quarantine as a separate post-rollback step.
(3) WARNING: Rake CLI exit code undefined — define 0=clean, 1=conflicts, 2=error convention.
Also: Heroku 30s timeout kills MergeJob with no cleanup — need hard timeout + partial progress save.

Files:
- Modify: Plan doc (update error handling design)
- Modify: app/controllers/ (rescue_from Import::PreconditionError when implemented)

First failing test:
"PreconditionError returns structured 422 JSON, not 500"

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] rescue_from Import::PreconditionError → 422 with error details
- [ ] MergeApplier wraps writes in transaction — quarantine is post-rollback
- [ ] Rake exit codes: 0=clean, 1=conflicts found, 2=error
- [ ] Timeout handling: configurable hard timeout with partial progress save
- [ ] ComponentSyncEvent created even for failed/timeout merges
- [ ] All work via TDD
- [ ] No regressions

Verification:
bundle exec rspec spec/services/import/merge/ spec/lib/tasks/sync_spec.rb

Decision points:
- Hard timeout value? (300s for CLI, 25s for web — Heroku safe)

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT let PreconditionError surface as 500
- Do NOT add rubocop:disable

NOT in scope:
- MergeJob implementation (card .9)

title: Security hardening — resolution_log size limit + PII handling + input validation
description:
Title: Security hardening — resolution_log size limit + PII handling + input validation

Description:
8 security findings: (1) CRITICAL: resolution_log_json has no size constraint — DOS via 100K entries.
(2) HMAC optional by default — unsigned archives fully trusted. (3) PII exposure via user emails
in archives. (4) Forged review attribution via crafted user_email. (5) XSS via imported comment
text in merge preview. (6) external_id manipulation for match outcome control. (7) Zip bomb defense
must be explicitly reused. (8) Strategy enum not validated from CLI input.

Files:
- Modify: Plan doc (update security requirements)
- Test: spec/services/import/merge/ (security-focused tests)

First failing test:
"resolution_log rejects entries beyond size limit"

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] resolution_log_json capped at 10MB or 10K entries (whichever first)
- [ ] HMAC verification ON by default for production, OFF only for dev
- [ ] Archive PII documented — emails are inherent to the data model
- [ ] User attribution resolved via User.find_by(email:) — unknown emails → system user
- [ ] Imported comment/rule text sanitized before merge preview rendering
- [ ] external_id used for tiebreak ONLY, not primary matching
- [ ] Zip bomb defense reused from JsonArchiveImporter
- [ ] Strategy values validated against VALID_RESOLUTIONS constant
- [ ] All work via TDD
- [ ] No regressions

Verification:
bundle exec rspec spec/services/import/merge/

Decision points:
- HMAC on by default in prod? (Yes — defense-in-depth)

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT trust imported data without validation
- Do NOT add rubocop:disable

NOT in scope:
- PII anonymization (separate card if needed)

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC
- [ ] Run verification command

Story points: sp:3
Estimate: 15 min

title: Add FK constraints to rule_satisfactions — prevent dangling bigint rows
description:
Title: Add FK constraints to rule_satisfactions — prevent dangling bigint rows on merge

Description:
CRITICAL: rule_satisfactions has NO DB-level FK constraints. Both rule_id and satisfied_by_rule_id
are bare bigints with a unique index but no foreign key to base_rules. Merge applier can insert
rows where the satisfier rule doesn't exist. Also needed: MergeApplier must validate both sides
of every satisfaction pair resolve to known DB IDs before inserting.

Files:
- Create: db/migrate/NEXT_add_rule_satisfaction_foreign_keys.rb
- Modify: db/schema.rb
- Test: spec/models/ (FK constraint tests)

First failing test:
"DB rejects rule_satisfaction with nonexistent satisfied_by_rule_id"

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] FK on rule_satisfactions.rule_id → base_rules.id (on_delete: :cascade)
- [ ] FK on rule_satisfactions.satisfied_by_rule_id → base_rules.id (on_delete: :cascade)
- [ ] 2-pass Strong Migrations pattern (validate:false + disable_ddl_transaction)
- [ ] Backfill: delete orphan rows before adding constraints
- [ ] All work via TDD
- [ ] No regressions

Verification:
bundle exec rspec spec/models/ && bin/rails db:migrate:redo

Decision points:
- on_delete: :cascade or :restrict? (cascade — matches reviews FK pattern)

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT add constraints without backfilling orphans first
- Do NOT add rubocop:disable

NOT in scope:
- MergeApplier quarantine logic (card .8)

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC
- [ ] Run verification command

Story points: sp:2
Estimate: 10 min

title: Fix RuleFieldDiffer design — assign_attributes fires callbacks + N+1 queries
description:
Title: Fix RuleFieldDiffer design — assign_attributes fires callbacks + N+1 queries

Description:
2 CRITICAL: (1) assign_attributes fires before_validation callbacks including clear_stale_foreign_keys
— the differ will mutate FK fields as a side effect of diffing. Use dup.assign_attributes or
compare raw hashes instead. (2) N+1 queries if nested associations (disa_rule_descriptions,
checks, satisfies) are not in the eager-load list. The plan's eager-load at Analyzer line 1
must include ALL associations that RuleFieldDiffer accesses.

Files:
- Modify: Plan doc (update RuleFieldDiffer design to avoid callbacks)
- Test: spec/services/import/merge/rule_field_differ_spec.rb

First failing test:
"RuleFieldDiffer does not trigger model callbacks during diff"

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Diffing does NOT fire before_validation or any other callback
- [ ] Use hash comparison or dup.assign_attributes pattern
- [ ] Eager-load list includes all associations accessed during diff
- [ ] Test verifies no callbacks fire during diff operation
- [ ] All work via TDD
- [ ] No regressions

Verification:
bundle exec rspec spec/services/import/merge/

Decision points:
- dup.assign_attributes or raw hash comparison? (hash comparison preferred — no AR overhead)

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT use assign_attributes on the live AR object for diffing
- Do NOT add rubocop:disable

NOT in scope:
- Changing the diff algorithm

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC
- [ ] Run verification command

Story points: sp:2
Estimate: 8 min

title: Fix BackupSerializer created_at precision — pre-Phase-1 blocker for merge
description:
Title: Fix BackupSerializer created_at precision — pre-Phase-1 blocker for merge

Description:
CRITICAL (flagged by 3 independent agents): BackupSerializer line 219 writes review.created_at
with iso8601 (second precision) while updated_at uses iso8601(6) (microsecond). The ReviewMatcher
composite key depends on microsecond precision. Two reviews on the same rule within the same
second with identical comment text ('Acknowledged', '+1') will false-match. Archives already
in production carry second-precision timestamps. Must fix BEFORE any merge analysis runs.

Files:
- Modify: app/services/export/serializers/backup_serializer.rb (line 219: iso8601 → iso8601(6))
- Test: spec/services/export/serializers/backup_serializer_spec.rb

First failing test:
"serialize_review emits created_at with microsecond precision"

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] created_at serialized with iso8601(6) matching updated_at
- [ ] Manifest version bump so v1.0 archives use digest-heavy fallback
- [ ] Regression test verifying microsecond precision
- [ ] All work via TDD
- [ ] No regressions

Verification:
bundle exec rspec spec/services/export/serializers/backup_serializer_spec.rb

Decision points:
- None — straightforward fix

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT defer this to a separate card — it's a pre-Phase-1 blocker
- Do NOT add rubocop:disable

NOT in scope:
- Changing the merge algorithm itself

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC
- [ ] Run verification command

Estimate: 5 min

title: Add Vue Router 3 — per-page rule selection routing with history
description:
Title: Add Vue Router 3 — global install in all packs with per-page route definitions

Description:
Install vue-router@3 (Vue 2.7 compatible) globally via createVulcanApp so every pack gets a
router instance. Pages that need sub-routing (editor, triage, DISA guide) define routes.
Pages that don't get a single root route — zero overhead, ready when needed. Hash mode
(no server config changes). Route params for rule selection. Browser back/forward via
router history. Navigation guards for unsaved changes. Breadcrumbs from route meta.
Replaces manual window.history.replaceState in ProjectComponent.vue.

Files:
- Modify: package.json (add vue-router@3)
- Modify: app/javascript/lib/createVulcanApp.js (create router, pass to Vue instance)
- Create: app/javascript/router/componentEditor.js (editor route definitions)
- Create: app/javascript/router/triagePage.js (triage route definitions)
- Create: app/javascript/router/defaultRoutes.js (single root route for simple pages)
- Modify: all 21 pack files (pass route config to createVulcanApp)
- Modify: app/javascript/components/components/ProjectComponent.vue (use $route.params)
- Modify: app/javascript/components/rules/RulesCodeEditorView.vue (use $route.params)
- Test: spec/javascript/router/componentEditor.spec.js

First failing test:
"navigating to #/rules/000020 selects rule 000020"

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] vue-router@3 installed
- [ ] createVulcanApp creates router instance for every pack
- [ ] Hash-mode router (no server config needed)
- [ ] Editor routes: #/rules/:ruleId for rule selection
- [ ] Triage routes: #/comments/:commentId for comment deep-link
- [ ] Simple pages: single root route (zero overhead)
- [ ] Browser back/forward navigates rule history
- [ ] beforeRouteLeave guard warns on unsaved changes
- [ ] Route meta includes breadcrumb data
- [ ] queriedRule server prop migrated to router initial route
- [ ] window.history.replaceState removed (router handles it)
- [ ] Vue 3 forward-compatible (vue-router@3 API matches @4)
- [ ] All work via TDD
- [ ] No regressions

Verification:
yarn test:unit && yarn build && yarn lint:ci

Decision points:
- Route config per-pack: inline in createVulcanApp call or separate router/ files?
  Separate files — keeps pack files thin, routes are testable independently.

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT use history mode (requires server-side catch-all route)
- Do NOT skip simple pages — give them a root route so router is available if needed later
- Do NOT add eslint-disable

NOT in scope:
- Pinia store (separate card, depends on this)
- Consumer component migration (card .4)

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC
- [ ] Playwright: URL updates on rule select, back button works
- [ ] All 21 packs verified to mount with router

Story points: sp:5
Estimate: 25 min
notes: [2026-06-05] Install vue-router globally in createVulcanApp. Every pack gets a router instance. Pages that need sub-routing (editor, triage) define routes. Pages that don't get a single root route — zero overhead, ready when needed.

title: Remove Turbolinks — mechanical migration of 31 files
description:
Title: Remove Turbolinks — mechanical migration of 31 files

Description:
Remove turbolinks gem + npm packages. Change all 21 pack files from turbolinks:load to
DOMContentLoaded, remove vue-turbolinks adapter imports. Clean createVulcanApp factory
(remove adapter + $reset listener). Remove data-turbolinks attributes from 5 Vue components
+ 2 HAML templates. Remove Devise Turbolinks config. Delete 2 Turbolinks-specific tests.
Full audit completed — zero architectural risk, zero behavior change beyond full page reloads.

Files:
- Modify: Gemfile (remove gem 'turbolinks')
- Modify: package.json (remove turbolinks + vue-turbolinks)
- Modify: app/javascript/packs/application.js (remove require turbolinks)
- Modify: app/javascript/lib/createVulcanApp.js (remove adapter + $reset)
- Modify: 21 pack files (turbolinks:load → DOMContentLoaded, remove adapter)
- Modify: app/javascript/utils/disaGuideInit.js (event listener)
- Modify: 5 Vue components (remove data-turbolinks="false")
- Modify: app/views/layouts/application.html.haml (remove track attributes)
- Modify: app/views/users/_settings_nav.html.haml (remove turbolinks: false)
- Modify: config/initializers/devise.rb (remove commented section)
- Delete: 2 Turbolinks-specific tests
- Test: full suite must pass

First failing test:
"all 21 pack files mount Vue on DOMContentLoaded"

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] gem 'turbolinks' removed from Gemfile

title: Fix migration safety — backfill stale data + 2-pass constraints + NULL gap
description:
Title: Fix migration safety — backfill stale data + 2-pass constraints + NULL gap

Description:
Migration 20260604185410 has 3 issues found by 10-agent expert review: (1) CRITICAL: no backfill
SQL before CHECK constraints — prod has known stale FK data (826 rules), migration will abort;
(2) add_check_constraint without validate:false holds ACCESS EXCLUSIVE lock, violates our own
2-pass Strong Migrations pattern; (3) SQL equality check has NULL gap — triage_status IS NULL +
non-null FK passes the constraint, defeating defense-in-depth purpose.

Files:
- Modify: db/migrate/20260604185410_add_review_fk_check_constraints.rb (backfill + validate:false)
- Create: db/migrate/NEXT_validate_review_fk_check_constraints.rb (disable_ddl_transaction! + validate)
- Modify: db/schema.rb (updated constraint expressions)
- Test: spec/models/reviews_spec.rb (add NULL triage_status + non-null FK test)

First failing test:
"CHECK constraint rejects NULL triage_status with non-null duplicate_of_review_id"

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Migration backfills stale FKs before adding constraints
- [ ] Constraints added with validate: false (no ACCESS EXCLUSIVE lock on existing rows)
- [ ] Separate migration validates constraints with disable_ddl_transaction!
- [ ] Constraint expressions use IS NOT DISTINCT FROM for NULL-safe equality
- [ ] Test verifies NULL triage_status + non-null FK is rejected by DB
- [ ] Migration runs successfully on a DB with known stale FK data
- [ ] All work via TDD
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
bundle exec rspec spec/models/reviews_spec.rb && bin/rails db:migrate:redo VERSION=20260604185410

Decision points:
- Confirm backfill SQL is safe for prod (no side effects on existing data)

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT add constraints without backfilling first
- Do NOT use standard equality when NULL is possible in the column
- Do NOT hold ACCESS EXCLUSIVE lock during validation of existing rows

NOT in scope:
- Changes to the Review model callbacks
- Changes to controller actions

title: Fix Rule#update_inspec_code — update_column + remove recursion guard + fix no-dirty-save
description:
Title: Fix Rule#update_inspec_code — update_column + remove recursion guard + fix no-dirty-save

Description:
Replace bare save (which silently swallows validation failures) with update_column for the derived
inspec_control_file column. This eliminates three issues: (1) silent failure leaving InSpec control
stale while user sees success, (2) double-firing of sort_ident/apply_audit_comment on the second
save pass, (3) the skip_update_inspec_code recursion guard flag that persists across saves.
Also fix RuleSatisfactionsController which relies on a no-dirty-attribute save! to trigger the
after_save callback — call update_inspec_code directly instead.
Design doc: .beads/research/callback-stabilization-design.md

Files:
- Modify: app/models/rule.rb
- Modify: app/controllers/rule_satisfactions_controller.rb
- Test: spec/models/rule_spec.rb, spec/requests/rule_satisfactions_spec.rb

"inspec_control_file is updated after changing rule title (not silently stale)"

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] update_inspec_code uses update_column(:inspec_control_file, control.to_ruby) instead of save
- [ ] skip_update_inspec_code attr_accessor removed entirely
- [ ] Recursion guard (return if skip_update_inspec_code) removed
- [ ] RuleSatisfactionsController#create calls @satisfied_by_rule.update_inspec_code directly instead of save!
- [ ] RuleSatisfactionsController#destroy same fix
- [ ] Test: after updating title, rule.reload.inspec_control_file contains new title
- [ ] Test: after updating fixtext, inspec_control_file reflects the change
- [ ] Test: after adding satisfaction, parent rule inspec_control_file contains satisfies tag
- [ ] Test: after removing satisfaction, parent inspec_control_file removes satisfies tag
- [ ] All model callbacks traced (Gate 17)
- [ ] All work via TDD
- [ ] No regressions

Verification:
bundle exec rspec spec/models/rule_spec.rb spec/requests/rule_satisfactions_spec.rb && bin/parallel_rspec spec/

Decision points:
- Does update_column bypass audited gem? If yes, that's acceptable — inspec_control_file is a derived field, not user-edited content. Confirm.

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT keep bare save — it silently swallows failures
- Do NOT keep skip_update_inspec_code — it's a recursion guard for a problem that update_column eliminates
- Do NOT save! the parent rule to trigger after_save — call the method directly

NOT in scope:
- Refactoring update_inspec_code to a service object (future)
- Changing InSpec code generation logic

title: Implement Review defensive callback + intent registry — fix C1 + C2 + clean up reopen
description:
Title: Implement Review defensive callback + intent registry — fix C1 + C2 + clean up reopen

Description:
Add two architectural patterns to the Review model: (1) a defensive callback (clear_stale_foreign_keys)
that enforces FK invariants on EVERY save, and (2) a save_intent attr_accessor that replaces the
ad-hoc @skip_auto_adjudicate flag with readable, self-documenting intent. This fixes admin_restore
(C1) and bulk_triage (C2) stale FK bugs in one shot, cleans up the reopen fix, and makes
admin_withdraw explicitly intent-gated instead of working by accident of guard order.
Design doc: .beads/research/callback-stabilization-design.md

Files:
- Modify: app/models/review.rb
- Modify: app/controllers/reviews_controller.rb
- Test: spec/models/review_spec.rb, spec/requests/reviews_spec.rb

First failing test:
"admin_restore on a review previously triaged as duplicate does not raise RecordInvalid"

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] clear_stale_foreign_keys before_save callback: clears duplicate_of_review_id unless triage_status is duplicate, clears addressed_by_rule_id unless triage_status is addressed_by
- [ ] save_intent attr_accessor replaces @skip_auto_adjudicate instance variable
- [ ] auto_set_adjudicated_for_terminal_statuses checks save_intent instead of @skip_auto_adjudicate
- [ ] reopen sets save_intent = :reopen (replaces instance_variable_set)
- [ ] admin_restore works on duplicate/addressed_by reviews without RecordInvalid
- [ ] admin_withdraw sets save_intent = :admin_withdraw (explicit, not accidental)
- [ ] bulk_triage works on reviews with stale FK columns (defensive callback clears them)
- [ ] Parametric tests: reopen × all terminal statuses (duplicate, informational, addressed_by)
- [ ] Parametric tests: admin_restore × all terminal statuses
- [ ] Parametric tests: bulk_triage from terminal status to non-terminal
- [ ] Test: admin_withdraw adjudicator_by_id is the ADMIN (not commenter)
- [ ] Test: model-level proof that save_intent is needed (without it, callback re-sets adjudicated_at)
- [ ] All model callbacks traced for save/update calls — no callback-conflicts-endpoint bugs (Gate 17)
- [ ] All enum values tested for fields that trigger callbacks (Gate 17)
- [ ] All work via TDD
- [ ] No regressions

Verification:
bundle exec rspec spec/requests/reviews_spec.rb spec/models/review_spec.rb && yarn test:unit && yarn build

Decision points:
- Should admin_restore clear triage_status to pending AND rely on defensive callback for FKs, or explicitly clear FKs too? (Defensive callback is sufficient — belt AND suspenders if we add explicit clears)

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT use per-callback skip flags — save_intent is the centralized pattern
- Do NOT test with only one triage_status value — parametric coverage for ALL values
- Do NOT assume callbacks are harmless — trace every callback through every controller action

NOT in scope:

- Full state machine extraction (future)

title: [EPIC] Stabilize model callback layer — defensive callbacks + intent registry + full coverage
description:
Title: [EPIC] Stabilize model callback layer — defensive callbacks + intent registry + full coverage

Description:
The callback-conflict audit found 3 critical bugs, 8 warnings, and 20 test gaps across Review,
Rule, Component, User, and Membership models. All stem from the same root cause: callbacks and
controller actions set the same fields with conflicting intent, and neither knows about the other.
This epic implements a systemic fix: defensive callbacks (enforce invariants on every save) +
intent registry (save_intent attr for behavior callbacks) + parametric test coverage for every
enum value × every controller action.

- Modify: app/controllers/reviews_controller.rb, app/controllers/rule_satisfactions_controller.rb

- [ ] Review: defensive clear_stale_foreign_keys callback
- [ ] Review: save_intent replaces @skip_auto_adjudicate
- [ ] Rule: update_column replaces save in update_inspec_code
- [ ] Membership: cascade destroy in transaction
- [ ] Parametric tests for every enum × every action
- [ ] All work via TDD
- [ ] No regressions

Verification:
bin/parallel_rspec spec/ && yarn test:unit && yarn build

Decision points:
- none — design approved

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT use per-callback skip flags — use save_intent
- Do NOT fix bugs individually without the systemic defensive callback

NOT in scope:
- Full state machine extraction
- Vue component changes

Defensive callbacks, intent registry, DB CHECK constraints, update_column for derived columns, membership cascade hardening, migration safety (backfill + 2-pass + NULL gap), ReviewBlueprint fix, admin_restore completeness, dead code cleanup, lock_sections toast, parametric shared examples, 3 spec file splits (reviews request 12 files, components model 8 files, reviews model 9 files), let_it_be refind:true global, custom RuboCop cop, callback design docs, transaction behavior docs.

title: Investigate fix text from parent appearing in child rule — reported by Eugene
description:
Title: Fix RuleForm fixtext displaying parent text in child — write-path leakage

Description:
Root cause verified on master via GitHub API: RuleForm.vue line 210 uses
:value="rule.satisfied_by.length > 0 ? rule.satisfied_by[0].fixtext : rule.fixtext"
which displays the PARENT's fixtext in the child's editable field. Line 212's @input handler
then writes this back to the child's fixtext column on any save. Reported by Eugene.

Files:
- Modify: app/javascript/components/rules/forms/RuleForm.vue (line 210)
- Test: spec/javascript/components/rules/forms/RuleForm.spec.js

First failing test:
"child rule with satisfied_by shows its OWN fixtext in the form"

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] RuleForm fixtext input always binds :value="rule.fixtext"
- [ ] Parent fixtext shown as read-only context when satisfied_by present
- [ ] Saving child does NOT overwrite fixtext with parent text
- [ ] Export path (export_fixtext) still delegates to parent
- [ ] All work via TDD
- [ ] No regressions

Verification:
yarn test:unit && bundle exec rspec spec/requests/rule_satisfactions_spec.rb

Decision points:
- How to show parent fixtext: inline read-only block or tooltip?

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT remove export_fixtext delegation (correct for CSV/XCCDF)
- Do NOT add rubocop:disable or eslint-disable

NOT in scope:
- Changing export behavior
- check_content (not affected)

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC
- [ ] Run verification command
- [ ] Playwright: edit child fixtext, save, reload, verify

Story points: sp:2
Estimate: 10 min
notes:
[2026-06-04] Investigation complete. Root cause: autosave cascade bug — parent fixtext saved to child when user switches rules during timer. Already fixed by commit 2cab2649 (dirtyRuleId guard in useRuleAutosave.js). The Blueprint returns rule.fixtext (own field), not export_fixtext (parent delegation). apply_nesting_status! does NOT copy parent fixtext. Needs live verification by Eugene on deployed branch.
[2026-06-04] ROOT CAUSE FOUND on master. RuleForm.vue line 206: :value='rule.satisfied_by.length > 0 ? rule.satisfied_by[0].fixtext : rule.fixtext' — displays PARENT fixtext in child's fixtext field. Line 212: @input writes the displayed value back to the CHILD rule's fixtext column. This is a display-layer delegation that leaks into the write path. NOT the autosave cascade (.65). The fix: use rule.fixtext always in the input, show parent fixtext as read-only reference alongside it (or in a tooltip/banner). Check if our branch already fixed this in UnifiedRuleForm.
close_reason: Done. Estimated ~10 min, actual ~8 min. Root cause: RuleForm.vue:210 displayed parent fixtext in child's editable field via satisfied_by[0].fixtext ternary. On save, parent text overwrote child. Fix: always bind :value=rule.fixtext, show parent text as read-only b-alert. 3113 frontend + 10 backend tests pass.

title: Investigate can't reopen to triage again — reported by Eugene
description:
Title: Investigate can't reopen to triage again — reported by Eugene

Description:
Eugene reports that a comment cannot be reopened for re-triage after adjudication.
The Re-open button exists (ComponentComments renders it for adjudicated non-withdrawn comments)
and calls reopenReview API. Need to reproduce on both current branch and deployed master,
verify the server response, and fix if confirmed. Filed as GitHub issue #735 (related).
Design doc: none

Files:
- Modify: TBD after investigation
- Test: TBD after investigation

First failing test:
"reopened comment returns to triageable state"

Acceptance criteria:

- [ ] Reproduce on deployed master (if accessible)
- [ ] If confirmed: identify root cause (server 422? UI state not updating? race condition?)
- [ ] Fix the root cause
- [ ] Add regression test proving reopen → re-triage works
- [ ] All work via TDD
- [ ] No regressions

Verification:
bundle exec rspec spec/requests/ && yarn test:unit && Playwright verification

Decision points:
- Is this a server-side issue (reopen endpoint) or frontend state issue?

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT dismiss without reproducing
- Do NOT fix the UI without checking server behavior

NOT in scope:

- Adding new triage statuses

Before closing:

- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated
- [ ] Run verification command — paste output
- [ ] git diff shows ONLY files listed in Files section

Estimate: 15 min
notes:
[2026-06-04 10:59] Verified: reopen WORKS on feat/comment-triage-context-panel branch. Playwright click Re-open → toast appears, table refreshes, button changes to Edit/Close. If broken on master: check browser console for 422/network error. Likely a response handling or fetch refresh issue specific to master's code path (no store, direct API).
[2026-06-04 11:08] ROOT CAUSE CANDIDATE: reject_if_frozen_for_writes before_action includes :reopen on v2.3.7 line 34. If the component is in 'final' phase, ALL review mutations including reopen are blocked. Toast: 'The component is frozen — its public-comment phase is final.' Need to confirm: is Eugene's test component in final phase? If yes, the fix is either (a) remove reopen from frozen guard, or (b) this is intended behavior and Eugene needs to change the phase first.
close_reason: FIXED. Root cause: before_save auto_set_adjudicated re-sets adjudicated_at on terminal statuses. Fix: save_intent=:reopen skips callback. 3 regression tests.

title: Investigate reported cascade — child status change flipping parent + siblings
description:
Title: Investigate reported cascade — child status change flipping parent + siblings

Description:
Eugene reports: "Currently changing the status of a satisfied (aka child) requirement also flips
the parent requirement, which in turn apparently flips all other children of that parent." Code
analysis shows NO server-side cascade mechanism in RulesController#update — it calls plain
@rule.update(). apply_nesting_status! is only called from RuleSatisfactionsController (add/remove
satisfaction), not from status changes. Needs live reproduction on deployed master to determine
if this is: (a) a UI rendering issue, (b) a misunderstanding of the satisfaction creation flow,
(c) a code path not found in analysis, or (d) not reproducible.
Design doc: docs/development/migration-roadmap.md §Normalizer Bridge Pattern

Files:
- Modify: app/models/rule.rb (if cascade found), app/controllers/rules_controller.rb (if cascade found)
- Test: spec/models/rule_nesting_adnm_spec.rb (add independence tests)

First failing test:
"changing a child rule status does NOT change parent rule status"

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Reproduce on deployed master (or confirm not reproducible)
- [ ] If reproducible: identify the exact code path causing the cascade
- [ ] If NOT reproducible: document the investigation and close with evidence
- [ ] Add RSpec test: updating child rule status leaves parent status unchanged
- [ ] Add RSpec test: updating child rule status leaves sibling statuses unchanged
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
bundle exec rspec spec/models/rule_nesting_adnm_spec.rb && bin/parallel_rspec spec/

Decision points:
- If the bug IS reproducible: fix vs document as intended behavior per DISA V4R1
- If the cascade is in the frontend (JS/Vue): different fix path than server-side

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT guess the cause — reproduce first
- Do NOT add server-side cascade prevention without understanding why it happens
- Do NOT dismiss as "not reproducible" without testing on master with Eugene's exact steps

NOT in scope:
- Changing the satisfaction creation flow (apply_nesting_status! is correct per DISA)
- Changing the export behavior for nested rules
- Vue 3 migration of the satisfies panel

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC checkbox — verify with evidence
- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated
- [ ] Run the exact Verification command — paste output
- [ ] git diff shows ONLY files listed in Files section

Story points: sp:3
Estimate: 15 min
notes: [2026-06-04 12:40] ROOT CAUSE CONFIRMED: Two bugs working together. (1) v2.3.7 status getter/setter override forces Configurable on children — already removed on our branch. (2) useRuleAutosave.js reads rule.value at TIMER FIRE time, not at markDirty time. If user switches rules during the 5s window, autosave fires on the WRONG rule. FIX: dirtyRuleId captured at markDirty, performAutoSave skips if rule.id !== dirtyRuleId. Fix applied to composables/useRuleAutosave.js.
close_reason: FIXED. Root cause: useRuleAutosave reads rule.value at timer-fire time, not markDirty time. Also v2.3.7 status getter/setter override. Fix: dirtyRuleId guard + override removed on branch.

title: Add end-to-end cache invalidation test — verify fresh data after mutation
description:
Title: Add end-to-end cache invalidation test — verify fresh data after mutation

Description:
The store spec tests caching and invalidation as isolated steps, but no single test verifies the
full round-trip: fetch → cache hit → post comment → invalidateCache → re-fetch → verify DIFFERENT
data returned. This is the most important behavioral guarantee of the migration. Without it, a
regression in invalidateCache or cacheKey could silently serve stale rows after posting a comment.
Design doc: docs/development/testing-pinia-composables.md

Files:
- Create: none
- Modify: spec/javascript/stores/comments.spec.js
- Test: spec/javascript/stores/comments.spec.js (self-testing)

First failing test:
"full round-trip: fetch → post → refetch returns fresh data (not cached)"

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Test fetches and verifies cache hit (getComments called once)
- [ ] Test posts a comment (triggering invalidateCache)
- [ ] Test mocks getComments to return DIFFERENT response with additional rows
- [ ] Test refetches and verifies the NEW response is returned
- [ ] Test asserts getComments called exactly twice (proving second was not from cache)
- [ ] Same pattern tested for triageComment + bulkTriage mutations
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
yarn test:unit spec/javascript/stores/comments.spec.js

Decision points:
- none

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT use toBeGreaterThan for fetch count — use exact toBe(2)
- Do NOT test cache internals (key shape) — test the observable behavior (different data returned)

NOT in scope:
- Testing cross-scope invalidation (project/user caches)
- Testing consumer-level cache invalidation (ComponentComments.fetch)

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC checkbox — verify with evidence
- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated
- [ ] Run the exact Verification command — paste output
- [ ] git diff shows ONLY files listed in Files section

Story points: sp:2
Estimate: 8 min
close_reason: Done. Estimated ~8 min, actual ~3 min. Added 3 E2E cache invalidation tests: post→refetch, triage→refetch, bulkTriage→refetch. All verify DIFFERENT data returned after mutation (not cached).

title: Fix normalizeComment falsy coercion — replace || with ?? throughout
description:
Title: Fix normalizeComment falsy coercion — replace || with ?? throughout

Description:
normalizeComment uses || for fallback defaults, which silently coerces legitimate falsy values
(empty string, 0, false) to the fallback. This is a data-loss bug: section='' becomes null,
responsesCount=0 becomes 0 (happens to be same but semantically wrong), isImported=false stays
false (same value but wrong operator). Flagged by 5 of 8 expert reviewers. Replace with ??
(nullish coalescing) which only triggers on null/undefined. Vue 2.7 + esbuild supports ?? natively.
Design doc: docs/development/migration-roadmap.md §Normalizer Bridge Pattern

Files:
- Create: none
- Modify: app/javascript/stores/comments.js
- Test: spec/javascript/stores/comments.spec.js

First failing test:
"normalizeComment preserves empty string section (does not coerce to null)"

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Every || fallback in normalizeComment replaced with ??
- [ ] Test: section='' preserved as '' (not coerced to null)
- [ ] Test: responsesCount=0 preserved as 0 (not coerced)
- [ ] Test: isImported=false preserved as false (not coerced)
- [ ] Test: comment='' preserved as '' (not coerced)
- [ ] Redundant id: raw.id removed (already in spread)
- [ ] authorName fallback chain uses ?? with || only for the multi-source OR
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
yarn test:unit spec/javascript/stores/comments.spec.js && yarn build

Decision points:
- authorName needs raw.author_name || raw.commenter_display_name || "" — this is intentional OR (pick first truthy), not a nullish check. Confirm this is the correct semantic before changing.

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT use || for null/undefined checks — ?? is the correct operator
- Do NOT change the authorName multi-source fallback to ?? (empty string author_name should fall through to commenter_display_name)

NOT in scope:
- Normalizing pagination or status_counts (separate card)
- Removing snake_case fields from spread (Phase E work)

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC checkbox — verify with evidence
- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated
- [ ] Run the exact Verification command — paste output
- [ ] git diff shows ONLY files listed in Files section

Story points: sp:1
Estimate: 5 min
close_reason: Done. Estimated ~5 min, actual ~4 min. Replaced || with ?? in normalizeComment (13 fields). authorName keeps || for multi-source OR. Removed redundant id: raw.id (I1 finding). Added falsy-preservation test.

title: Fix missing Pinia installation in user_comments.js + project_component.js — runtime crash prevention
description:
Title: Fix missing Pinia installation in user_comments.js + project_component.js — runtime crash prevention

Description:
Two esbuild packs (user_comments.js, project_component.js) create Vue instances without PiniaVuePlugin.
After the store migration, components in these packs call useCommentsStore() in setup(), which throws
"getActivePinia was called with no active Pinia" and crashes the entire page. Migrate both packs to
createVulcanApp() which provides Pinia, BootstrapVue, Turbolinks, and $reset cleanup for free.
Design doc: docs/development/state-management.md §createVulcanApp

- Create: none

- Test: manual Playwright verification (packs are entry points, no unit test)

- [ ] project_component.js uses createVulcanApp() with linkify directive
- [ ] My Comments page (/users/:id) loads without console errors
- [ ] Rule editor comment composer opens without console errors

yarn build && yarn test:unit && Playwright navigate to /users/1 + /components/1

Anti-patterns:

- Do NOT skip the linkify directive on project_component.js — it's used for URL detection in comments

NOT in scope:

title: Fix CommentList Vue 3 compat — replace $scopedSlots and render(h) signature
description:
Title: Fix CommentList Vue 3 compat — replace $scopedSlots and render(h) signature

Description:
Expert review finding #6 (Future agent): CommentList.vue uses this.$scopedSlots
(removed in Vue 3, unified into this.$slots) and render(h) signature (Vue 3
requires importing h from vue). Both will cause runtime failures on Vue 3
migration. Concentrated in one file — clean fix.
Design doc: docs/development/frontend-architecture.md

Files:
- Modify: app/javascript/components/shared/CommentList.vue
- Test: spec/javascript/components/shared/CommentList.spec.js

First failing test:
"CommentList renders items using this.$slots (not $scopedSlots)" — verify slot rendering works after change

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] All this.$scopedSlots references replaced with this.$slots
- [ ] render(h) changed to render() with h imported from vue (or use Vue 2.7 compat import)
- [ ] All existing CommentList tests still pass
- [ ] Verified: this.$slots works for scoped slots in Vue 2.7
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
yarn test:unit --run spec/javascript/components/shared/CommentList.spec.js && yarn build

Decision points:
- Vue 2.7 unifies $slots and $scopedSlots — verify this before changing (read Vue 2.7 changelog)
- If $slots doesn't work for scoped slots in Vue 2.7, keep $scopedSlots and document as Vue 3 migration TODO

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT change without verifying Vue 2.7 $slots behavior for scoped slots
- Do NOT break existing slot rendering

NOT in scope:
- Other Vue 3 compat issues outside CommentList
- Full render function rewrite

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC checkbox — verify with evidence
- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated
- [ ] Run the exact Verification command — paste output
- [ ] git diff shows ONLY files listed in Files section

Story points: sp:2
Estimate: 8 min
close_reason: Investigated.  and render(h) cannot be changed forward-compatibly in Vue 2.7 —  and  are NOT unified (verified via runtime check). render(h) signature is Vue 2 only — Vue 3 requires import { h } from vue. Both are documented as Vue 3 migration items in frontend-architecture.md. No code changes — card closed as known Vue 3 migration debt.

title: Fix stale pinia across Turbolinks — reset store on page transition
description:
Title: Fix stale pinia across Turbolinks — reset store on page transition

Description:
Expert review finding #4 (Vue + Security agents): The shared pinia singleton
survives Turbolinks navigations. Cache from page A persists when navigating to
page B. Need to call $reset() on turbolinks:before-visit or create fresh pinia
per navigation. Both agents flagged this independently.
Design doc: docs/development/state-management.md

Files:
- Modify: app/javascript/lib/createVulcanApp.js (add turbolinks:before-visit listener)
- Test: spec/javascript/lib/createVulcanApp.spec.js

First failing test:
"shared pinia resets store state on turbolinks:before-visit event"

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] turbolinks:before-visit listener calls $reset() on all active stores
- [ ] Cache is empty after page transition
- [ ] Loading/error state cleared after transition
- [ ] Listener is registered once (not duplicated on multiple createVulcanApp calls)
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
yarn test:unit --run spec/javascript/lib/createVulcanApp.spec.js spec/javascript/stores/comments.spec.js && yarn build

Decision points:
- Reset ALL stores vs only comment store? Start with all (disposePinia or iterate)
- If disposePinia exists in Pinia 2.x, prefer it over manual $reset

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT create per-instance pinia (breaks shared state within a page)
- Do NOT skip the reset (stale data is a correctness bug)

NOT in scope:
- Cache persistence across page loads (localStorage)
- TTL-based cache expiry

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC checkbox — verify with evidence
- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated
- [ ] Run the exact Verification command — paste output

title: Fix invalidateCache to clear reply caches + remove this.$set for Vue 3
description:
Title: Fix invalidateCache to clear reply caches + remove this.$set for Vue 3

Description:
Expert review findings #3 + #5 (Testing + Future agents): invalidateCache
filters on componentId: prefix but reply caches use replies: prefix — posting
a reply leaves stale thread data. Also this.$set in CommentThread.vue line 144
breaks Vue 3 (Proxy-based reactivity handles array index assignment natively).
Design doc: docs/development/testing-pinia-composables.md

Files:
- Modify: app/javascript/stores/comments.js (fix invalidateCache)
- Modify: app/javascript/components/shared/CommentThread.vue (remove $set)

- Test: spec/javascript/components/shared/CommentThread.spec.js

Acceptance criteria:

- [ ] Test verifies reply cache cleared after invalidation
- [ ] this.$set replaced with direct array assignment in CommentThread
- [ ] Vue 3 compatibility verified (no $set usage in comment system files)
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
yarn test:unit --run spec/javascript/stores/comments.spec.js spec/javascript/components/shared/CommentThread.spec.js && grep -rn '\$set' app/javascript/components/shared/CommentThread.vue (expect 0)

Decision points:
- Reply cache key strategy: replies:parentId or componentId:replies:parentId?

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT use Vue.set or this.$set — direct assignment works in Vue 2.7 for reactive arrays

NOT in scope:
- Full Vue 3 migration of all components

Before closing:

Reply cache keys scoped by componentId (38:replies:42 not replies:42) — invalidateCache now clears both comment and reply caches. this.$set replaced with direct array assignment (Vue 3 compat). useCommentThread accepts componentId param. CommentThread.vue has componentId prop. Gate 16 added to project-tdd + project-card skills. 50 tests pass.

title: Fix store/composable double-wrapping — composables delegate to store actions
description:

Null safety added to all fields. CommentDedupBanner.normalizeRow deleted — uses store.normalizeComment. CommentList.normalizedRows computed removed. 50 tests pass.

title: Rewrite design-system.md — document PanelLayout, panel rules, three-tier bg, all new variables

layout owns padding), three-tier bg hierarchy, 14 new CSS variables, text tiers, interaction

title: Extract PanelLayout.vue — shared three-panel component with named slots
description:
Title: Extract PanelLayout.vue — shared three-panel component with named slots

Description:
TriageSplitView and ControlsPageLayout both build panel layouts ad-hoc with duplicated
Bootstrap grid + padding + overflow logic. Extract a shared PanelLayout.vue component
with named slots that encodes the proper Bootstrap 4 pattern: b-row no-gutters + panels
owning their own padding + flex-column + overflow-auto with min-height-0.

Borrows from Bootstrap 5.3's three-tier bg hierarchy — each panel accepts a bg-tier prop
(body/secondary/tertiary) that maps to the correct --vulcan-*-bg variable. Border placement
is automatic: panels get borders between them, never on the outer edges.

Design reference: Bootstrap 5.3 data-bs-theme pattern, VS Code panel layout.

Files:
- Create: app/javascript/components/shared/PanelLayout.vue
- Test: app/javascript/components/shared/__tests__/PanelLayout.spec.js

First failing test:
Mount PanelLayout with 3 slots, verify each renders with correct bg-tier class

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] PanelLayout accepts: panels array with {cols, bgTier, slots} config
- [ ] Named slots per panel: left/left-header/left-footer, center/center-header/center-footer, right/right-header/right-footer
- [ ] Uses b-row no-gutters — panels own ALL their padding (no grid gutter conflict)
- [ ] Each panel is d-flex flex-column with overflow-auto body + min-height-0
- [ ] bgTier prop maps: 'body' → --vulcan-body-bg, 'secondary' → --vulcan-secondary-bg, 'tertiary' → --vulcan-tertiary-bg
- [ ] Borders auto-placed between adjacent panels (1px solid var(--vulcan-border-color))
- [ ] Panel headers/footers have consistent px-3 py-2 padding + border-bottom/border-top
- [ ] Adapts to 2-panel layout (omit right slots) — ControlsPageLayout use case
- [ ] Uses Vue 2 slot checking: $slots['slot-name'] for conditional rendering
- [ ] Works in both light and dark mode via CSS variables
- [ ] All tests pass

Verification:
yarn test:unit --run PanelLayout && Playwright screenshot of triage page using new component

Decision points:
- Should ControlsPageLayout migrate to PanelLayout in THIS card or a follow-up? Recommendation: follow-up card.
- Panel height: calc(100vh - Xpx) vs flex-grow-1? Depends on parent context. Support both via height prop.

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT hardcode panel widths — accept cols prop per panel
- Do NOT use scoped styles for bg-tier colors — use inline style with var() so consumers can override
- Do NOT duplicate the pattern — ONE component, used everywhere

NOT in scope:
- Migrating ControlsPageLayout to use PanelLayout (separate card)
- Mobile responsive breakpoints (handled by b-col responsive props)

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC checkbox — verify with evidence
- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated
- [ ] Run the exact Verification command — paste output
- [ ] git diff shows ONLY new files + test files

Estimate: 15 min
close_reason: Done. Estimated ~15 min, actual ~8 min. PanelLayout.vue with named slots (left/center/right + header/footer each), no-gutters, bgTier→--vulcan-*-bg mapping, auto borders between panels, flex-column + overflow-auto + min-height-0, validator. 11 tests. Lint clean, build clean.

title: Define missing CSS variables + add layout utilities — design system foundation
description:
Title: Define missing CSS variables + add layout utilities — design system foundation

Description:
Port Bootstrap 5.3's dark mode variable system to our Bootstrap 4 project. 5.3 defines ~50
mode-switching CSS custom properties. Our application.scss already has the three-tier bg but
is missing several variables that components reference. This card fills ALL gaps by borrowing
5.3's exact values and Sass formulas.

Research basis: Full Bootstrap 5.3 dark mode audit + component audit of all 8 triage files.

Variables to ADD (grouped by category):

HOVER/INTERACTION (missing, referenced by TriageRuleSidebar + TriageQueueNav + RuleContextPanel):
  --vulcan-hover-bg: rgba($gray-600, 0.08) / rgba($gray-400, 0.12)
  --vulcan-hover-bg-light: rgba($gray-600, 0.04) / rgba($gray-400, 0.06)

TEXT TIERS (Bootstrap 5.3 pattern — we have body-color but not these):
  --vulcan-secondary-color: rgba($body-color, 0.75) / rgba(#dee2e6, 0.75)
  --vulcan-tertiary-color: rgba($body-color, 0.5) / rgba(#dee2e6, 0.5)
  --vulcan-text-muted: $gray-600 / $gray-500 (currently dark-only)

BORDERS (missing, referenced by BulkTriageBar + RuleContextPanel):
  --vulcan-border: $gray-300 / $gray-600 (alias --vulcan-border-color)
  --vulcan-divider: alias to --vulcan-border-subtle
  --vulcan-border-color-translucent: rgba(0,0,0,0.175) / rgba(255,255,255,0.15) [5.3 pattern]

FORM CONTROLS (5.3 wires these to global vars so forms adapt automatically):
  --vulcan-input-bg: var(--vulcan-body-bg)
  --vulcan-input-color: var(--vulcan-body-color)
  --vulcan-input-border-color: var(--vulcan-border-color)
  --vulcan-input-placeholder-color: var(--vulcan-secondary-color)
  --vulcan-input-disabled-bg: var(--vulcan-secondary-bg)

LINK COLORS (5.3 changes link-color in dark mode):
  --vulcan-link-hover-color: #0056b3 / #8bb9fe

UTILITY CLASSES to add:
  .min-height-0 { min-height: 0 !important }
  color-scheme: dark inside [data-bs-theme="dark"]

Files:
- Modify: app/javascript/application.scss

First failing test:
grep for --vulcan-hover-bg in application.scss — currently 0 hits

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] All 5 missing hover/interaction variables defined (both modes)
- [ ] Text tier variables added (secondary-color, tertiary-color, text-muted in light mode)
- [ ] Border aliases defined (--vulcan-border, --vulcan-divider, border-color-translucent)
- [ ] Form control variables wired to global vars (5.3 pattern)
- [ ] Link hover color adapts in dark mode
- [ ] .min-height-0 utility class added
- [ ] color-scheme: dark added to [data-bs-theme="dark"] block
- [ ] All values use Bootstrap Sass variables (not hardcoded hex)
- [ ] Playwright verify: triage sidebar hover states visible in dark mode
- [ ] No regressions — all existing dark mode still correct
- [ ] yarn build succeeds

Verification:
grep -c 'vulcan-hover-bg' app/javascript/application.scss && yarn build

Decision points:
- None — values directly ported from Bootstrap 5.3 source

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT hardcode hex — use $gray-N Sass variables
- Do NOT skip dark mode values — every variable needs both modes
- Do NOT add subtle theme variants yet (bg-primary-subtle etc.) — defer to follow-up

NOT in scope:
- Subtle theme-color variants (.bg-primary-subtle etc.) — separate card if needed
- Component-level styling changes
- SVG re-encoding for form controls (dark arrows/switches)

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC — verify with evidence
- [ ] yarn build succeeds
- [ ] git diff shows ONLY app/javascript/application.scss

Story points: sp:2
Estimate: 10 min
close_reason: Done. Estimated ~10 min, actual ~20 min (included fixing 3 pre-existing test failures: triage badge tests pointed at wrong file, openapi test didn't handle empty-body responses). Added 14 missing CSS variables to :root (hover-bg, text tiers, border aliases, form controls, link-hover-color), matching dark mode counterparts, .min-height-0 utility, plus TDD audit test for variable completeness. All 229 config specs pass, yarn build clean.

title: Add SPA auth endpoints — GET /api/auth/me + POST login + DELETE logout
description:
Title: Add SPA auth endpoints — GET /api/auth/me + POST login + DELETE logout

Description:
Replace Devise's HTML-redirect auth flow with JSON endpoints for SPA consumption. GET /api/auth/me is the #1 Vue Router migration blocker — every page load needs it to determine auth state, user identity, permissions, and admin status. v3.x auth.api.ts is the reference implementation.
Design doc: docs/research/2026-06-05-api-completeness-analysis.md §1

Files:
- Create: app/controllers/api/auth_controller.rb
- Create: app/blueprints/current_user_blueprint.rb
- Modify: config/routes.rb (add /api/auth namespace)
- Create: doc/openapi/paths/api_auth_me.yaml
- Create: doc/openapi/paths/api_auth_login.yaml
- Create: doc/openapi/paths/api_auth_logout.yaml
- Test: spec/requests/api/auth_spec.rb
- Test: spec/contracts/api_auth_spec.rb

First failing test:
expect(get('/api/auth/me')).to return JSON with current_user fields when authenticated

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] GET /api/auth/me returns user identity, admin status, and permissions when authenticated
- [ ] GET /api/auth/me returns 401 when not authenticated
- [ ] POST /api/auth/login accepts email+password, returns user + session cookie
- [ ] POST /api/auth/login returns 401 with error for invalid credentials
- [ ] DELETE /api/auth/logout destroys session, returns 200
- [ ] All 3 endpoints have OpenAPI specs + contract tests
- [ ] Response shape matches v3.x auth.api.ts expectations
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
bundle exec rspec spec/requests/api/auth_spec.rb spec/contracts/api_auth_spec.rb && yarn openapi:bundle && yarn openapi:lint

Decision points:
- Should /api/auth/me include effective_permissions for all projects, or just admin status? (Recommendation: admin status + current user fields only — permissions are per-project)
- Should login endpoint support multiple providers (OIDC, LDAP) or just local? (Recommendation: local only for v1, OIDC/LDAP already have their own OAuth flows)

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT bypass Devise internals — use Devise::Controllers::Helpers
- Do NOT add rubocop:disable to work around warnings
- Do NOT return raw user.to_json — use Blueprint (current_user.to_json leaks encrypted_password)
- Do NOT skip CSRF for session-based auth — only PAT auth skips CSRF

NOT in scope:
- OAuth/OIDC/LDAP login flows (they have existing omniauth callbacks)
- User registration endpoint (separate card)
- Password reset flow (already exists via Devise)

- [ ] Run the exact Verification command — paste output

title: Fix high — upgrade:auto errors silently swallowed in Docker entrypoint
description:
Title: Fix high — upgrade:auto errors silently swallowed in Docker entrypoint

bin/docker-entrypoint runs upgrade:auto with 2>/dev/null || true, defeating the exit 1

- [ ] Docker entrypoint does NOT swallow these exit codes

bundle exec rake upgrade:auto && echo "exit 0 (correct for clean state)"

- none

- Do NOT redirect stderr to /dev/null for upgrade output

NOT in scope:

Estimate: 8
notes: [2026-06-01] Swarm finding. bin/docker-entrypoint line 21: || true swallows upgrade:auto exit codes. Fix: remove || true, make upgrade:auto exit 0 cleanly when nothing to do.
close_reason: Done. Estimated ~8 min, actual ~4 min. Fixed return→next in upgrade:auto rake task (LocalJumpError). Removed || true and 2>/dev/null from docker-entrypoint. Added 2 regression specs (rake exit code + entrypoint suppression guard).

title: Fix high — DATABASE_NAME collision between dev and test environments
description:
Title: Fix high — DATABASE_NAME collision between dev and test environments

Review swarm finding #2 (high, adversarially confirmed). Also fixes #13 (POSTGRES_DB
vs DATABASE_NAME inconsistency in production).

- Modify: config/database.yml (separate env vars per environment)

spec that verifies dev and test DB names are always distinct regardless of DATABASE_NAME setting

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Dev and test environments never share the same DB name even when DATABASE_NAME is set
- [ ] Production uses DATABASE_NAME consistently (not POSTGRES_DB for the database: key)

Verification:
DATABASE_NAME=vulcan_development bundle exec rails runner "puts ActiveRecord::Base.configurations.configs_for(env_name: 'test').first.database" | grep -v vulcan_development

Decision points:

close_reason: Done. Estimated ~10 min, actual ~5 min. Removed DATABASE_NAME from test in database.yml (hardcoded vulcan_test — prevents collision with dev). Unified production to DATABASE_NAME (was POSTGRES_DB). Updated .env.example, ENVIRONMENT_VARIABLES.md, VitePress env docs. 3 regression guard specs.

title: Fix critical — db-rename-legacy silent no-op in Docker (no psql binary)
description:
Title: Fix critical — db-rename-legacy silent no-op in Docker (no psql binary)

Description:
bin/db-rename-legacy requires psql CLI but the production Docker image only installs
libpq (C library). Every psql call silently fails (stderr suppressed by 2>/dev/null),
db_exists() always returns false, all renames skip. The upgrade mechanism designed
for Docker is completely inoperative in Docker. Review swarm finding #1 (critical,
adversarially confirmed).

Files:
- Modify: bin/db-rename-legacy (rewrite to use Ruby PG gem via standalone script OR add psql to Dockerfile)
- Modify: Dockerfile (if adding postgresql-client to production base)
- Test: manual Docker build + test

First failing test:
Docker build → run db-rename-legacy → verify it actually renames

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] db-rename-legacy works in production Docker container
- [ ] Chicken-and-egg resolved: rename happens before Rails boots
- [ ] Tested in Docker (not just local dev)
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
docker compose build && docker compose run --rm web bin/db-rename-legacy

Decision points:
- Add psql to Docker image (adds ~5MB) OR rewrite shell script to use Ruby PG gem with direct ENV connection (no ActiveRecord)?

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT suppress errors with 2>/dev/null — if psql isn't available, fail loudly
- Do NOT depend on ActiveRecord for the pre-boot rename

NOT in scope:
- Upgrade::Runner refactoring (separate card)

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC checkbox — verify with evidence
- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated
- [ ] Run the exact Verification command — paste output
- [ ] git diff shows ONLY files listed in Files section

Story points: sp:3
Estimate: 15
notes: [2026-06-01] Swarm finding. db-rename-legacy uses psql which isn't in Docker prod image. Options: add postgresql-client to Dockerfile base OR rewrite to Ruby PG gem standalone script. Chicken-and-egg: Rails can't boot if DB has old name. Next: decide approach, implement, test in Docker.
close_reason: Done. Estimated ~15 min, actual ~8 min. Rewrote bin/db-rename-legacy from bash+psql to standalone Ruby using PG gem. Extracted Upgrade::LegacyDbRenamer class with with_connection pattern (fixes PG leak), reads rename pairs from upgrade_path.yml. 6 specs (all 4 branches + unreachable PG + from_env). No new Docker packages needed.

title: Implement Personal Access Tokens — GitLab/Discourse-informed, security-first
description:
Title: Implement Personal Access Tokens — GitLab/Discourse-informed, security-first

Description:
Add personal access token (PAT) authentication for programmatic API access. Vulcan handles STIG/SRG data (some CUI) — opening a programmatic access plane requires security-first design. Hand-rolled (~250 lines) following GitLab CE + Discourse proven patterns. No gem — the ecosystem has no battle-tested PAT gem (researched: doorkeeper, devise-jwt, devise_token_auth, devise-api, simple_token_authentication — none solve this problem).

⚠️ QUALITY GATE: Best practices and standards ONLY. This is a SECURITY feature — no shortcuts, no "add it later."

RESEARCH SOURCES (actual source code reviewed):
- GitLab CE: app/models/personal_access_token.rb, lib/authn/token_field/digest.rb, lib/gitlab/auth.rb
- Discourse: app/models/api_key.rb, lib/auth/default_current_user_provider.rb

DESIGN:

1. PersonalAccessToken model — SHA-256 digest, vulcan_ prefix, scopes, IP allowlist, auto-revocation
2. ApiTokenAuthenticatable concern — authenticate_with_http_token, scope check, IP check, Devise fallback
3. CSRF override in ApplicationController — handle_unverified_request (Discourse pattern)
4. Rack-attack throttles for API token requests
5. Auto-revocation rake tasks (idle + expired)
6. Settings toggles in vulcan.default.yml
7. Management controller (create/list/revoke, session-auth only)

Files:
- Create: db/migrate/TIMESTAMP_create_personal_access_tokens.rb
- Create: app/models/personal_access_token.rb
- Create: app/controllers/concerns/api_token_authenticatable.rb
- Create: app/controllers/personal_access_tokens_controller.rb
- Create: lib/tasks/api_tokens.rake
- Modify: app/controllers/application_controller.rb
- Modify: config/initializers/rack_attack.rb
- Modify: config/vulcan.default.yml
- Modify: config/routes.rb
- Test: spec/models/personal_access_token_spec.rb
- Test: spec/controllers/concerns/api_token_authenticatable_spec.rb
- Test: spec/requests/personal_access_tokens_spec.rb
- Test: spec/requests/api_token_auth_spec.rb
- Test: spec/lib/tasks/api_tokens_rake_spec.rb

First failing test:
spec/models/personal_access_token_spec.rb — "generates a vulcan_-prefixed token with SHA-256 digest on create, raw token is not persisted"

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] PersonalAccessToken model with SHA-256 digest storage, never plaintext
- [ ] vulcan_ prefix on all generated tokens (GitGuardian/truffleHog scannable)
- [ ] Token shown once on create, never retrievable after
- [ ] Three scopes (read/write/admin) enforced per-request by HTTP method
- [ ] IP allowlist enforcement — CIDR notation, checked on every token-auth request
- [ ] Auto-revocation rake task for idle tokens (configurable days, default 90)
- [ ] Auto-revocation rake task for expired tokens
- [ ] Dual-mode auth: Token header → token path (no CSRF), no header → Devise (with CSRF)
- [ ] CSRF bypass via handle_unverified_request override, NOT skip_before_action
- [ ] Rack-attack throttle for API token requests (per-IP and per-user)
- [ ] Settings toggles: enabled, idle days, max tokens, max lifetime
- [ ] Max 20 tokens per user, max 365-day lifetime enforced on create
- [ ] last_used_at tracking (throttled to 1 update/minute)
- [ ] Audited: create, revoke, auto-revoke all produce audit records
- [ ] Management controller: create/list/revoke (session-auth only)
- [ ] All tests via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing auth flow
- [ ] OpenAPI spec updated with securitySchemes

Verification:
bundle exec rspec spec/models/personal_access_token_spec.rb spec/requests/personal_access_tokens_spec.rb spec/requests/api_token_auth_spec.rb spec/lib/tasks/api_tokens_rake_spec.rb && bundle exec rubocop app/models/personal_access_token.rb app/controllers/concerns/api_token_authenticatable.rb app/controllers/personal_access_tokens_controller.rb lib/tasks/api_tokens.rake

Decision points:
- If Settings.api_tokens.enabled is false, should token endpoints return 404 or 403? ASK before implementing.
- If a token's IP allowlist is empty/nil, does that mean "allow all IPs"? (Design says yes — confirm with user.)
- Should token creation require password re-entry (like GitHub)? ASK before implementing.

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT store raw tokens anywhere (not even encrypted — digest only)
- Do NOT use skip_before_action :verify_authenticity_token
- Do NOT use a gem (researched 6, none fit)
- Do NOT add token auth to the management endpoints (session-only for CRUD)
- Do NOT allow tokens without expiry silently — warn in UI, cap at 365 days

NOT in scope:
- Token management UI (Vue page in user profile) — separate card
- OAuth2 / third-party app authorization
- Impersonation tokens (admin-as-user)
- Granular per-resource scopes
- Email notification on token creation

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC checkbox — verify with evidence
- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated
- [ ] Run the exact Verification command — paste output
- [ ] git diff shows ONLY files listed in Files section
- [ ] Security review: no raw tokens in logs, DB, or responses (except create response)
- [ ] Existing session auth flow unchanged (login, CSRF, Devise redirects all work)

Story points: sp:8
Estimate: 60 min Claude-pace
close_reason: Complete: PersonalAccessToken model + ApiTokenAuthenticatable concern + controller + rake tasks + rack-attack + settings + env vars + docs. 58 PAT tests + 102 contract tests = 160 total, 0 failures, 0 RuboCop offenses. 7/7 live API scenarios verified. Estimated ~60 min, actual ~45 min.

title: Add missing endpoints to domain cards + fix endpoint count mismatches
description:
Title: Add missing endpoints to domain cards + fix endpoint count mismatches

Description:

description:

title: Fix reviewsApi gold standard violations — triageReview + updateSection wrapping
description:
Title: Fix reviewsApi gold standard violations — triageReview + updateSection wrapping

Description:
Two functions in reviewsApi.js violate the API gold standard: triageReview passes payload directly without { review: } wrapping, and updateSection sends a flat body instead of { review: { section, audit_comment } }. Callers currently build the wrapper themselves, which is the leaky abstraction the gold standard eliminates. Fix both functions and update all callers.
Design doc: N/A — found by API consistency audit

Files:
- Modify: app/javascript/api/reviewsApi.js (triageReview line 32, updateSection line 16)
- Modify: app/javascript/services/triageService.js (if it builds { review: } wrapper)
- Modify: app/javascript/components/ (any callers that build the wrapper)
- Test: spec/javascript/api/reviewsApi.spec.js

First failing test:
spec/javascript/api/reviewsApi.spec.js — 'triageReview wraps payload in { review: } key'

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] triageReview(reviewId, data) wraps as api.patch(url, { review: data })
- [ ] updateSection(reviewId, section, auditComment) wraps as api.patch(url, { review: { section, audit_comment } })
- [ ] All callers updated to pass data only (no wrapper)
- [ ] Existing tests updated to expect wrapped payload
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
yarn test:unit --run spec/javascript/api/reviewsApi.spec.js && yarn test:unit --run

Decision points:
- Whether updateSection's audit_comment should be snake_case or camelCase in the JS function signature

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT leave callers wrapping — the whole point is API-side wrapping
- Do NOT change the HTTP method or URL — only the body wrapping

NOT in scope:
- Other reviewsApi functions (already gold standard)
- Backend controller changes (Rails reads params[:review] already)

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC checkbox — verify with evidence
- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated
- [ ] Run the exact Verification command — paste output
- [ ] git diff shows ONLY files listed in Files section

Story points: sp:2
Estimate: 8 min
close_reason: Not a bug. triageReview and updateSection correctly send flat params because the Rails controllers read params[:triage_status] and params[:section] at the top level, NOT under params[:review]. Wrapping in { review: } would silently break both endpoints. The gold standard wrapping convention applies to CRUD actions (create/update) that use strong params, not to lifecycle actions that read individual params. Verified by reading reviews_controller.rb lines 131-144 (triage) and 415 (section).

title: Fix BackupSerializer serialize_reactions N+1 — bulk preload reactions
description:
Title: Fix BackupSerializer serialize_reactions N+1 — bulk preload reactions

Description:
BackupSerializer#serialize_reactions calls review.reactions.includes(:user) per review inside a loop. The .includes on an already-loaded association proxy fires a separate query per review. For a component with 300 reviews, this is 300 queries. Fix by bulk-preloading reactions on the all_reviews collection before iteration.
Design doc: N/A — found by code quality audit

Files:
- Modify: app/services/export/serializers/backup_serializer.rb (preload reactions before loop)
- Test: spec/services/export/serializers/backup_serializer_spec.rb

spec/services/export/serializers/backup_serializer_spec.rb — 'serialize_reviews does not N+1 on reactions'

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Reactions bulk-preloaded on all_reviews before serialize_reviews loop
- [ ] Zero per-review reaction queries during serialization
- [ ] Export output unchanged (same JSON shape)
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
bundle exec rspec spec/services/export/serializers/backup_serializer_spec.rb

Decision points:
- Whether to use ActiveRecord::Associations::Preloader or .includes on the initial query

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT use .includes inside a per-record loop — that IS the N+1
- Do NOT change the export JSON shape

NOT in scope:
- Other export performance issues
- Changing the backup format

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC checkbox — verify with evidence
- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated
- [ ] Run the exact Verification command — paste output
- [ ] git diff shows ONLY files listed in Files section

Story points: sp:2
Estimate: 8 min
close_reason: Done. Estimated ~8 min, actual ~8 min. Added ActiveRecord::Associations::Preloader for reactions+users before serialize_reviews loop. Removed .includes(:user) from per-review call. Test: 50 reviews × 2 reactions = 100 reactions, ≤1 query. 38 backup serializer specs pass. RuboCop clean.
