title: Add Find & Replace API — 5 endpoints with undo
description:
Title: Add Find & Replace API — 5 endpoints with undo

Description:
v3.x has a full find-and-replace system (253-line API client) with instance-level targeting, field-level targeting, and audit-backed undo. v2.x only has basic POST /components/:id/find (text search). This is new functionality that enables bulk rule text editing across a component.
Design doc: docs/research/2026-06-05-api-completeness-analysis.md §8
Reference: ~/github/mitre/vulcan-v3.x/app/javascript/apis/findReplace.api.ts

Files:
- Create: app/controllers/api/find_replace_controller.rb
- Create: app/services/find_replace_service.rb
- Modify: config/routes.rb (add /api/components/:id/find_replace namespace)
- Create: doc/openapi/paths/api_components_{componentId}_find_replace_find.yaml
- Create: doc/openapi/paths/api_components_{componentId}_find_replace_replace_instance.yaml
- Create: doc/openapi/paths/api_components_{componentId}_find_replace_replace_field.yaml
- Create: doc/openapi/paths/api_components_{componentId}_find_replace_replace_all.yaml
- Create: doc/openapi/paths/api_components_{componentId}_find_replace_undo.yaml
- Test: spec/requests/api/find_replace_spec.rb
- Test: spec/services/find_replace_service_spec.rb

First failing test:
expect(post('/api/components/1/find_replace/find', params: {query: 'foo'})).to return matches with field and position context

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] POST find: returns matches with rule_id, field name, position, surrounding context
- [ ] POST replace_instance: replaces single match by rule_id + field + position
- [ ] POST replace_field: replaces all matches in one field of one rule
- [ ] POST replace_all: replaces all matches across all rules in component
- [ ] POST undo: reverts last replace operation using audit trail
- [ ] All operations respect section locks (locked sections cannot be replaced)
- [ ] All operations create audited entries for undo support
- [ ] Admin or author role required (viewer/reviewer cannot replace)
- [ ] OpenAPI specs + contract tests for all 5 endpoints
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
bundle exec rspec spec/requests/api/find_replace_spec.rb spec/services/find_replace_service_spec.rb && yarn openapi:bundle && yarn openapi:lint

Decision points:
- Should undo support multiple levels or just last operation? (Recommendation: last operation only for v1)
- Should replace operations use transactions for atomicity? (Recommendation: yes)

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT modify the existing POST /components/:id/find — it serves the current frontend
- Do NOT skip section lock checks — locked content must be immutable
- Do NOT add rubocop:disable to work around warnings

NOT in scope:
- Frontend find/replace UI (separate epic)
- Regex find/replace (v1 is literal text only)

title: Evaluate consolidating 24 Vue instances into 1 SPA — cost/benefit analysis
description:
Title: Evaluate consolidating 24 Vue instances — expert review complete, Option C recommended

Description:
6-agent expert review complete (83 findings). v3.x repo already has full SPA (112 sessions,
Vue Router, 13 Pinia stores, 30 routes). Key finding: v2.x is already 60% Vue 3 compatible
(Pinia, Composition API, composables). The real task is a build-system swap (Turbolinks → Vue
Router), NOT porting v3.x components (which need 371 Bootstrap 5→4 rewrites and 95 script-setup
conversions). Option C (build-system swap in v2.x) is recommended — lowest LOE, highest stability.
Full report: docs/research/2026-06-05-vue-consolidation-loe.md

Files:
- Read: docs/research/2026-06-05-vue-consolidation-loe.md (expert review report)
- Read: ~/github/mitre/vulcan-v3.x/app/javascript/routes/index.ts (v3.x route structure)
- Read: ~/github/mitre/vulcan-v3.x/app/javascript/stores/ (v3.x Pinia stores)
- Create: docs/research/vue-instance-consolidation-decision.md (final decision document)

First failing test:
N/A — decision card

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Expert review report read and understood (83 findings, 24 CRITICAL)
- [ ] v3.x codebase reviewed (routes, stores, pages, composables)
- [ ] Three options evaluated with data:
  - A: Port v3.x into v2.x — HIGH LOE (371 BVN rewrites), wrong direction
  - B: Merge v2.x into v3.x — HIGH LOE (40 migrations, lose test coverage)
  - C: Build-system swap in v2.x — LOW LOE (Turbolinks→Router+Pinia), RECOMMENDED
- [ ] Bootstrap 4→5 migration identified as separate concern from SPA architecture
- [ ] Decision documented with rationale
- [ ] v3.x route structure can be ported (routes compatible, factory swap only)
- [ ] v3.x Pinia stores port directly (composition API identical)
- [ ] 6 v2.x-only features identified that must be preserved (comments, triage, autosave, DISA guide, dark mode, section locks)
- [ ] Aaron made a decision

Verification:
Decision document reviewed by Aaron + Will

Decision points:
- Option C (build-system swap) vs full port — the data says C
- Should Bootstrap 5 migration be coupled with SPA or separate epic?
- Should v3.x route structure be used as-is or adapted for v2.x page structure?
- Timeline: do this before or after the Turbolinks removal epic (v2-9k7)?

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT port v3.x components that use Bootstrap-Vue-Next (371 rewrites)
- Do NOT attempt to merge 6 months of schema delta from v2.x into v3.x
- Do NOT assume v3.x is "ahead" — v2.x has more features and 6x more tests
- Do NOT add rubocop:disable/eslint-disable

NOT in scope:
- Actually doing the consolidation (separate epic)
- Bootstrap 5 migration (separate epic)
- Vue 3 upgrade (separate epic)

title: Address merge algorithm gaps — :manual strategy + orphaned replies + concurrent merge + cycle detection
description:
Title: Address merge algorithm gaps — :manual strategy + orphaned replies + concurrent merge + cycle detection

Description:
7 findings: (1) No :manual strategy for deferred human resolution. (2) Orphaned reply chains when
parent is only_theirs but children are matched. (3) Concurrent merge race condition — advisory lock
window unprotected. (4) CRITICAL: DFS cycle detection infinite-loops on self-referencing reviews.
(5) Partition invariant formula double-counts matched. (6) Timezone edge case in created_at check.
(7) Degenerate collision groups for short identical comments.

Files:
- Modify: Plan doc (update algorithm design)
- Test: spec/services/import/merge/ (edge case tests)

"cycle detection handles self-referencing review without infinite loop"

- [ ] :manual resolution strategy added to valid options
- [ ] Reply chain FK patching for matched children with only_theirs parents
- [ ] Advisory lock wraps entire analyze+apply as one atomic operation
- [ ] Self-referencing review (responding_to = self) detected before DFS
- [ ] Partition invariant formula corrected
- [ ] Timezone-aware created_at check (use Time.current not UTC comparison)
- [ ] Degenerate collision groups logged as warnings in MergePlan
- [ ] All work via TDD

data-turbolinks="false" attributes. Removing it is a 30-min mechanical migration (31 files).

the features we were going to hand-build in a Pinia store. Pinia still needed for UI state

event bus, useRuleSelection composable, SelectedRulesMixin, and manual localStorage persistence.

plus Gemfile and package.json. Zero architectural risk. Full audit completed 2026-06-05.

title: Build InspecBatchRegenerator — batch all rules in a component post-import
description:
Title: Build InspecBatchRegenerator — batch all rules in a component post-import

Description:
Create app/services/inspec/batch_regenerator.rb that regenerates inspec_control_file for all
rules in a component in a single pass. Uses InspecControlBuilder + InspecControlSerializer
internally. Replaces the N individual update_column calls that currently fire during
JSON archive import (one per rule via after_save). The batch regenerator suppresses the
per-rule callback during import, then runs one batch pass after all rules are saved.
This is the implementation of card v2-05f.68.7 — supersedes it.

- Create: app/services/inspec/batch_regenerator.rb
- Modify: app/services/import/json_archive/rule_builder.rb (suppress callback during build)

"InspecBatchRegenerator regenerates all rules in a component"

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] InspecBatchRegenerator.call(component) regenerates all rules' inspec_control_file
- [ ] Uses builder + serializer internally (no inline InSpec logic)
- [ ] Batch uses update_all or individual update_column in find_each (decide in implementation)
- [ ] JSON archive import suppresses per-rule callback during rule creation
- [ ] JSON archive import calls batch regenerator after all rules are saved
- [ ] Import of 50+ rules does NOT fire 50 individual after_save update_column calls

title: Harden Membership cascade — deadlock prevention + destroy! + remove redundant transaction
description:
Title: Harden Membership cascade — deadlock prevention + destroy! + remove redundant transaction

Description:
Three issues in Membership#remove_equal_or_lesser_component_permissions found by transaction
safety and Rails expert agents: (1) Concurrent membership creates on the same project can
deadlock — component_memberships.each(&:destroy) acquires row locks in non-deterministic order,
fix with sort_by(&:id); (2) Membership.transaction wrapper is redundant — after_save already
runs inside a transaction, this just creates a SAVEPOINT with no additional atomicity guarantee,
misleading readers; (3) destroy (not destroy!) silently swallows failed destroys — a membership
that fails to destroy is silently skipped. Also flagged: N+1 cascade through
update_admin_contact_info on each destroy — pre-existing but worth documenting.

Files:
- Modify: app/models/membership.rb (lines 62-65 — sorted destroy! without redundant transaction)
- Test: spec/models/membership_spec.rb (add concurrent cascade test if feasible, verify destroy! behavior)

First failing test:
"cascade destroy raises on failed membership destroy"

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] component_memberships.sort_by(&:id).each(&:destroy!) replaces current code
- [ ] Redundant Membership.transaction wrapper removed
- [ ] destroy! used instead of destroy for fail-fast behavior
- [ ] N+1 via update_admin_contact_info documented as known perf characteristic
- [ ] All work via TDD
- [ ] No regressions

Verification:
bundle exec rspec spec/models/membership_spec.rb

Decision points:
- Is the N+1 worth fixing now or is it acceptable for typical project sizes?
- Should we add an ORDER BY to the Membership.where query itself?

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT use destroy (silent failure) when destroy! (fail-fast) is appropriate
- Do NOT wrap in transaction when already inside a transaction (misleading)
- Do NOT acquire row locks in non-deterministic order

NOT in scope:
- Optimizing the N+1 (acceptable for typical sizes, document for future)
- Changes to update_admin_contact_info callback

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC — verify with evidence
- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated
- [ ] Run verification command — paste output

title: Clean up dead code — save_intent :admin_withdraw + unreachable absence validators
description:
Title: Clean up dead code — save_intent :admin_withdraw + unreachable absence validators

Description:
Two dead code issues found by 4+ agents independently: (1) save_intent = :admin_withdraw is
set in reviews_controller.rb:308 but never checked anywhere in the model — it works by
coincidence because admin_withdraw explicitly sets adjudicated_at which causes
auto_set_adjudicated to return early on its adjudicated_at.present? guard; (2) Absence
validators on review.rb lines 243-244 and 248-249 are permanently unreachable — the
before_validation :clear_stale_foreign_keys callback always clears the FK fields before
these validators fire, so they can never produce a validation error. Both are maintenance
traps that mislead future developers. Additionally, evaluate whether save_intent should use
Rails custom validation contexts (on: :reopen) instead of attr_accessor — research needed.

Files:
- Modify: app/controllers/reviews_controller.rb (remove dead save_intent = :admin_withdraw, add explanatory comment)
- Modify: app/models/review.rb (add documentation comments to absence validators explaining they are retained as documentation guards, or remove them)
- Test: spec/models/reviews_spec.rb (verify behavior unchanged after cleanup)

First failing test:
"admin_withdraw works without save_intent (adjudicated_at.present? guard is sufficient)"

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] save_intent = :admin_withdraw removed from reviews_controller.rb
- [ ] Comment added explaining why admin_withdraw doesn't need save_intent
- [ ] Absence validators documented as retained guards or removed with justification
- [ ] Research note added on save_intent vs validation contexts trade-off
- [ ] All work via TDD
- [ ] No regressions

Verification:
bundle exec rspec spec/requests/reviews_spec.rb spec/models/reviews_spec.rb

Decision points:
- Keep absence validators as documentation/regression guards, or remove as dead code?
- Evaluate save_intent attr_accessor vs Rails validation contexts (on: :reopen)
- If validators are kept, add comments explaining the before_validation interaction

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT leave dead code without documentation explaining why it exists
- Do NOT set values in controllers that are never read by the model

NOT in scope:
- Changing the before_validation callback placement
- Refactoring save_intent to validation contexts (research only)

title: Fix admin_restore semantic completeness — explicit FK clearing + triage attribution reset
description:
Title: Fix admin_restore semantic completeness — explicit FK clearing + triage attribution reset

Description:
Three issues in admin_restore found by cross-layer and security agents: (1) Does not clear
triage_set_by_id/triage_set_at — leaves ghost attribution on a 'pending' review, disposition
matrix shows triager name/timestamp from the overridden decision; (2) Implicitly relies on
clear_stale_foreign_keys callback to clear duplicate_of_review_id and addressed_by_rule_id
instead of setting them explicitly — hidden dependency on callback side-effect; (3) The
adjudicated_at timestamp is not in vulcan_audited only: list (documented Rails 7.1 YAML
limitation) — the transition timestamp is only recoverable from audit row's created_at.
Document this or find a workaround.

title: Fix weak test assertions — use exact fetch count instead of toBeGreaterThan
description:
Title: Fix weak test assertions — use exact fetch count instead of toBeGreaterThan

Description:
Multiple ComponentComments tests assert getComments.mock.calls.length is greater than
initialFetchCount. This would pass even if code made 5 spurious extra fetches. The requirement
is exactly ONE re-fetch per action. Weak assertions cannot detect excessive fetching, which is
a real performance bug.
Design doc: none

Files:
- Create: none
- Modify: spec/javascript/components/components/ComponentComments.spec.js
- Test: spec/javascript/components/components/ComponentComments.spec.js (self)

First failing test:
N/A — tests currently pass but with weak assertions. Tighten to exact counts.

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] All toBeGreaterThan(initialFetchCount) replaced with toBe(initialFetchCount + 1)
- [ ] Grep confirms zero toBeGreaterThan on mock.calls.length in this file
- [ ] Tests still pass with exact counts (proving exactly 1 refetch per action)
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
yarn test:unit spec/javascript/components/components/ComponentComments.spec.js

Decision points:
- If a test fails with exact count (more fetches than expected), that's a real bug — investigate, don't weaken the assertion

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT use toBeGreaterThan for fetch counts — exact counts catch performance regressions
- Do NOT use toBeGreaterThanOrEqual — same weakness as toBeGreaterThan

NOT in scope:
- Fixing excessive fetches if discovered (separate bug card)
- Adding fetch count assertions to other spec files

Before closing:

- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated

title: Add cache size cap — prevent unbounded memory growth in long sessions
description:
Title: Add cache size cap — prevent unbounded memory growth in long sessions

Description:
The store cache is a plain object that grows with each unique (componentId, params) combination.
Only eviction paths are invalidateCache (one componentId) and $reset (everything). A power user
navigating multiple components with different filters accumulates entries indefinitely. Add a
lightweight FIFO entry-count cap to bound memory usage.
Design doc: docs/development/state-management.md

Files:
- Create: none
- Modify: app/javascript/stores/comments.js
- Test: spec/javascript/stores/comments.spec.js

First failing test:
"setCacheEntry evicts oldest entry when cache exceeds MAX_CACHE_ENTRIES"

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] MAX_CACHE_ENTRIES constant (50) at module scope
- [ ] setCacheEntry checks entry count before adding — if at cap, removes oldest entry
- [ ] "Oldest" determined by insertion order (Object.keys first key in JS preserves insertion order)
- [ ] Test: adding 51st entry evicts the 1st
- [ ] Test: invalidateCache still works correctly with capped cache
- [ ] Test: $reset still clears everything
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
yarn test:unit spec/javascript/stores/comments.spec.js && yarn build

Decision points:
- Cap value: 50 entries is ~50-100KB for typical payloads. Adjust if profiling shows different needs.

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT use LRU or time-based eviction — FIFO is sufficient for this use case
- Do NOT add a WeakMap or external cache library — plain JS object with entry-count check
- Do NOT change the cache key format

NOT in scope:
- TTL-based expiry (fetch always returns cached if available — page-session lifecycle is fine)
- Per-entry size tracking

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC checkbox — verify with evidence
- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated
- [ ] Run the exact Verification command — paste output
- [ ] git diff shows ONLY files listed in Files section

Story points: sp:2
Estimate: 8 min
close_reason: Done. ~3 min. Added MAX_CACHE_ENTRIES=50 cap. setCacheEntry evicts oldest entry (FIFO) when at cap.

description:
Title: DRY mutation boilerplate — extract withMutation helper for store + withSubmitting for composables

Description:
Store mutation methods (postComment, postComponentComment, triageComment, bulkTriage) repeat identical
error-clear/try-catch/invalidate pattern 4 times. Composable methods (postComment, postComponentComment,
triage, bulkTriage) repeat identical submitting/error try-catch-finally wrapper 4 times. Extract
private helpers: mutateAndInvalidate for store, withSubmitting for composables. Mirrors the existing
fetchAndNormalize DRY pattern.
Design doc: docs/development/frontend-architecture.md §Layer 2

"mutateAndInvalidate wraps API call with error handling and cache invalidation"

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Private mutateAndInvalidate(apiFn, componentId, ...apiArgs) in store — shared by all 4 mutation methods
- [ ] Private withSubmitting(submitting, submitError, fn) in composable util — shared by all 4 composable methods
- [ ] Each store mutation method reduced to 1-2 lines calling the helper
- [ ] Each composable method reduced to 1-2 lines calling the helper
- [ ] Behavior unchanged — same error handling, same invalidation, same return values
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)

- Do NOT remove submitError/submitting refs — they're the public loading API for future template binding
- Do NOT merge store and composable helpers — they serve different layers

NOT in scope:

title: Fix shared loading/error refs — replace with per-fetch tracking or remove dead API
description:
Title: Fix shared loading/error refs — replace with per-fetch tracking or remove dead API

Description:
The store has single loading and error refs shared by all fetch methods. If two consumers fetch
concurrently (ComponentComments + CommentDedupBanner on mount), the first to resolve sets
loading=false while the second is in-flight. No current consumer reads store.loading or store.error
(they maintain local state), making this dead API that is misleading. Flagged by 5 of 8 reviewers.
Design doc: docs/development/state-management.md

Files:
- Create: none
- Modify: app/javascript/stores/comments.js
- Test: spec/javascript/stores/comments.spec.js

First failing test:
"concurrent fetchComments calls do not clobber each other's loading state"

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] loading replaced with reference counter (increment on start, decrement in finally) OR removed from public API
- [ ] error scoped per-operation OR removed from public API (consumers catch locally)
- [ ] If kept: concurrent fetch test proves loading stays true until ALL in-flight fetches resolve
- [ ] If removed: no consumer references store.loading or store.error (grep confirms)
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
yarn test:unit spec/javascript/stores/comments.spec.js && yarn build

Decision points:
- Keep loading/error as public API (with ref-counting fix) vs remove entirely? Check if any consumer or devtools integration reads them.

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT keep the single-boolean loading if concurrent fetches are possible
- Do NOT add per-method loading refs (too many) — ref-count or remove

NOT in scope:
- Per-consumer loading state (consumers already manage their own)
- Error reporting/telemetry integration

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC checkbox — verify with evidence
- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated
- [ ] Run the exact Verification command — paste output
- [ ] git diff shows ONLY files listed in Files section

Story points: sp:2
Estimate: 8 min
close_reason: Done. Estimated ~8 min, actual ~3 min. Replaced boolean loading with ref-counted loadingCount. loading is now a computed (loadingCount > 0). Concurrent fetches no longer clobber each other.

title: Fix updateRowInPlace — normalize payload before merging into store-normalized rows
description:
Title: Fix updateRowInPlace — normalize payload before merging into store-normalized rows

Description:
updateRowInPlace splices raw server response (snake_case only) into rows that have both snake_case
and camelCase fields from normalizeComment. After the splice, camelCase aliases retain old values
while snake_case has fresh values. Any future template using camelCase sees stale data. Same issue
in onTriageResponsePosted which increments responses_count but not responsesCount.
Design doc: docs/development/migration-roadmap.md §Normalizer Bridge Pattern

Files:
- Create: none
- Modify: app/javascript/components/components/ComponentComments.vue
- Test: spec/javascript/components/components/ComponentComments.spec.js

"updateRowInPlace normalizes payload — camelCase aliases match fresh snake_case values"

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] updateRowInPlace calls commentsStore.normalizeComment(updatedReview) before merging
- [ ] onTriageResponsePosted updates both responses_count AND responsesCount
- [ ] Test: after updateRowInPlace, row.triageStatus matches row.triage_status
- [ ] Test: after onTriageResponsePosted, row.responsesCount equals row.responses_count
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
yarn test:unit spec/javascript/components/components/ComponentComments.spec.js && yarn build

Decision points:
- none

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT skip normalization — raw merge creates stale alias fields
- Do NOT manually update individual camelCase fields — normalize the whole object

NOT in scope:
- Migrating templates to camelCase (Phase E)
- Normalizing pagination or status_counts

- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated

title: Port Bootstrap 5.3 dark mode component system — systematic audit + DRY centralization
description:
Title: Port Bootstrap 5.3 dark mode component system — systematic audit + DRY centralization

Description:
Bootstrap 5.3 implements dark mode via CSS custom properties that auto-adapt
every component. Vulcan's dark mode is a partial port of this system onto
Bootstrap 4.6.2, but many components still use ad-hoc overrides instead of
the 5.3 pattern. This session's toast fix established the correct approach:
one @each loop generating variant tints for all colored components.

Apply this systematically to every Bootstrap component in the dark mode block.

REFERENCE: https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.3/customize/color-modes/
REFERENCE: https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.3/components/toasts/#css
REFERENCE: https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.3/components/alerts/#css
REFERENCE: https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.3/customize/css-variables/

THE PATTERN (established this session):
1. Light mode: Bootstrap 4 defaults — DO NOT TOUCH
2. Dark mode: [data-bs-theme="dark"] block overrides component selectors
3. Variant colors: ONE @each loop with shared tint formula
4. Specificity: must match or exceed BootstrapVue's selectors
5. Bootstrap 5.3 source is the reference for WHAT to override per component

Files:
- Modify: app/javascript/application.scss
- Modify: docs/development/design-system.md
- Test: Playwright verification all variants in both modes

First failing test:
Playwright: badge-outline-success is unreadable in dark mode (known issue)

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Badge .badge-outline-* variants: @each loop (currently 6 separate blocks)
- [ ] Badge .bg-* variants: @each loop (currently 5 separate blocks)
- [ ] All hardcoded rgba(255,255,255,...) in dark block replaced with --vulcan-* vars
- [ ] Triage status --status-color uses --vulcan-*-tint vars (not inline mix())
- [ ] Alert + toast + badge tint formula uses SAME mix percentages from shared @each
- [ ] Toast variant selectors include .b-toast-solid for specificity (0,4,0)
- [ ] Design system docs: new "Dark Mode Component Overrides" section covering:
  - The pattern (variables in :root + [data-bs-theme="dark"], never global !important)
  - The @each variant tint formula with Sass mix() values
  - The specificity rule (must beat BootstrapVue selector depth)
  - The "don't touch light mode" rule
  - The Bootstrap 5.3 reference links for each component
  - Table: which BS4 components have been ported vs remaining
- [ ] Playwright screenshots: toast (success/danger/warning/info), alert variants,
      badge variants — all in BOTH light and dark mode
- [ ] All work via TDD
- [ ] No regressions

Verification:
yarn build && yarn test:unit && Playwright screenshots light + dark for each variant

Decision points:
- Tint text: 60% mix or 40%? (60% established for toasts, alerts still at 60% from DRY loop)
- Should we add --vulcan-{variant}-bg-subtle / --vulcan-{variant}-border-subtle / --vulcan-{variant}-text-emphasis variables matching Bootstrap 5.3's naming?

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT add global component rules — dark overrides go ONLY in [data-bs-theme="dark"]
- Do NOT use !important unless matching existing pattern (.card uses it for load-order defense)
- Do NOT hardcode colors — use Sass mix() or --vulcan-* vars
- Do NOT guess at values — READ Bootstrap 5.3 source for the component first

NOT in scope:
- New components not yet in the dark mode block
- Vue component refactors
- VulcanMarkdown module extraction (v2-k96.1)

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC — verify with evidence
- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated
- [ ] Run verification command — paste output
- [ ] git diff shows ONLY files listed in Files section
- [ ] Design system docs updated and reviewed

Story points: sp:5
Estimate: 25 min

title: Fix ADNM export — Check/Fix must be blank for nested rules
description:
Title: Fix ADNM export — Check/Fix must be blank for nested rules

Description:
When a rule is nested (satisfied_by a parent), export_fixtext and export_checktext
return the parent's content. Per DISA V4R1 §4.1.11/§4.1.13, Check and Fix must be

title: Fix useCommentReactions silent error swallowing — add error feedback
description:
Title: Fix useCommentReactions silent error swallowing — add error feedback

Description:
Expert review finding #11 (Security agent): useCommentReactions catch block
(line 34) silently swallows errors — no console.error, no toast, no error ref.
If API returns 403 or 422, user sees reaction toggle snap back with zero
explanation. Every other composable stores errors in a submitError ref and
re-throws. This one does neither.

title: Fix cacheKey fragility + make private + standardize param order
description:
Title: Fix cacheKey fragility + make private + standardize param order

Description:
Expert review findings #10, #12, #15 (API + Vue + Future agents): cacheKey
uses JSON.stringify which is key-order dependent — {a:1,b:2} and {b:2,a:1}
produce different keys for identical queries. Also cacheKey is exposed as
public store API but is an implementation detail. Also componentId parameter
position is inconsistent across store actions (first in postComment, last in
triageComment).
Design doc: docs/development/state-management.md

Files:
- Modify: app/javascript/stores/comments.js
- Test: spec/javascript/stores/comments.spec.js

First failing test:
"cacheKey produces identical keys for {a:1,b:2} and {b:2,a:1}"

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] cacheKey sorts object keys before JSON.stringify
- [ ] cacheKey removed from store return object (closure-scoped private)
- [ ] componentId is consistently first parameter in all mutation actions
- [ ] Store action signatures: postComment(componentId, ruleId, data), triageComment(componentId, reviewId, payload), bulkTriage(componentId, reviewIds, payload)
- [ ] All composable callers updated for new param order
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
yarn test:unit --run spec/javascript/stores/ spec/javascript/composables/ && yarn build

Decision points:
- none

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT expose implementation details (cacheKey) as public store API
- Do NOT use JSON.stringify without sorting keys

NOT in scope:
- Cache TTL strategy
- Normalizer changes (separate card)

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC checkbox — verify with evidence
- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated
- [ ] Run the exact Verification command — paste output
- [ ] git diff shows ONLY files listed in Files section

Estimate: 8 min
close_reason: Done. Estimated ~8 min, actual ~6 min. cacheKey sorts object keys before JSON.stringify (deterministic). cacheKey removed from public store API (closure-scoped). componentId standardized as first param on triageComment + bulkTriage. Composable callers updated. 30 tests pass.

[2026-06-04 03:38] DESIGN DECISION: normalizeComment uses spread-then-override pattern (`{ ...raw, camelCaseAlias: raw.snake_case }`). Templates that reference snake_case continue working — migration to camelCase is incremental across future cards, not forced in this migration. title: Build CommentItem + CommentBody + CommentActions compound components
description:
Title: Build CommentItem + CommentBody + CommentActions compound components

Description:
Create the compound component set for rendering a single comment. Uses named
scoped slots so consumers control layout while sharing rendering logic. Based
on Vue 2.7 slot patterns + Bootstrap-Vue b-media for avatar layout.

Architecture (slot-based compound component):
- CommentItem: outer wrapper, accepts comment prop, exposes named slots
  - #header: default renders CommentAuthorLine + SectionLabel + timestamp
  - #body: default renders comment text with white-space pre-wrap
  - #status: default renders TriageStatusBadge
  - #actions: default renders CommentActions (reactions + thread)
  - #extra: empty slot for consumer-specific content (triage form, merge UI)
- CommentBody: text display + imported badge (extracted from 4 consumers)
- CommentActions: ReactionButtons + CommentThread bundled (they always co-occur)

Consumers override slots they need different, use defaults for everything else.

Files:
- Create: app/javascript/components/shared/CommentItem.vue

title: Build useCommentsStore — centralized comment state with fetch/cache/triage
description:
Title: Build useCommentsStore — centralized comment state with fetch/cache/triage

Description:
Implement the Pinia Setup Store for comments. Centralizes the data fetching
(getComments, getReviewResponses), caching (keyed by component+filters),
triage mutations (optimistic update + API call + rollback on error), and
reply state. Currently each of the 4 consumers independently calls the API
and manages its own state — this store becomes the single source of truth.

Uses Setup Store pattern (defineStore with setup function) to match v3.x
architecture. Existing Options API consumers use mapState/mapActions.

Files:
- Modify: app/javascript/stores/comments.js (full implementation)
- Create: app/javascript/composables/useCommentReactions.js (extract from ReactionToggleMixin)
- Create: app/javascript/composables/useCommentThread.js (extract from CommentThread data/methods)
- Test: spec/javascript/stores/comments.spec.js (full store tests)
- Test: spec/javascript/composables/useCommentReactions.spec.js
- Test: spec/javascript/composables/useCommentThread.spec.js

First failing test:
"useCommentsStore.fetchComments calls getComments API and caches result" — mock API, verify store state

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] fetchComments(componentId, params) calls API and stores result in reactive state
- [ ] Cache keyed by componentId + serialized params — second call returns cached
- [ ] invalidateCache(componentId) clears cache for that component
- [ ] triageComment(reviewId, payload) does optimistic update + API call + rollback
- [ ] addReply(parentId, comment) calls API and appends to thread cache
- [ ] useCommentReactions composable replaces ReactionToggleMixin for comment components
- [ ] useCommentThread composable replaces CommentThread data/fetch/toggle logic
- [ ] All composables testable in isolation (no component mount needed)
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
yarn test:unit --run spec/javascript/stores/ spec/javascript/composables/ && yarn build

Decision points:
- Cache invalidation strategy: TTL vs manual invalidate — start with manual, add TTL if needed
- If triage optimistic update is complex, implement pessimistic first, optimize later

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT put rendering logic in the store — store is data only
- Do NOT import Vue components in the store
- Do NOT use Options Store syntax — Setup Store only

NOT in scope:
- Component migration to use the store (next card)
- Tree threading data structure (v2-05f.60)

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC checkbox — verify with evidence
- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated
- [ ] Run the exact Verification command — paste output
- [ ] git diff shows ONLY files listed in Files section

Estimate: 30 min
close_reason: Done. Estimated ~30 min, actual ~12 min. useCommentsStore: fetch/cache/invalidate/normalize/ (13 tests). useCommentReactions: optimistic toggle + API + rollback (8 tests). useCommentThread: expand/collapse/fetch/refresh (5 tests). 26 total tests, all composables testable without component mount.

title: Install Pinia + create shared Vue instance setup helper
description:

PiniaVuePlugin + createPinia() for all 22 Vue instances. Vue 2.7 has Composition
API built in — no @vue/composition-api needed. The shared helper replaces the
repeated Vue.use(BootstrapVue) + Vue.use(IconsPlugin) pattern with a single
createVulcanApp() that also installs Pinia.

title: [EPIC] Comment system DRY extraction — Pinia store + composables + compound components
description:
Title: [EPIC] Comment system DRY extraction — Pinia store + composables + compound components

Description:
Restructure the comment rendering system using the four-layer architecture
documented in docs/development/frontend-architecture.md:
Layer 1 (API) → Layer 2 (Stores + Composables) → Layer 3 (Containers) → Layer 4 (Presentational).

Key design: store normalizes API responses to a stable shape, so DB 3NF changes
only affect the normalizer. Composables replace mixins. Presentational components
use slots for layout flexibility. Container/Page components are the ONLY ones
that touch stores.

Architecture doc: docs/development/frontend-architecture.md
State management guide: docs/development/state-management.md

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] All child cards closed
- [ ] Layer boundaries respected (no presentational component imports a store)
- [ ] Store normalizer produces stable shape regardless of API response format
- [ ] Full Playwright verification light + dark at 375px, 768px, 1440px
- [ ] All tests pass — no regressions

Story points: sp:13
Estimate: 120 min

title: Refactor seed system — YAML-driven data, shared context, infrastructure wrapper
description:
Title: Refactor seed system — YAML-driven data, shared context, infrastructure wrapper

Description:
The seed system has 14 files that each independently resolve users, projects,
and components via scattered find_by calls. Memberships are created in 3+
files. Idempotency patterns are inconsistent (find_or_create_by vs unless
exists? vs helpers). Researched Discourse, GitLab, Mastodon, Chatwoot, and
Forem seed architectures. Adopting: Chatwoot's YAML data files for declarative
thread/conversation definitions, GitLab's infrastructure wrapper to suppress
side effects, Mastodon's create_with().find_or_create_by upsert pattern, and
a shared SeedContext for centralized lookups. No new gems — pure Ruby.
Design doc: research report from seed architecture analysis (2026-06-03)

Files:
- Create: lib/seed_context.rb (shared context — users, projects, components, rules)
- Create: db/seeds/data/threads.yml (declarative thread definitions — Chatwoot pattern)
- Modify: lib/seed_helpers.rb (add infrastructure wrapper, upsert helpers, orphan cleanup)
- Modify: db/seeds.rb (instantiate SeedContext, pass to seed files, wrap in quiet block)
- Modify: db/seeds/data/00_users.rb (use SeedContext for user pool)
- Modify: db/seeds/data/01_projects.rb (register projects in SeedContext)
- Modify: db/seeds/data/04_components.rb (register components in SeedContext)
- Modify: db/seeds/data/05_memberships.rb (centralize ALL memberships — DRY)
- Modify: db/seeds/data/10_comments.rb (read from threads.yml, use seed_thread)
- Modify: db/seeds/data/13_container_srg_test.rb (use SeedContext)
- Test: verify via rails db:seed idempotent re-run + rails dev:verify

First failing test:
"rails db:seed runs idempotently with SeedContext" — run twice, verify identical counts

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] SeedContext class: resolves ALL users once at initialization, provides users/projects/components/rules hashes
- [ ] Every seed file receives SeedContext instead of doing its own User.find_by lookups
- [ ] threads.yml: all comment thread definitions as YAML (author key, section, comment text, replies, triage)
- [ ] 10_comments.rb reads threads.yml and calls seed_thread for each — no inline thread definitions
- [ ] Infrastructure wrapper: suppress Devise emails + audit logging during seed (GitLab pattern)
- [ ] Membership creation centralized in 05_memberships.rb ONLY — removed from all other files
- [ ] create_with().find_or_create_by pattern used for users and projects (Mastodon pattern)
- [ ] Orphan cleanup runs at seed start (stale commentable_id, stale membership references)
- [ ] Re-running rails db:seed produces identical counts (idempotent)
- [ ] rails dev:verify passes after seed
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
rails db:seed && rails db:seed && rails dev:verify && rails runner "puts SeedHelpers.status_report.inspect"

Decision points:
- If a seed file has complex logic beyond CRUD (e.g., XML import in 13_container_srg_test.rb), keep the logic in Ruby but use SeedContext for lookups
- If threads.yml becomes too large (>500 lines), split into per-project YAML files

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT add seed-fu, seedbank, or any new gems — pure Ruby + YAML
- Do NOT use FactoryBot for seeds — factories are for test isolation
- Do NOT use destroy_all clean slate — additive idempotency only
- Do NOT hardcode user emails or component IDs in seed files — always resolve via SeedContext
- Do NOT scatter membership creation across files — ONE file owns memberships

NOT in scope:
- Admin-driven re-seeding UI (Discourse pattern — future feature)
- Production/development directory split (current env var gate is sufficient)
- SEEDS_MULTIPLIER volume scaling (Forem pattern — not needed)
- Stress test data generation (separate card v2-lg1)

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC checkbox — verify with evidence
- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated
- [ ] Run the exact Verification command — paste output
- [ ] git diff shows ONLY files listed in Files section

Story points: sp:5
Estimate: 30 min
close_reason: Done. Estimated ~30 min, actual ~25 min. Created SeedContext class, threads.yml YAML data file, SeedHelpers.quiet infrastructure wrapper, load_threads with normalization. Removed duplicate membership creation from 10_comments.rb. Centralized community persona roles in 05_memberships.rb. 26 tests, idempotent seed verified.

title: Add recursive reply tree threading to CommentThread — Reddit-style nested conversations
description:
Title: Add recursive reply tree threading to CommentThread — Reddit-style nested conversations

Description:
Currently all replies render flat (same indent level) regardless of which
comment they respond to. User chose Option B: full tree threading (Reddit
style) — each reply nests visually under the comment it responds to, with
increasing indent per depth level. The API already returns
responding_to_review_id on each reply, so tree building is client-side only.
ONE shared recursive component handles all consumers: triage split-pane,
comment table, rule editor modal, and user comments page.
Design doc: none (design via ASCII mockups with user before implementation)

Files:
- Create: app/javascript/components/shared/CommentReplyTree.vue (recursive tree renderer)
- Modify: app/javascript/components/shared/CommentThread.vue (use CommentReplyTree instead of flat v-for)
- Modify: app/javascript/utils/buildReplyTree.js (pure function: flat array → tree structure)
- Modify: app/controllers/reviews_controller.rb (add commenter_email to reply response)
- Test: spec/javascript/components/shared/CommentReplyTree.spec.js
- Test: spec/javascript/utils/buildReplyTree.spec.js

First failing test:
"buildReplyTree groups replies by responding_to_review_id into nested children arrays" — pure function test with flat input, expect tree output

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] buildReplyTree.js: pure function converts flat reply array into tree by responding_to_review_id
- [ ] CommentReplyTree.vue: recursive component renders b-media with nested children
- [ ] Each nesting level indents via native b-media nesting (aside + body)
- [ ] Reply button on a reply creates response to THAT reply (not the parent)
- [ ] All 5 consumers use the same shared components (DRY, centralized):
      - TriageSplitView (triage split-pane)
      - ComponentComments (comment table)
      - CommentTriageModal (rule editor modal)
      - RuleReviews (rule editor inline comments — from user's screenshot)
      - UserComments (My Comments page)
- [ ] UserBadge renders on every tree node (avatar + popover)
- [ ] Triage status background tints apply per-comment (not inherited from parent)
- [ ] Reply endpoint returns commenter_email for UserBadge popover
- [ ] Works in dark mode
- [ ] Design system compliance verified (--vulcan-* variables)
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
yarn test:unit -- --reporter verbose CommentReplyTree buildReplyTree && yarn build && bin/parallel_rspec spec/

Decision points:
- ASCII mockup of tree layout BEFORE coding — get user approval on indent depth, max nesting, visual treatment
- If tree depth exceeds 4 levels, discuss whether to cap with "in reply to @Name" indicator
- If Reply button targeting changes break any consumer's composer flow, stop and assess

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT scatter tree rendering logic across consumers — ONE shared CommentReplyTree component
- Do NOT build the tree on the server — client-side from the flat API response
- Do NOT change the API response shape — add fields, don't restructure
- Do NOT duplicate tree logic — one buildReplyTree.js utility, tested independently

NOT in scope:
- Collapsing/expanding individual sub-threads (future enhancement)
- Threading indicators (vertical lines connecting parent to child — future polish)
- Real-time reply updates (WebSocket push — separate feature)
- Profile image upload (UserBadge uses initials for now)

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC checkbox — verify with evidence
- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated
- [ ] Run the exact Verification command — paste output
- [ ] git diff shows ONLY files listed in Files section

Story points: sp:5
Estimate: 30 min
notes: [2026-06-03] User directive: MUST fully read and review https://bootstrap-vue.org/docs/components/media before starting implementation. Understand nesting, aside slots, and responsive behavior from the docs — do not implement from memory.

title: Add realistic comment thread seed data — multi-pattern visual testing
description:
Title: Add realistic comment thread seed data — multi-pattern visual testing

Description:
The current seed data (10_comments.rb) has decent single-reply threads but
lacks the diverse thread patterns needed to visually test UserBadge, b-media
nesting, triage statuses, and the conversation chain UX. We need: multi-reply
chains (3-4 users going back and forth), deeply nested sub-threads, threads
with mixed triage statuses, imported commenter data, long-form comments that
test wrapping, and edge cases (empty name, email-only authors). This is the
foundation for all visual UX testing — without it we're flying blind.
Design doc: none (seed data, not feature code)

Files:
- Modify: db/seeds/data/10_comments.rb (add thread patterns)
- Modify: lib/seed_helpers.rb (add helper if needed for multi-reply seeding)
- Create: none
- Test: rails db:seed idempotent re-run + Playwright visual verification

First failing test:
"rails db:seed produces at least 3 comments with 3+ replies each" — verify thread depth exists

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Multi-reply thread: 3-4 users exchanging 4-5 replies on one parent comment (back-and-forth conversation)
- [ ] Rapid-fire thread: 2 users going back and forth quickly (simulates real-time discussion)
- [ ] Mixed-status thread: parent triaged as concur, but replies include dissenting follow-ups
- [ ] Imported commenter thread: reply from commenter_imported=true with no user record (tests fallback initials)
- [ ] Long comment thread: one reply with 3+ paragraphs (tests text wrapping with avatar indent)
- [ ] Single-reply thread: quick acknowledgment (already exists, verify preserved)
- [ ] Zero-reply comments: parent with no replies (already exists, verify preserved)
- [ ] Component-level comment thread: replies to a component-scoped (no rule) comment
- [ ] All data idempotent — re-running rails db:seed does not duplicate
- [ ] At least 5 distinct users visible in thread avatars (tests UserBadge initials diversity)
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
rails db:seed && rails runner "puts Review.where.not(responding_to_review_id: nil).count" shows 15+

Decision points:
- If the Container Platform component doesn't have enough rules for all thread patterns, use additional rules
- If a new user persona is needed (e.g., imported commenter), add to SeedHelpers::COMMUNITY_PERSONAS

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT use random/generated comment text — write realistic STIG review comments
- Do NOT break idempotency — all seeds use find_or_seed pattern
- Do NOT create orphan replies (responding_to a nonexistent parent)
- Do NOT seed data that violates model validations

NOT in scope:
- Changing the UserBadge component (separate card)
- Changing the comment thread rendering (already done)
- Automated visual regression tests (Playwright screenshots are manual verification)
- Stress test / load test data (separate card v2-lg1)

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC checkbox — verify with evidence
- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated
- [ ] Run the exact Verification command — paste output
- [ ] git diff shows ONLY files listed in Files section

Story points: sp:3
Estimate: 15 min
close_reason: Done. Estimated ~15 min, actual ~20 min. Refactored 10_comments.rb to declarative thread patterns via SeedHelpers.seed_thread. Added 5 diverse thread patterns (multi-reply, rapid-fire, mixed-status, long-form, component-scoped). Fixed orphaned review bug (stale commentable_id). Fixed find_or_seed_reply for component-scoped parents. Added cleanup_orphaned_reviews! helper. 121 replies, 5 deep threads, 10 distinct authors. NOTE: broader seed system needs full DRY/centralized refactor — all 14 seed files use scattered lookups, duplicated membership creation, inconsistent idempotency. Separate card.

title: Polish openapi_first PR #479 + post technical response to ahx
description:
Title: Polish openapi_first PR #479 + post technical response to ahx

The openapi_first maintainer (ahx) asked 3 specific questions about OAS 3.2

Key finding shared: most features ahx asked about are NOT in the published 3.2.0 spec (release notes vs published spec discrepancy).

title: Add additionalOperations support to openapi_first PR #479
description:
Title: Add additionalOperations support to openapi_first PR #479

Description:
Expert review found that OAS 3.2 additionalOperations (non-standard HTTP
methods like COPY, LINK) are completely invisible in openapi_first. Builder
only checks keys.intersection(REQUEST_METHODS), silently ignoring operations
under additionalOperations. The fix is ~10-15 lines in Builder#router: after
the REQUEST_METHODS loop, iterate path_item_object['additionalOperations']
and process each entry identically. This gives the PR real substance beyond
version acceptance. All changes via GitHub API to aaronlippold/openapi_first fork.

Files:
- Modify: lib/openapi_first/builder.rb (add additionalOperations iteration)
- Modify: spec/definition_spec.rb (add additionalOperations test)
- Create: none
- Test: spec/definition_spec.rb (on fork)

First failing test:
"routes requests to operations defined under additionalOperations" — document with COPY method under additionalOperations, verify it is discovered

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Builder#router iterates additionalOperations after REQUEST_METHODS
- [ ] Operations under additionalOperations are registered in the router
- [ ] Test: document with additionalOperations COPY method is parsed correctly
- [ ] Test: paths are discoverable for non-standard methods
- [ ] Existing REQUEST_METHODS behavior unchanged (no regression)
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
Fork CI passes (or local test verification documented)

Decision points:
- If Builder#router has changed since our fork branched (upstream is active — v3.4.3 released May 29), rebase first
- If additionalOperations needs more than ~15 lines, stop and reassess

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT modify REQUEST_METHODS constant
- Do NOT change how standard HTTP methods are handled
- Do NOT assume method case — additionalOperations keys are uppercase per spec

NOT in scope:
- discriminator.defaultMapping support (json_schemer responsibility)
- itemSchema/prefixEncoding/itemEncoding (architectural streaming change)
- Full multipart validation improvements

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC checkbox — verify with evidence
- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated
- [ ] Run the exact Verification command — paste output
- [ ] PR diff verified on GitHub

Story points: sp:3
Estimate: 15 min
close_reason: Done. Estimated ~15 min, actual ~15 min. Added additionalOperations support to openapi_first Builder#router. Extracted register_operation helper (DRY). Rebased on upstream v3.4.3. 3 tests, 570 total examples, 0 failures, 100% coverage.

title: Complete json_schemer PR #230 — full 3.2 document schema + discriminator.defaultMapping
description:
Title: Complete json_schemer PR #230 — full 3.2 document schema + discriminator.defaultMapping

Description:
Expert review found that our PR claims "3.2 support" but document validation
rejects ALL 3.2-specific fields because document.rb uses unevaluatedProperties:
false on 29 objects. User directive: do it fully, no follow-ups. This card
updates document.rb with all ~20 new 3.2 fields across ~15 objects (Tag, Server,
Response, Discriminator, Example, Media Type, Encoding, Path Item, OAuth Flows,
Security Scheme, Components, OpenAPI Object, Parameter, XML), adds discriminator
defaultMapping runtime support in the validator, rewrites PR description/CHANGELOG
to match, and adds comprehensive tests. All via GitHub API to fork.

Files:
- Modify: lib/json_schemer/openapi31/document.rb (~15 object schemas need new properties)
- Modify: lib/json_schemer/openapi31/vocab/base.rb (Discriminator#validate — defaultMapping fallback)
- Modify: test/open_api_test.rb (boundary tests + 3.2 feature tests + defaultMapping test)
- Modify: CHANGELOG.md (accurate scope)
- Modify: README.md (accurate scope)
- Create: none
- Test: test/open_api_test.rb (on fork)

First failing test:
test_openapi_3_2_document_with_tag_parent — document using tag.parent (3.2 field) passes openapi.valid?

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] document.rb updated: Tag gets summary, parent, kind fields
- [ ] document.rb updated: Server gets name field
- [ ] document.rb updated: Response description no longer required, gets summary
- [ ] document.rb updated: Discriminator gets defaultMapping field
- [ ] document.rb updated: Example gets dataValue, serializedValue fields
- [ ] document.rb updated: Media Type gets itemSchema, prefixEncoding, itemEncoding
- [ ] document.rb updated: Encoding gets nested encoding, prefixEncoding, itemEncoding
- [ ] document.rb updated: Path Item gets query, additionalOperations
- [ ] document.rb updated: OAuth Flows gets deviceAuthorization
- [ ] document.rb updated: OAuth Flow gets deviceAuthorizationUrl
- [ ] document.rb updated: Security Scheme gets oauth2MetadataUrl, deprecated
- [ ] document.rb updated: Components gets mediaTypes
- [ ] document.rb updated: OpenAPI Object gets $self
- [ ] document.rb updated: Parameter in supports querystring, cookie style supports cookie
- [ ] document.rb updated: XML gets nodeType
- [ ] Discriminator#validate supports defaultMapping fallback when property absent or unmapped
- [ ] Test: 3.2.1 accepted, 3.3.0 rejected (boundary tests)
- [ ] Test: document with 3.2 fields passes openapi.valid?
- [ ] Test: discriminator with defaultMapping validates correctly
- [ ] PR description rewritten with accurate scope
- [ ] CHANGELOG reflects full 3.2 support
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
All tests on fork pass including new 3.2 document validation and defaultMapping tests

Decision points:
- Read document.rb FULLY before modifying — understand the schema structure
- If a 3.2 field has complex validation semantics beyond simple property addition, stop and research
- If discriminator defaultMapping interacts with existing mapping logic in unexpected ways, stop and assess

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT guess at field types — read the OAS 3.2 spec for each field
- Do NOT use sed
- Do NOT add properties without understanding their constraints (required, type, enum, etc.)
- Do NOT break existing 3.0/3.1 document validation

NOT in scope:
- Runtime support for streaming multipart (itemSchema validation at parse time — that's openapi_first's domain)
- Runtime support for additionalOperations routing (that's openapi_first's domain)

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC checkbox — verify with evidence
- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated
- [ ] Run the exact Verification command — paste output
- [ ] PR diff verified on GitHub

Story points: sp:5
Estimate: 30 min
close_reason: Done. Estimated ~30 min, actual ~25 min. Full OAS 3.2.0 document schema support: 8 new fields across 5 objects + new media-type-or-reference def + querystring enum value. 208 tests, 0 failures, 100% coverage. Gate 14 saved us — expert review agent listed ~20 nonexistent fields from release notes vs published spec.

title: Polish swagcov PR #202 — fix newlines, test quality, edge cases
description:
Title: Polish swagcov PR #202 — fix newlines, test quality, edge cases

Description:
Expert review found quality issues in our swagcov PR #202. Missing trailing
newlines on all 3 files, overly broad test assertion, and missing edge case
tests (nested optionals, base-path-only). Also need to reply to Copilot's
incorrect YAML indentation comment. All changes pushed via GitHub API to
aaronlippold/swagcov fix/optional-route-segments branch.

- Modify: spec/fixtures/openapi/optional_segments.yml (trailing newline)
- Create: none
- Test: spec/swagcov/openapi_files_spec.rb (on fork)

First failing test:
"does not raise RegexpError on routes with nested optional segments" — new test for /foo(/:bar(/:baz))

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] All 3 files end with trailing newline (no "No newline at end of file" in diff)
- [ ] raise_error assertion narrowed to raise_error(RegexpError)
- [ ] Test added: nested optional segments /foo(/:bar(/:baz)) does not crash
- [ ] Test added: base-path-only OpenAPI (route with optional segment, spec only has base path) returns nil
- [ ] Copilot YAML indentation comment replied to (dismissed — our format matches upstream conventions)
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
gh pr diff 202 --repo smridge/swagcov | grep -c "No newline at end of file" returns 0

Decision points:
- If maintainer responds with different feedback before we push, adjust accordingly

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT use sed — push files via GitHub API
- Do NOT add PUT/PATCH equivalence to this PR (separate concern)
- Do NOT fix the pre-existing trailing ? regex bug (note it, don't fix)

NOT in scope:

- Pre-existing trailing ? bug in regex
- Switching Vulcan Gemfile to fork (wait for upstream merge)

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC checkbox — verify with evidence
- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated
- [ ] Run the exact Verification command — paste output
- [ ] PR diff verified on GitHub

Story points: sp:2
Estimate: 10 min
close_reason: Done. Estimated ~10 min, actual ~20 min (extra time: skill update for Gate 14, researching RSpec raise_error docs after blindly following wrong agent recommendation). 3 files fixed (newlines), 3 edge case tests added, 3 Copilot comments replied to. 139 full suite tests pass, 0 warnings.

title: Add PATCH operations to 5 OpenAPI path specs — REST best practice compliance
description:
Title: Add PATCH operations to 5 OpenAPI path specs — REST best practice compliance

Description:
5 OpenAPI path specs document PUT but not PATCH for update operations. Rails generates
BOTH PATCH and PUT routes for every update action (PATCH is primary since Rails 4).
OpenAPI best practice: document both with correct semantics (PATCH=partial, PUT=full).
swagcov reports these as 5 uncovered routes because of the missing PATCH specs.

Files:
- Modify: doc/openapi/paths/users_{userId}.yaml (add patch:)
- Modify: doc/openapi/paths/memberships_{membershipId}.yaml (add patch:)
- Modify: doc/openapi/paths/projects_{projectId}.yaml (add patch:)
- Modify: doc/openapi/paths/rules_{ruleId}.yaml (add patch:)
- Modify: doc/openapi/paths/reviews_{reviewId}.yaml (add patch:)
- Test: yarn openapi:bundle && yarn openapi:lint && bundle exec swagcov

First failing test:
bundle exec swagcov shows 5 PATCH routes as "none"

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Each spec has both put: and patch: operations
- [ ] patch: has correct operationId (patchUser, patchProject, etc.)
- [ ] patch: description notes "partial update — send only changed fields"
- [ ] put: description notes "full replacement — all fields required"
- [ ] OpenAPI bundles and lints clean
- [ ] swagcov shows 0 uncovered PATCH routes
- [ ] Contract tests still pass
- [ ] Use Read + Edit tool ONLY — NEVER sed

Verification:
yarn openapi:bundle && yarn openapi:lint && bundle exec swagcov | grep -c none

Decision points:
- None — straightforward addition

Anti-patterns:
- NEVER use sed or shell text manipulation on these files
- Do NOT batch — one file at a time with Read + Edit
- Do NOT rush

NOT in scope:
- Changing controller behavior
- Adding new endpoints

Before closing:
- [ ] Each file read before editing
- [ ] OpenAPI valid
- [ ] swagcov coverage improved

Story points: sp:2
Estimate: 15 min
close_reason: Done. Estimated ~15 min, actual ~8 min. Added PATCH operations to 4 OpenAPI path specs (users, memberships, projects, rules). reviews/:id has PUT-only in Rails — no PATCH needed. swagcov PATCH coverage: 4 none → 0 none. 116 contract tests pass.

title: Add triage response template management UI — create, edit, delete

Reverse OpenAPI coverage test added (pending — catches 89 undocumented routes). 7 tests pass, Playwright verified dark mode. Build + lint clean.

notes:

(2) Needs a proper icon on the bar. (3) b-form-select native dropdown — should use FilterDropdown or b-dropdown for viewport-aware menu.

title: Migrate comment threads to b-media — proper Bootstrap comment/reply layout
description:
Title: Migrate comment threads to b-media — proper Bootstrap comment/reply layout

Description:
CommentThread.vue and all consumers use ad-hoc div/flexbox layouts for comment
display. Bootstrap-Vue's b-media component provides the canonical media object
pattern (avatar left, content right) used by GitHub, Discourse, and every major
comment UI. This migration standardizes the DOM structure across all 8 consumers,
enables b-avatar integration, and reduces custom CSS.
Design doc: docs/development/design-system.md §Bootstrap-Vue components to adopt

Files:
- Modify: app/javascript/components/shared/CommentThread.vue (replace div layout with b-media)
- Modify: app/javascript/components/shared/CommentAuthorLine.vue (slot into b-media aside)
- Modify: app/javascript/components/triage/TriageSplitView.vue (verify no regression)
- Modify: app/javascript/components/components/CommentTriageModal.vue (verify no regression)
- Modify: app/javascript/components/components/ComponentComments.vue (verify no regression)
- Modify: app/javascript/components/components/CommentsByRule.vue (verify no regression)
- Modify: app/javascript/components/users/UserComments.vue (verify no regression)
- Modify: app/javascript/components/rules/RuleReviews.vue (verify no regression)
- Test: spec/javascript/components/shared/CommentThread.spec.js

First failing test:
"renders b-media wrapper around comment content" — mount CommentThread, expect b-media-body to exist

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] CommentThread uses b-media with b-media-aside (left) and b-media-body (right)
- [ ] Replies nest as b-media inside parent b-media-body (native b-media nesting)
- [ ] All 8 consumers render correctly (no layout regression)
- [ ] Dark mode renders correctly (design system variables, no raw Bootstrap vars)
- [ ] Reply indentation maintained via b-media nesting (not manual padding)
- [ ] Design system compliance verified (--vulcan-* variables, PanelLayout for layouts, BvConfig defaults)
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
yarn test:unit -- --reporter verbose CommentThread && yarn build && bundle exec rspec spec/

Decision points:
- If b-media doesn't support the current reply threading depth, stop and assess
- If RuleReviews.vue uses CommentThread differently than triage consumers, clarify shared API first

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT add custom CSS for indent/nesting — use b-media's native nesting
- Do NOT change the data shape or props — only the template structure
- Do NOT use raw Bootstrap vars (--primary etc.) — use --vulcan-* design system

NOT in scope:
- b-avatar integration (separate card v2-6gq.3)
- Comment merge UI (separate card v2-05f.12)
- Reply triage-status background bug (separate card v2-05f.46, depends on this)

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC checkbox — verify with evidence
- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated
- [ ] Run the exact Verification command — paste output
- [ ] git diff shows ONLY files listed in Files section

Story points: sp:5
Estimate: 30 min
close_reason: Done. Estimated ~30 min, actual ~20 min. CommentThread replies now use b-media (aside placeholder + body). Avatar placeholder styled with design system vars. Fixed pre-existing ResponseTemplateDropdown test failures (stale onChange→onSelect after b-dropdown rewrite). 17 CommentThread tests + 5 ResponseTemplateDropdown tests pass. 2942 total Vue tests pass. Build clean.

title: Fix BulkTriageBar overlap with classification banner — fixed-bottom pattern
description: BulkTriageBar uses sticky bottom:0 which overlaps the fixed classification banner. Fix: switch to fixed-bottom with CSS variable offset for banner height. Standard pattern (GitHub/Jira bulk selection bars).

title: Migrate ControlsPageLayout to PanelLayout — apply panel layout rules app-wide
description:
Title: Migrate ControlsPageLayout to PanelLayout — apply panel layout rules app-wide

Description:
ControlsPageLayout.vue has the SAME three bugs we fixed in TriageSplitView:
(1) Uses .row WITH gutters (15px) + custom padding = conflict
(2) Sidebar bg override in application.scss fights layout bg
(3) No shared padding on the layout body

Migrate to PanelLayout (no-gutters, layout owns bg+padding, components transparent).
Used by ProjectComponent (rule editor) and RulesCodeEditorView — the two most important
pages after triage.

Files:
- Modify: app/javascript/components/rules/ControlsPageLayout.vue (use PanelLayout)
- Modify: app/javascript/application.scss (remove sidebar bg override if needed)
- Test: Playwright rule editor page in both modes

First failing test:
Playwright screenshot — rule editor sidebar + content panel misaligned/no padding

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] ControlsPageLayout uses PanelLayout component internally
- [ ] no-gutters eliminates grid padding conflict
- [ ] Sidebar bg from PanelLayout bgTier (not CSS override)
- [ ] Consistent p-3 padding on panel bodies
- [ ] Rule editor page looks correct in both modes
- [ ] RulesCodeEditorView still works
- [ ] No regressions — all existing tests pass

Verification:
yarn test:unit --run ControlsPageLayout && Playwright rule editor in both modes

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT keep ad-hoc .row + custom CSS alongside PanelLayout
- Do NOT add padding on slot content — PanelLayout body owns it

NOT in scope:
- Other .row usages (RuleEditorHeader, BenchmarkTable, etc. — those are content-flow rows, not panel layouts)

Story points: sp:2
Estimate: 10 min
close_reason: Done. Estimated ~10 min, actual ~8 min. Migrated ControlsPageLayout from ad-hoc row/col to PanelLayout: no-gutters (eliminates grid/padding conflict), two-panel config (secondary + body bgTier), border auto-placed. Removed old scoped CSS (sidebar-border-right, left-sidebar-column). Updated 2 tests to verify PanelLayout props instead of CSS classes. 18 tests pass, build + lint clean. Playwright verified rule editor in dark mode.

title: Fix form-group vertical spacing — bottom margin clipping across all forms
description:
Title: Fix form-group vertical spacing — leverage b-form-group built-ins across all forms

Description:
Form fields clip at the bottom (IA Control label cut off). Research found Bootstrap-Vue's
b-form-group has built-in layout features we're underusing: content-cols-* for responsive
content widths, label-size for consistent label sizing, description slot for help text,
and automatic margin-bottom: 1rem from .form-group class.

Root cause: something is overriding Bootstrap's default .form-group margin-bottom: 1rem,
or the container is clipping overflow. Need to identify and fix the override globally.

Also audit ALL form pages for: (1) manual .row > .col > strong patterns that should be
b-form-group label-cols, (2) inconsistent spacing between form sections, (3) missing
.form-row on multi-column form fields.

Research basis: b-form-group generates responsive props for every breakpoint. content-cols-*
complements label-cols-* for explicit content width control.

Files:
- Modify: app/javascript/application.scss (fix any .form-group margin override)
- Modify: app/javascript/components/rules/forms/RuleForm.vue (verify .form-row usage)
- Modify: Any other form components found during audit
- Test: Playwright screenshots of rule editor, component settings, profile page

First failing test:
Playwright screenshot — IA Control label clipped at bottom of form group

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Root cause identified: what overrides .form-group margin-bottom?
- [ ] Fix applied globally (not per-component)
- [ ] All form-group elements have consistent bottom margin (Bootstrap default 1rem)
- [ ] No form labels clipped by container overflow
- [ ] Multi-column form fields use .form-row (not .row)
- [ ] Manual label:value layouts migrated to b-form-group label-cols where appropriate
- [ ] Verified on: rule editor, component settings, profile page, modals
- [ ] Playwright screenshots as evidence

Verification:
Playwright screenshots of rule editor + component settings + profile in both modes

Decision points:
- If .form-group margin is being overridden by a parent container's overflow, fix the container not the margin

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT fix per-component — fix the global pattern
- Do NOT add arbitrary px margins — use Bootstrap spacing scale (0.25rem increments)
- Do NOT use .row for form fields — use .form-row (5px gutters vs 15px)

NOT in scope:
- Form field redesign or new form features
- Horizontal spacing changes (covered by .form-row migration already done)

Before closing:
- [ ] Playwright screenshots in BOTH modes for all form pages
- [ ] grep -r 'class="row"' in components/rules/forms/ returns 0 (all migrated to form-row)

Estimate: 15 min

Fix: removed margin-bottom from .rule-form-field — let .form-group own vertical spacing. Added TDD guard test to prevent re-introduction. DOM-verified: all form-group elements now 16px margin-bottom. 5 design system tests pass, build + lint clean.

title: Fix diff viewer layout — footer overlap + filter sidebar boundary
description:
Title: Fix diff viewer layout — footer overlap + filter sidebar boundary

Description:
The diff viewer (used in rule history) has layout issues: footer overlaps content and the
filter sidebar has no visible boundary in dark mode. Research shows this is a flex layout
issue — the diff viewer likely needs the same flex-column + flex-grow-1 + min-height-0
pattern used in PanelLayout.

- Modify: app/javascript/components/rules/RuleHistories.vue (or relevant diff component)

- [ ] Footer stays at bottom of container (not overlapping content)

- [ ] Diff viewer backgrounds use design system variables
- [ ] Added/removed highlights visible in dark mode (--vulcan-highlight-added/removed)

Playwright diff viewer page in both modes — scroll content, verify footer doesn't overlap

title: Fix sidebar active item highlight + panel dividers in dark mode
description:
Title: Fix sidebar active item highlight + panel dividers in dark mode

Description:
Shared pattern across SRG detail, STIG detail, triage split-pane, and rule editor sidebar.
Active item highlight is barely visible in dark mode. Panel dividers invisible. Research found
the root cause: --vulcan-hover-bg is referenced in TriageRuleSidebar and TriageQueueNav but
NEVER DEFINED (fixed by .10.8). This card applies the fixed variables + ensures all 4 sidebar
pages use the same active/hover state pattern.

Research basis: Bootstrap 5.3 uses --bs-tertiary-bg for hover states on list items.
Bootstrap 4 equivalent: our --vulcan-hover-bg (rgba of gray with 8-12% opacity).

title: Fix triage split-pane panel boundaries + bottom bar in dark mode
description:
Title: Refactor TriageSplitView to use PanelLayout — fix spacing + alignment

Description:
TriageSplitView builds a 3-panel layout ad-hoc with b-row + custom CSS. Research found the
root cause of the spacing/alignment issues: b-row has 15px gutters that conflict with
.triage-panel's own padding. The fix is to use the new PanelLayout.vue component (.10.9)
which uses no-gutters and lets panels own all their padding.

This card migrates TriageSplitView from ad-hoc layout to PanelLayout, fixing:
1. Left sidebar interior padding (currently eaten by grid/padding conflict)
2. Panel top alignment (flex align-items-start vs stretch interaction)
3. Consistent three-tier bg (sidebar=secondary, content=body, form=tertiary)

Research basis: Bootstrap 4 no-gutters eliminates grid padding; panels own their spacing.
Bootstrap 5.3 three-tier bg: body (#212529), secondary (#343a40), tertiary (#2b3035).

Files:
- Modify: app/javascript/components/triage/TriageSplitView.vue (migrate to PanelLayout)
- Modify: app/javascript/components/triage/TriageRuleSidebar.vue (remove internal padding hacks if any)
- Test: Playwright screenshots in both modes

First failing test:
Playwright screenshot — left sidebar items pressed against left edge (no visible padding)

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] TriageSplitView uses PanelLayout component (no ad-hoc b-row layout)
- [ ] Left sidebar has visible interior padding on all sides (px-3 equivalent = 1rem)
- [ ] All three panel headings align at the same vertical position
- [ ] Sidebar: --vulcan-secondary-bg + right border
- [ ] Content: --vulcan-body-bg (inherited)
- [ ] Form: --vulcan-tertiary-bg + left border
- [ ] Scrollable panels with overflow-auto + min-height-0
- [ ] BulkTriageBar sticky footer still works
- [ ] No layout shifts or regressions
- [ ] Playwright screenshots as evidence

Verification:
yarn build && Playwright triage page in both modes — all 3 panels properly spaced and colored

Decision points:
- If PanelLayout doesn't support sticky footer, add footer slot before migrating

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT keep ad-hoc layout alongside PanelLayout — full migration
- Do NOT hardcode heights — use calc(100vh - X) or flex-grow-1 from PanelLayout
- Do NOT add padding hacks on child components — padding comes from PanelLayout

NOT in scope:
- ControlsPageLayout migration (separate card)
- Mobile responsive layout
- Triage form content changes

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC — verify with Playwright evidence
- [ ] Playwright screenshots in BOTH modes
- [ ] Side-by-side comparison with current layout — no regressions

Story points: sp:3
Estimate: 15 min
notes:
[2026-06-02] Aaron screenshot confirms: sidebar needs left padding (content against edge), panel divider missing, comment count badges need vertical space.
[2026-06-02] Shared .triage-panel base class created with padding + three-tier bg + borders. Center and right panels have padding now. REMAINING: (1) left sidebar still no interior padding — TriageRuleSidebar content fills edge-to-edge. (2) Top alignment: center and right panel headings don't start at same vertical position. (3) Need to research Bootstrap b-row flexbox alignment-items for consistent panel tops. DO NOT hack — research Bootstrap grid alignment docs + look at how VS Code / GitHub panels handle this.
close_reason: Done. Estimated ~15 min, actual ~30 min. Migrated TriageSplitView to PanelLayout. Three root causes fixed: (1) no-gutters eliminates grid/custom padding conflict, (2) removed dark mode bg overrides on child components that fought PanelLayout bgTier, (3) moved padding from slot content to PanelLayout body div for consistency. DOM-verified: all 3 panels at bodyTop=525, padding=16px, firstContent within 1px. 62 tests pass, lint clean, build clean. Lessons saved to beads memory + ControlsPageLayout migration carded as .10.11.

title: Full visual UX audit — dark mode + spacing + responsiveness via Playwright page-by-page
description:
Title: Full visual UX audit — dark mode + spacing + responsiveness via Playwright page-by-page

v2-fad.9 (table responsiveness) into one thorough visual review. Every user-facing
page gets a Playwright screenshot in BOTH light and dark mode, with specific checks
for spacing consistency, dark mode color correctness, and table/layout responsiveness.

The grep-based CSS variable replacement (session 21) fixed the code patterns but did
NOT verify the visual result. This card is the visual verification + fix pass.

Pages to audit (18 total):
1. / (projects index)
2. /projects/:id (project show + components)
3. /projects/:id/triage (project triage)
4. /components/:id/edit (rule editor)

15. /users/edit/activity (activity log)

17. /users/sign_in (login)
18. /api/docs (Scalar)

Reference: Bootstrap 4 spacing system (0.25rem increments: p-1=0.25rem, p-2=0.5rem, etc.)
