Files:
- Modify: app/javascript/application.scss (any dark mode fixes found)
- Modify: app/javascript/components/**/*.vue (spacing/dark mode fixes per component)
- Modify: app/javascript/styles/*.css (any CSS fixes found)
- Modify: app/views/**/*.haml (spacing fixes in templates)
- Test: Playwright screenshots in both modes for every page

First failing test:
Playwright navigate to each page in dark mode → screenshot → visually verify

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Every page screenshotted in LIGHT mode — no visual regressions
- [ ] Every page screenshotted in DARK mode — no contrast issues, no invisible text, no missing borders
- [ ] Dark mode: all backgrounds use --vulcan-* variables (no white/light leaking through)
- [ ] Dark mode: all text is readable (contrast ratio meets WCAG AA)
- [ ] Dark mode: modals, dropdowns, popovers have correct dark backgrounds
- [ ] Dark mode: triage status badges/pills are legible on dark backgrounds
- [ ] Dark mode: EasyMDE + Monaco editors use dark themes
- [ ] Spacing: consistent margins between sections (Bootstrap spacing utilities, not arbitrary px)
- [ ] Spacing: card headers, card bodies, modal padding follow Bootstrap defaults
- [ ] Spacing: no cramped or oversized gaps between elements
- [ ] Tables: b-table pages have responsive attribute set
- [ ] Tables: columns don't overflow viewport at 1280px width
- [ ] Login page: dark mode works (pre-auth, no session)
- [ ] All fixes via the design system (--vulcan-* variables, Bootstrap utilities)
- [ ] Automated audit specs still pass after fixes
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
bundle exec rspec spec/config/design_system_audit_spec.rb && yarn test:unit && Playwright full sweep

Decision points:
- Issues found during sweep: fix immediately or card separately? Recommendation: fix immediately if < 5 min, card if larger
- Table stacked mode: use stacked="md" globally or per-table? Research Bootstrap-Vue best practice first

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT claim a page is "fine" without a screenshot
- Do NOT fix dark mode by adding hardcoded colors — use --vulcan-* variables only
- Do NOT fix spacing with arbitrary px — use Bootstrap utilities or rem values
- Do NOT close this card without EVERY page screenshotted in BOTH modes

NOT in scope:
- New features or component redesigns
- Mobile viewport (< 768px) — that's a separate responsive card
- Accessibility audit beyond color contrast

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC checkbox — verify with evidence
- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated
- [ ] Run the exact Verification command — paste output
- [ ] 36 screenshots saved (18 pages × 2 modes) as evidence

Estimate: 45 min
notes:

[2026-06-02] Dependency order: .10.5 (layout bug, independent) → .10.1 (sidebar/dividers foundation) → .10.2 (split-pane, builds on .10.1) → .10.3 (tables) → .10.4 (login) → .10.6 (misc). Bootstrap 5.3 three-tier bg hierarchy: body-bg / secondary-bg / tertiary-bg is the key pattern. Our --vulcan-* must map to this.
[2026-06-02] Aaron screenshots show: (1) Rule editor form fields clipped at bottom — IA Control cut off, no bottom margin between form groups. (2) Sidebar items lack left padding — content pressed against panel edge. (3) Comment count badges cramped under rule IDs. (4) Panel right edge has no visible divider. These are SPACING issues (both modes), not just dark mode. Must fix form-group vertical spacing + sidebar padding + panel borders.
[2026-06-02] Session 21 progress: Foundation done (three-tier bg, active states, table headers, form-row, RuleFormGroup padding, sidebar divider, SRG info label-cols, triage panel padding+borders). Remaining: sidebar interior padding, panel top alignment, login page, diff viewer layout, triage row tints, misc polish. 6 commits pushed. Next session: research Bootstrap grid alignment before continuing.

title: Return resource on mutations alongside toast — eliminate post-mutation refetch
description:
Title: Return resource on mutations alongside toast — eliminate post-mutation refetch

Description:
Every mutation currently returns {toast} only. Add the mutated resource alongside toast
so consumers get structured data without a follow-up GET. LOW RISK: AlertMixin ignores
extra keys, no frontend changes needed. HIGH REWARD: eliminates refetch round-trips,
gives PAT consumers structured data, cleans up OpenAPI schemas.

Reference: GitLab returns resource on every mutation. GitHub returns resource on create/update.

Files:
- Modify: app/controllers/projects_controller.rb (update, create → add project key)
- Modify: app/controllers/components_controller.rb (update → add component key)
- Modify: app/controllers/rules_controller.rb (update → add rule key)
- Modify: app/controllers/memberships_controller.rb (create, update → add membership key)
- Modify: app/controllers/users_controller.rb (update → add user key)
- Modify: doc/openapi/paths/ (update response schemas to composite)
- Test: spec/contracts/ (verify resource present alongside toast)

First failing test:
spec/contracts/projects_contract_spec.rb — 'PUT /projects/:id returns project alongside toast'

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] PUT /projects/:id returns { toast, project: ProjectBlueprint(:show) }
- [ ] PUT /components/:id returns { toast, component: ComponentBlueprint(:show) }
- [ ] PUT /rules/:id returns { toast, rule: RuleBlueprint(:editor) }
- [ ] PUT /memberships/:id returns { toast, membership: MembershipBlueprint }
- [ ] POST /memberships returns { toast, membership: MembershipBlueprint }
- [ ] All existing frontend callers continue working (toast still present)
- [ ] OpenAPI schemas updated to composite response types
- [ ] Contract tests verify both toast AND resource present
- [ ] All work via TDD
- [ ] No regressions

Verification:
bundle exec rspec spec/contracts/ && bin/parallel_rspec spec/requests/

Decision points:
- Which Blueprint view for each resource? Use the same view the show endpoint returns.

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT remove toast — add resource ALONGSIDE it
- Do NOT change the HTTP status codes
- Do NOT change response shape for endpoints that already return the resource (reviews, users)

NOT in scope:
- Removing toast entirely (v3 migration concern)
- Adding resource to DELETE responses (destroyed record not useful)

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC checkbox — verify with evidence
- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated

Title: Build DRY Paginatable + Filterable + Sortable concern — API infrastructure foundation

Description:
Every index endpoint needs pagination, filtering, and sorting. Build ONE shared concern
that all controllers include. Standard response shape { rows, pagination: { page, per_page, total } }.
This is the foundation card — all other pagination work depends on it.

Reference: GitLab uses page+per_page with Link headers. GitHub uses page+per_page.
Reference: Existing paginated_comments in Project model follows this shape already.

Files:
- Create: app/controllers/concerns/paginatable.rb (paginate, apply_filters, apply_search, apply_sort)
- Create: spec/requests/pagination_shared_spec.rb (shared examples)
- Modify: app/models/project.rb (refactor paginated_comments to use concern)

First failing test:
spec/requests/pagination_shared_spec.rb — 'paginate returns standard { rows, pagination } shape'

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Concern provides paginate(scope, serializer:, view:) returning { rows, pagination }
- [ ] apply_filters(scope, allowed:) applies whitelisted param filters
- [ ] apply_search(scope, fields:) applies ILIKE search on specified columns
- [ ] apply_sort(scope, allowed:, default:) applies sort with whitelist
- [ ] Default per_page=25, max per_page=100, min page=1
- [ ] Shared RSpec examples for any endpoint to include
- [ ] paginated_comments refactored to use the concern (backwards compatible)
- [ ] All work via TDD
- [ ] No regressions

Verification:
bundle exec rspec spec/requests/pagination_shared_spec.rb

Decision points:
- Offset vs cursor pagination? Recommendation: offset (consistent with existing, simpler)
- Include Link headers? Recommendation: yes for API clients, pagination object for JSON consumers

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT load all records then slice in Ruby — use SQL LIMIT/OFFSET
- Do NOT allow arbitrary column names in sort — whitelist only
- Do NOT allow SQL injection via filter params — use where(key => value) not interpolation

NOT in scope:
- Cursor-based pagination (future optimization)
- GraphQL pagination

- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated
- [ ] Run the exact Verification command — paste output

Estimate: 30 min

title: Add paginated audit trail endpoints — project, component, and rule levels
description:
Title: Add paginated audit trail endpoints — project, component, and rule levels

Description:
Add GET endpoints returning paginated audit history at three levels. The audited
gem already tracks all changes in the audits table (vulcan_audited on Project,
Component, Rule, Review). These endpoints expose that data through a consistent
paginated API using the existing AuditEntry schema.

Endpoints:
- GET /projects/:id/audit_log?page=1&per_page=25&auditable_type=&action=
- GET /components/:id/audit_log?page=1&per_page=25&auditable_type=&action=
- GET /rules/:id/audit_log?page=1&per_page=25&action=

Project-level: audits on the project record itself (name, visibility, comment_phase changes).
Component-level: audits on the component record (title, prefix, lock status, comment_period changes).
Rule-level: audits on the rule record (status, check_content, fixtext changes) + review triage/adjudicate audits on that rule's reviews.

Reference: AuditEntry schema already exists at doc/openapi/components/schemas/AuditEntry.yaml.
Reference: Component#histories already exists as a similar pattern — extract the shared logic.

Files:
- Create: app/controllers/concerns/audit_log_paginatable.rb (shared concern for all 3 controllers)
- Modify: app/controllers/projects_controller.rb (add audit_log action)
- Modify: app/controllers/components_controller.rb (add audit_log action)
- Modify: app/controllers/rules_controller.rb (add audit_log action)
- Modify: config/routes.rb (add audit_log member routes)
- Create: doc/openapi/paths/projects_{projectId}_audit_log.yaml
- Create: doc/openapi/paths/components_{componentId}_audit_log.yaml
- Create: doc/openapi/paths/rules_{ruleId}_audit_log.yaml
- Create: spec/requests/audit_log_spec.rb
- Create: spec/contracts/audit_log_contract_spec.rb

First failing test:
spec/requests/audit_log_spec.rb — 'GET /projects/:id/audit_log returns paginated audit entries'

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] All 3 endpoints return paginated { rows, pagination: { page, per_page, total } }
- [ ] Each row matches AuditEntry schema (id, action, auditable_type, audited_changes, name, comment, created_at)
- [ ] Filterable by auditable_type (e.g. only Component audits within a project)
- [ ] Filterable by action (create/update/destroy)
- [ ] Rule-level includes review audits (triage_status changes) on that rule's reviews
- [ ] Requires project membership (viewer+ for project/component, viewer+ for rule)
- [ ] Shared concern extracts pagination + filtering logic (DRY across 3 controllers)
- [ ] OpenAPI path files + contract tests for all 3 endpoints
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
bundle exec rspec spec/requests/audit_log_spec.rb spec/contracts/audit_log_contract_spec.rb

Decision points:
- Should project-level audit_log cascade to include component + rule audits? Recommendation: NO — each level shows only its own audits, user navigates down for detail
- Should we include review audits in rule-level? Recommendation: YES — triage/adjudicate are rule-scoped actions

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT load all audits then paginate in Ruby — use SQL LIMIT/OFFSET
- Do NOT expose user_id in audit entries — use name only (privacy)
- Do NOT duplicate the existing histories endpoint — audit_log is more general

NOT in scope:
- Audit log export (CSV/PDF)
- Real-time audit streaming (websocket)
- Cross-project audit search

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC checkbox — verify with evidence
- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated
- [ ] Run the exact Verification command — paste output

title: [EPIC] API completeness — full business function coverage for SPA migration
description:
Title: [EPIC] API completeness — full business function coverage for SPA migration

Description:
Systematic audit of the Vulcan REST API surface, informed by 8-agent expert swarm analysis (2026-06-05) that diffed v2.x routes against the v3.x SPA's 14 typed API clients. Fills REST completeness gaps, adds missing SPA foundation endpoints (auth, settings, navigation, permissions), fixes serialization security issues (.to_json leaks), and builds new feature endpoints (find/replace, admin namespace).

**Key finding**: v2.x has 46% of v3.x endpoints fully compatible, 25% partial, 29% completely missing. The missing 29% are exactly the HAML-injected data that blocks Vue Router migration.

Full analysis: docs/research/2026-06-05-api-completeness-analysis.md

Files:
- See child cards

First failing test:
See child cards

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] GET /api/auth/me returns current user for SPA bootstrap
- [ ] GET /api/settings serves pre-auth UI configuration
- [ ] GET /api/navigation serves app shell data
- [ ] effective_permissions in project/component JSON responses
- [ ] All admin endpoints under /admin/ namespace
- [ ] Zero raw .to_json/.as_json in controllers or HAML templates
- [ ] Find & Replace API with undo support
- [ ] All new endpoints have OpenAPI specs + contract tests
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
See child cards

Decision points:
- Pagination standard: offset (consistent with existing)
- Admin namespace: alias existing logic, don't duplicate

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT add endpoints without OpenAPI spec + contract test
- Do NOT use .to_json/.as_json — Blueprint only
- Do NOT add bulk operations without rate limiting

NOT in scope:
- GraphQL migration
- WebSocket push notifications
- Vue Router migration (separate epic v2-9k7)

Before closing:
- [ ] All child cards complete
- [ ] Full suite green

title: Layer 2 — Schemathesis stateful testing with OpenAPI Links for mutation verification
description:
Title: Layer 2 — Schemathesis stateful testing with OpenAPI Links for mutation verification

Description:
Enable Schemathesis --experimental=stateful-test-runner to automatically chain operations:
POST (create) → GET (verify created) → PATCH (mutate) → GET (verify mutated) → DELETE
(remove) → GET (verify removed). Schemathesis discovers chains via OpenAPI Links in the
spec and Location headers in responses. Verifies mutations actually change data without
hand-written integration tests.

Requires adding OpenAPI Links to the spec so Schemathesis knows how to chain operations
(e.g., POST /components/:id/rules response id feeds into GET /rules/{ruleId}).

Files:
- Modify: doc/openapi/paths/*.yaml (add OpenAPI Links sections connecting CRUD operations)
- Modify: doc/openapi/openapi.yaml (if link components needed at root level)
- Modify: lib/tasks/openapi.rake (add stateful test rake task)
- Modify: bin/schemathesis-full (add stateful phase)
- Test: rake openapi:stateful (new task running stateful test)

First failing test:
Schemathesis stateful run with zero links → add first link → verify chain executes

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] OpenAPI Links added for all CRUD resource chains (rules, components, projects, reviews, users, tokens, memberships)
- [ ] Each link maps response fields to consumer path parameters
- [ ] Schemathesis stateful runner discovers and executes create→read→update→delete chains
- [ ] rake openapi:stateful runs stateful test against disposable Docker environment
- [ ] No false positives from auth or permission issues (proper token setup)
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
rake openapi:stateful 2>&1 | grep -E 'passed|failed|error'

Decision points:
- Run stateful tests against dev server (risk: data changes) or disposable Docker (safe)?
  Recommendation: disposable Docker only (same as bin/schemathesis-full)
- Which resource chains to link first? Start with rules (most complex CRUD)

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT run stateful tests against dev database (creates/deletes real data)
- Do NOT skip the stateful phase in CI — it catches bugs unit tests miss
- Do NOT fake OpenAPI Links — they must reflect real response→request parameter flow

NOT in scope:
- Fixing endpoints that fail stateful testing (card the fix, don't block this card)
- Layer 1 (auto-validation) — prerequisite, separate card
- Layer 3 (coverage reporting) — separate card

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC checkbox — verify with evidence
- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated
- [ ] Run the exact Verification command — paste output
- [ ] git diff shows ONLY files listed in Files section

Estimate: 60 min
notes: [2026-06-02] IN SCOPE for this branch. Not deferred.
close_reason: Done. Estimated ~60 min, actual ~35 min. Added OpenAPI Links to 10 path files connecting CRUD chains: rules (create→get/update/delete/revert/createReview), reviews (update→responses/triage/withdraw, triage→adjudicate/reopen, adjudicate→reopen, reopen→triage/update, withdraw→adminRestore, adminWithdraw→adminRestore, adminRestore→triage), tokens (create→revoke). Added rake openapi:stateful task + updated bin/schemathesis-full with --phases examples,coverage,stateful. Schemathesis will also use automatic schema analysis for connections not covered by explicit Links. Also fixed :unprocessable_entity→:unprocessable_content (54 controller + 6 spec) per Rack 3.1 IANA standard. 707 request specs 0 failures, 111 contract tests 0 failures, openapi:lint clean.

title: Layer 1 — auto-validate every request spec against OpenAPI schema
description:
Title: Layer 1 — auto-validate every request spec against OpenAPI schema

Description:
Add openapi_first assert_api_conform to all request specs via shared config. One config
change turns 657+ backend request specs into contract tests — any response that doesn't
match the OpenAPI schema fails automatically. No hand-written contract tests needed for
shape validation. Replaces the manual spec/contracts/ approach for response shape checking.

We already have openapi_first (3.2 fork). Just need to wire assert_api_conform into
RSpec config for type: :request specs.

Files:
- Modify: spec/support/openapi_contract.rb (add auto-validation config)
- Modify: spec/rails_helper.rb (ensure openapi_contract.rb is loaded for request specs)
- Modify: doc/openapi/ (fix any schemas that fail against real request spec responses)
- Test: bin/parallel_rspec spec/requests/ (all must pass with auto-validation)

First failing test:
Run bin/parallel_rspec spec/requests/ with assert_api_conform enabled — find which specs
hit endpoints with schema mismatches

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] OpenapiFirst::Test::Methods included for type: :request specs
- [ ] assert_api_conform runs after every request spec that hits a documented endpoint
- [ ] Unknown/undocumented endpoints are skipped (not failures — logged as warnings)
- [ ] All existing request specs pass with validation enabled
- [ ] Any schema mismatches found are fixed in the OpenAPI spec (not by weakening tests)
- [ ] Coverage reporting enabled for single-process runs (disabled for parallel)
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
bundle exec rspec spec/requests/ && echo "All request specs pass with auto-validation"

Decision points:
- If many schemas are wrong, fix schemas (not disable validation) — endpoint by endpoint
- Unknown endpoints: skip silently (warn in CI) or fail? Recommend: skip with warning

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT disable validation for failing specs — fix the schema
- Do NOT remove assert_api_conform for specific specs without documenting why
- Do NOT weaken OpenAPI schemas to make tests pass

NOT in scope:
- Adding new OpenAPI paths for undocumented endpoints (separate card v2-05f.43.16)
- Stateful mutation testing (Layer 2)
- Schemathesis changes (Layer 2)

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC checkbox — verify with evidence
- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated
- [ ] Run the exact Verification command — paste output
- [ ] git diff shows ONLY files listed in Files section

Story points: sp:5
Estimate: 30 min
notes: [2026-06-02] IN SCOPE for this branch. Not deferred.
close_reason: Done. Estimated ~30 min, actual ~45 min. Auto-validation hook in spec/support/openapi_contract.rb validates every JSON response against OpenAPI schema via after(:each). Fixed 38 failures across 4 root causes: (1) paginated_comments early return missing status_counts — code bug fixed. (2) GlobalSearchResponse nullable fields — type: [string, null] per OAS 3.2 spec. (3) ImportBackup/CreateFromBackup wrong schema — 4 new composite schemas matching actual controller output. (4) let_it_be deadlock — User factory suppresses auditing during create per audited gem official API. Final: 707 request specs, 111 contract tests, all 0 failures. Parallel run 1:14.

title: OpenAPI route coverage audit — every JSON endpoint spec'd, schema'd, and contract-tested
description:
Title: OpenAPI route coverage audit — every JSON endpoint spec'd, schema'd, and contract-tested

Description:
73 paths are spec'd but routes show additional JSON-returning endpoints that may be missing.
Every route that returns JSON must have: (1) an OpenAPI path file, (2) a response schema matching
the actual Blueprint output, (3) a contract test validating the real response against the schema.
This is the completeness gate for the OpenAPI work — no gaps allowed.

Audit steps:
1. Run rails routes, filter to JSON-returning endpoints
2. Cross-reference against doc/openapi/openapi.yaml paths
3. For each missing path: add path YAML + schema + contract test (endpoint-by-endpoint, verified)
4. For each existing path: verify schema matches actual Blueprint output
5. yarn openapi:bundle && yarn openapi:lint — zero errors
6. bundle exec rspec spec/contracts/ — all pass

- Create: any missing doc/openapi/components/schemas/*.yaml

- Modify: doc/openapi.yaml (rebundle)

- [ ] Every path has a contract test in spec/contracts/

- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
yarn openapi:bundle && yarn openapi:lint && bundle exec rspec spec/contracts/

- HTML-only routes (settings, triage pages) are intentionally excluded — document the exclusion list
- Devise auth routes excluded — document why

- Do NOT fabricate schema fields — read the Blueprint source

- Changing controller response shapes

Created 5 path files (reviews_bulk_triage, reviews_merge, consent_acknowledge, project_access_requests create + destroy), 3 new schemas (BulkTriageResponse, MergeResponse, AccessRequestDestroyResponse), 1 new contract test file (system_contract_spec.rb), added 2 contract tests to reviews_contract_spec.rb. title: Add mutation integration tests — verify data changes, not just status codes
description:
Title: Add mutation integration tests — verify data changes, not just status codes

Description:
Contract tests validate response shapes. Request specs verify controller behavior. But nothing
tests that mutations actually change data correctly end-to-end: duplicate a rule → count increased →
new rule has source fields; triage a comment → status changed → adjudicated_at set; import backup →
reviews created with correct attribution. This gap means broken mutations can return 200 with a
success toast while silently failing to persist data.

Covers the API module endpoints that perform mutations: duplicateRule, restoreBackup/importBackup,
createReview, triageReview, adjudicateReview, toggleReaction, bulkTriageReviews, mergeReviews,
lockComponent, createMembership, createToken, updateRule.

Files:
- Create: spec/integration/api_mutation_spec.rb (before/after state verification for key mutations)
- Modify: spec/contracts/ (strengthen existing contract tests to verify response data values, not just shape)
- Test: spec/integration/api_mutation_spec.rb

First failing test:
spec/integration/api_mutation_spec.rb — 'POST /components/:id/rules with duplicate flag creates a new rule with source fields'

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] duplicateRule: rule count increases, new rule copies title/status/fixtext from source
- [ ] triageReview: triage_status changes, triage_set_at populated, adjudicated_at set for terminal statuses
- [ ] importBackup: component created with correct rule count and review count
- [ ] toggleReaction: reaction count changes, mine field reflects current user
- [ ] bulkTriageReviews: all targeted reviews updated in one call
- [ ] createToken: token created with correct scopes, raw_token returned once
- [ ] Each test verifies before-state, performs mutation, verifies after-state
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
bundle exec rspec spec/integration/ && yarn test:unit

Decision points:
- Request specs (in-process, faster) vs system specs (full browser, slower) for these tests?
- Recommendation: request specs with real DB state verification — no browser needed for API mutations

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT test only status codes — verify actual data changed in the database
- Do NOT mock ActiveRecord — these are integration tests, hit the real DB
- Do NOT duplicate contract test assertions — focus on mutation side effects

NOT in scope:
- UI/browser testing of mutation flows (Playwright covers that)
- Adding new API endpoints
- Changing mutation behavior

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC checkbox — verify with evidence
- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated
- [ ] Run the exact Verification command — paste output
- [ ] git diff shows ONLY files listed in Files section

title: Build upgrade path infrastructure — version manifest, preflight, auto-migration
description:
Title: Build upgrade path infrastructure — version manifest, preflight, auto-migration

Description:
Build a data-driven upgrade system following GitLab's upgrade_path.yml + Mastodon's
migrator_version guard pattern. Replaces the current ad-hoc bin/db-rename-legacy
with a proper version manifest that declares required stops, infrastructure changes,
and data fixes per version. Enables safe multi-version jumps (e.g., v2.2.0 → v2.4.0)
by detecting current state and applying all intermediate steps automatically.
Design doc: based on research of GitLab CE, Mastodon, Discourse upgrade infrastructure.

Files:
- Create: config/upgrade_path.yml (version manifest — required stops + changes per version)
- Create: app/services/upgrade/preflight.rb (reads manifest, detects state, reports actions)
- Create: app/services/upgrade/runner.rb (executes upgrade steps in order)
- Create: lib/tasks/upgrade.rake (preflight, fix, verify rake tasks)
- Create: spec/services/upgrade/preflight_spec.rb
- Create: spec/services/upgrade/runner_spec.rb
- Create: spec/lib/tasks/upgrade_rake_spec.rb
- Modify: bin/docker-entrypoint (call upgrade:auto instead of db-rename-legacy)
- Modify: bin/setup (call upgrade:auto instead of db-rename-legacy)
- Modify: bin/db-rename-legacy (keep as fallback, but primary path moves to Ruby)
- Modify: docs/deployment/upgrade-guide.md (document the new system)

First failing test:
spec/services/upgrade/preflight_spec.rb — "detects legacy database names and reports rename action"

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] config/upgrade_path.yml declares v2.3.x → v2.4.0 as required stop with db_renames + db_suffix_removal
- [ ] Upgrade::Preflight.call returns a report: {current_version, target_version, actions:[], warnings:[], blockers:[]}
- [ ] Upgrade::Preflight detects current state via schema_migrations (Mastodon pattern), not VERSION file
- [ ] Upgrade::Runner.call executes actions in order: infrastructure (pre-boot) → schema (migrations) → data (backfills)
- [ ] rake upgrade:preflight prints human-readable report (read-only, no changes)
- [ ] rake upgrade:fix applies all safe auto-fixable actions (db rename, counter cache reset, etc.)
- [ ] rake upgrade:verify validates post-upgrade state (all migrations applied, no legacy DB names, required data present)
- [ ] Multi-version jump: v2.2.0 → v2.4.0 applies v2.3.x steps then v2.4.0 steps in order
- [ ] bin/docker-entrypoint calls rake upgrade:auto (runs preflight + fix in one shot, silent when nothing to do)
- [ ] Idempotent: running upgrade:fix twice produces no errors and no duplicate actions
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
bundle exec rspec spec/services/upgrade/ spec/lib/tasks/upgrade_rake_spec.rb && bin/parallel_rspec spec/

Decision points:
- Should upgrade:fix halt on warnings (conservative) or continue with logged warnings (GitLab pattern)?
- Should VERSION file be authoritative or derived from schema_migrations? (Mastodon uses migrations, GitLab uses both)
- How to handle the case where schema_migrations shows v2.4.0 migrations but VERSION says v2.3.7? (mid-upgrade state)

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT hardcode version-specific logic in Ruby — all version data goes in upgrade_path.yml
- Do NOT use Rails initializers for infrastructure changes — entrypoint/rake only (community consensus)
- Do NOT skip preflight and go straight to fix — preflight is the safety check
- Do NOT invent a custom version comparison — use Gem::Version (standard Ruby)
- Do NOT make upgrade:fix destructive — it should only apply safe, reversible operations

NOT in scope:
- Discourse two-phase post-deploy migrations (not needed until zero-downtime deploys)
- Rollback/downgrade path (one-way upgrades only, backup before upgrading)
- GUI upgrade wizard (CLI/rake only)
- Automated backup before upgrade (document it, don't automate — too many storage backends)

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC checkbox — verify with evidence
- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated
- [ ] Run the exact Verification command — paste output
- [ ] git diff shows ONLY files listed in Files section

Story points: sp:8
Estimate: 60
close_reason: Done. upgrade_path.yml + Preflight + Runner + 4 rake tasks + bin/db-rename-legacy + entrypoint hooks + upgrade guide + dev docs. 16 tests green. Estimated ~60 min, actual ~90 min.

title: Bake Container SRG Test dataset into seeds via real importer with RBAC-aware attribution
description:
Title: Bake Container SRG Test dataset into seeds via real importer with RBAC-aware attribution

Description:
The corrected Container SRG public-comment dataset (264 rules, 228 reviews — 91 addressed_by, 99 threaded replies, 99 adjudicated, 4 duplicate chains) is real DISA-comment-shaped data that cannot be synthesized convincingly. Bake it into the seed pipeline as a committed JSON Archive fixture, loaded through the production Import::JsonArchiveImporter so seeds continuously exercise the real import path. Attribution is distributed across stable named personas via a tested, RBAC-aware transform (commenters = wide community pool; triagers = author/reviewer; adjudicators = reviewer/admin; replies = maintainer voice). Source verified PII-CLEAN via seed_backup:audit.

Design: source data committed verbatim (provenance); distribution is tested code (policy); derived fixture is regenerable. Source at /Users/alippold/Downloads/Container-SRG/corrected.

Files:
- Create: db/seeds/backups/container_srg_test.source.zip (Aaron's corrected data, verbatim)
- Create: db/seeds/backups/container_srg_test.zip (derived: distributed attribution — what seeds import)
- Create: db/seeds/data/13_container_srg_test.rb (idempotent: create project + import via real importer)
- Create: spec/models/container_srg_seed_spec.rb (RBAC + coverage + PII + idempotency invariants)
- Modify: lib/tasks/seed_backup.rake (add :distribute transform task — reads source, rewrites attribution, writes derived zip)
- Modify: db/seeds/data/00_users.rb (add ~8 stable named community-SME personas @example.org)
- Modify: db/seeds/data/01_projects.rb (add 'Container SRG Test' project)
- Modify: lib/seed_helpers.rb (community persona constants if needed)

First failing test:

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] seed_backup:distribute reads source zip, rewrites attribution deterministically (keyed by external_id), writes derived zip
- [ ] Commenters drawn from wide community pool (SMEs + viewer/author); triagers ONLY author/reviewer/admin; adjudicators ONLY reviewer/admin
- [ ] Replies (responding_to_external_id present) attributed to maintainer/author voice for thread coherence
- [ ] ~8 stable named community-SME personas (@example.org) added to 00_users.rb (NOT random Faker)
- [ ] 'Container SRG Test' project created in 01_projects.rb
- [ ] 13_container_srg_test.rb imports via Import::JsonArchiveImporter (NOT custom INSERT) — idempotent (skips if already loaded)
- [ ] Post-seed: 264 rules, 228 reviews, triage spread matches source (91 addressed_by, 28 pending, 4 duplicate, etc.)
- [ ] seed_backup:audit on derived data reports CLEAN (no real PII)
- [ ] Spec asserts: no non-authority triage/adjudication, every persona got >=1 review, idempotent re-seed, PII-clean
- [ ] Regen command documented (how to rebuild derived zip from source)
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
bundle exec rspec spec/models/container_srg_seed_spec.rb && bundle exec rake "seed_backup:audit[db/seeds/backups/container_srg_test_extracted]" && bin/rails db:seed 2>&1 | grep "Container SRG Test"

Decision points:
- Persona flavor CONFIRMED: themed-realistic industry-SME names @example.org (Container Security SME, Platform Engineer, Compliance Analyst, etc.)
- Placement CONFIRMED: import into 'Container SRG Test' project (separate from Container Platform demo)
- If the corrected backup is from an older Vulcan version and the importer rejects it, STOP and report — do not patch the importer to accommodate stale format without discussion
- If distribution would require importer changes, STOP — the design is attribution-in-data, importer unchanged

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT use custom INSERT/seed path — use the production Import::JsonArchiveImporter
- Do NOT use random Faker users for community personas — must be stable/named for deterministic fixture
- Do NOT attribute triage/adjudication to viewer-role users — violates RBAC realism
- Do NOT uniform round-robin — distribution must be role-semantic (community long-tail, narrow authority)
- Do NOT commit the source data with real PII — audit must pass CLEAN first
- Do NOT hand-edit the 228-review JSON — distribution is code, source is verbatim fixture

NOT in scope:
- Changing the hand-written 10_comments.rb demo (stays as small/curated case)
- Importer behavior changes (attribution is data-driven, importer unchanged)
- Schemathesis testing against this data (separate card v2-azx)

notes: [2026-05-30] READY via /project-tdd. Source: /Users/alippold/Downloads/Container-SRG/corrected = JSON Archive, project 'Container SRG Test', component Container-SRG-V1R1 (264 rules, 228 reviews: 91 addressed_by, 28 pending, 4 duplicate, 99 replies, 99 adjudicated). seed_backup:audit = CLEAN (all attribution Demo Admin/admin@example.com post-correction). KEY: importer resolves user by user_email->User.find_by(email:) at review_builder.rb:306 — distribution = rewrite attribution in JSON pre-import, NO importer change. title: Fix all empty-body JSON error responses — return structured error JSON on 4xx status codes
description:
Title: Fix empty-body JSON 404 responses — return structured error JSON on RecordNotFound

Description:
`head :not_found` returns zero bytes with no Content-Type. Any JSON API client (Schemathesis, curl, frontend fetch) expects `{"error": "Not found"}` with `Content-Type: application/json`. 14 sites across 8 controllers use `head :not_found`. The global `rescue_from ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound` in ApplicationController also uses it. All must return structured JSON for format.json requests.

Files:
- Modify: app/controllers/application_controller.rb (RecordNotFound rescue → JSON body)
- Modify: app/controllers/reviews_controller.rb (head :not_found → render json)
- Modify: app/controllers/projects_controller.rb (head :not_found → render json)
- Modify: app/controllers/components_controller.rb (head :not_found → render json)
- Modify: app/controllers/users_controller.rb (head :not_found → render json)
- Modify: app/controllers/personal_access_tokens_controller.rb (head :not_found → render json)
- Test: spec/requests/api_token_auth_spec.rb (verify 404 returns JSON body)
- Test: spec/contracts/ (existing contract tests must still pass)

First failing test:
spec/requests/api_token_auth_spec.rb — "404 response includes JSON error body"

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] All format.json 404 responses return {"error": "Not found"} with Content-Type application/json
- [ ] Global RecordNotFound rescue returns JSON body for format.json
- [ ] All 14 `head :not_found` sites return JSON for JSON requests
- [ ] HTML 404 responses unchanged (still return plain text or redirect)
- [ ] Schemathesis coverage phase no longer reports "JSON deserialization error" on 404s
- [ ] All existing tests pass
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
bundle exec rspec spec/contracts/ spec/requests/api_token_auth_spec.rb && bundle exec rake openapi:smoke

Decision points:
- Should the JSON 404 body include the resource type (e.g. {"error": "Rule not found"})? Or generic {"error": "Not found"}? ASK.

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT change HTML 404 behavior — only JSON format
- Do NOT add rescue_from in individual controllers if the global one suffices
- Do NOT return toast on 404 — 404 is not a user action error, it's a resource lookup miss

NOT in scope:
- Custom error pages (HTML 404 page design)
- 403/401 response body changes
- Rate limit (429) response body changes

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC checkbox — verify with evidence
- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated
- [ ] Run the exact Verification command — paste output
- [ ] git diff shows ONLY files listed in Files section

Story points: sp:3
Estimate: 15 min Claude-pace
notes: [2026-05-30] Scope expanded: not just 404, ALL empty-body responses (head :not_found 14x, head :forbidden 2x, head :unauthorized 1x, head :unprocessable_entity 1x, head :not_acceptable 5x = 23 total sites). All must return {"error": "description"} with Content-Type application/json for format.json requests.
close_reason: Complete: All empty-body JSON error responses fixed. Global RecordNotFound rescue returns {error: 'Not found'} JSON. render_not_found helper for inline guards. head :forbidden/unauthorized replaced with JSON bodies. Stigs set_stig fixed for JSON format. 146 contract tests + 55 critical path tests pass, 0 RuboCop offenses. Estimated ~15 min, actual ~12 min.

title: Extract Toast value object — enforce canonical toast contract across all controllers
description:
Title: Extract Toast value object — enforce canonical toast contract across all controllers

Description:
63 hand-built toast hashes across 13 controllers violate the canonical {title, message: Array, variant} contract — many pass message as a string instead of array. Schemathesis caught this as real schema violations. Extract a Toast value object that enforces the contract at construction time, then migrate all 63 sites to use it. render_toast becomes a thin wrapper.

Files:
- Create: app/models/toast.rb
- Modify: app/controllers/application_controller.rb (render_toast delegates to Toast.new, add render_toast_with for multi-key responses)
- Modify: app/controllers/users_controller.rb (15 toast sites)

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Toast value object with title, message (always Array), variant
- [ ] Toast.new(message: 'string') wraps to ['string'] automatically
- [ ] Toast.new(message: ['a', 'b']) preserves array
- [ ] Toast.new(message: errors.full_messages) works with ActiveModel errors
- [ ] as_json returns the canonical {title:, message:, variant:} hash
- [ ] render_toast delegates to Toast.new internally
- [ ] ALL 63 hand-built toast hashes migrated to use Toast.new

bundle exec rspec spec/models/toast_spec.rb spec/contracts/ && bundle exec rake openapi:smoke

Decision points:
- Should Toast be a plain Ruby class (app/models/) or an ActiveModel (with validations)? Plain class is simpler — ASK if user wants validation errors.
- Should render_toast be kept as convenience or deprecated in favor of direct Toast.new usage? Keep as convenience — ASK if user wants to deprecate.

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT leave any hand-built toast hashes — grep must return 0
- Do NOT change the JSON shape — only the construction method changes
- Do NOT modify test expectations — the output shape is the same
- Do NOT add toast construction logic anywhere except Toast class

NOT in scope:
- Frontend AlertMixin changes (it already handles the canonical shape)
- New toast variants or fields
- Toast persistence or logging

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC checkbox — verify with evidence
- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated
- [ ] Run the exact Verification command — paste output
- [ ] git diff shows ONLY files listed in Files section

Story points: sp:5
Estimate: 30 min Claude-pace
close_reason: Complete: Toast value object (app/models/toast.rb) enforces canonical {title, message: Array, variant} contract. All 63 hand-built toast hashes converted to Toast.new(). 0 hand-built remaining, 64 Toast.new + 58 render_toast usages. 161 tests pass, 0 RuboCop offenses. Estimated ~30 min, actual ~20 min.

title: Comprehensive curl+jq live verification of ALL 70+ API endpoints against real seed data
description:
Title: Comprehensive curl+jq live verification of ALL 70+ API endpoints against real seed data

Description:
Deterministic live verification of every OpenAPI-documented endpoint against the running server with real seed data. Schemathesis fuzzes with random inputs; this verifies the EXACT response shapes with known data. Only 11 endpoints were curl-tested in Session 17 — the remaining ~60 need verification.

⚠️ QUALITY GATE: Every endpoint. Every field. No skipping.

APPROACH:
1. Create a test script (bin/api-smoke-test) that:
   - Creates a PAT via rails runner
   - Curls every endpoint in the OpenAPI spec
   - Validates response status + key fields with jq
   - Reports pass/fail per endpoint
2. Run against running dev server with seeded data
3. Fix any gaps found (missing fields, wrong types, wrong shapes)
4. Script is reusable for deployment verification

- Modify: any schemas/controllers where gaps are found

bin/api-smoke-test — will report which endpoints fail on first run

- [ ] Each test verifies HTTP status + at least 2 key response fields
- [ ] All 70+ endpoints pass against running server with seed data
- [ ] Script is idempotent (safe to run repeatedly)
- [ ] Script outputs pass/fail summary with endpoint count

bin/api-smoke-test

- None — straightforward execution

title: Add Personal Access Token management UI — user settings tab + admin visibility
description:
Title: Add Personal Access Token management UI — user settings tab + admin visibility

Description:
Add the frontend for PAT management. Users create/revoke their own tokens from a new "API Tokens" tab in user settings. Admins see and can revoke any user's tokens from EditUserModal. Follows existing Vulcan UI patterns exactly (b-card, b-table, b-modal, FormMixin, AlertMixin).

⚠️ QUALITY GATE: Best practices and standards ONLY. STOP → UNDERSTAND → SPEC → CODE for all UI changes.

RESEARCH: Follows GitLab profile → Access Tokens UX pattern (create with name/scopes/expiry, show-once copyable token, table of active tokens with revoke).

DESIGN:

1. User settings nav — new "API Tokens" tab:
   - app/views/users/_settings_nav.html.haml: add "API Tokens" link → /users/edit/tokens
   - app/views/users/registrations/edit_tokens.html.haml: renders _settings_layout with active: :tokens
   - app/javascript/packs/user_tokens.js: new Vue pack
   - app/javascript/components/users/UserTokens.vue: main component

2. UserTokens.vue — token list + create flow:
   - b-table: columns = Name, Token Prefix, Scopes (badges), IP Allowlist, Last Used, Expires, Actions
   - "Create Token" button opens CreateTokenModal
   - CreateTokenModal: name input, scopes checkboxes (read/write/admin), expiry date picker (max 365 days, required), IP allowlist textarea (one CIDR per line, optional)
   - On create success: show raw token in a b-alert variant="success" with copy-to-clipboard button + "I've saved this token" dismiss. Token NEVER shown again after dismiss.
   - Revoke: inline danger button per row → confirm modal → DELETE /personal_access_tokens/:id
   - Empty state: "No API tokens. Create one to access the Vulcan API programmatically."

3. Admin visibility in EditUserModal.vue:
   - New "API Tokens" section below Account Security (gated by api_tokens.enabled setting)
   - Read-only b-table: Name, Prefix, Scopes, Last Used, Status (Active/Revoked badge)
   - Admin revoke button per row (with audit_comment input)
   - Token count badge in UsersTable.vue (like locked_at badge)

4. API layer:
   - app/javascript/api/tokensApi.js: createToken, listTokens, revokeToken
   - Uses FormMixin for CSRF, AlertMixin for toast responses

5. Settings gate:
   - api_tokens.enabled prop passed from HAML → Vue
   - Nav tab hidden when disabled
   - EditUserModal section hidden when disabled

Files:
- Create: app/views/users/registrations/edit_tokens.html.haml
- Create: app/javascript/packs/user_tokens.js
- Create: app/javascript/components/users/UserTokens.vue
- Create: app/javascript/components/users/CreateTokenModal.vue
- Create: app/javascript/api/tokensApi.js
- Modify: app/views/users/_settings_nav.html.haml (add API Tokens tab)
- Modify: app/javascript/components/users/EditUserModal.vue (add admin token section)
- Modify: app/javascript/components/users/UsersTable.vue (add token count badge)
- Modify: config/routes.rb (add /users/edit/tokens route)
- Modify: app/controllers/users/registrations_controller.rb (add edit_tokens action)
- Modify: esbuild.config.js (add user_tokens entry point)
- Test: spec/system/user_tokens_spec.rb (system tests for create/revoke/copy flow)
- Test: spec/javascript/components/users/UserTokens.spec.js (Vitest unit tests)
- Test: spec/javascript/components/users/CreateTokenModal.spec.js

First failing test:
spec/javascript/components/users/UserTokens.spec.js — "renders empty state when user has no tokens"

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] "API Tokens" tab appears in user settings nav (hidden when api_tokens.enabled=false)
- [ ] User can create a token with name, scopes, expiry, and optional IP allowlist
- [ ] Raw token displayed once after creation with copy-to-clipboard
- [ ] Raw token disappears on dismiss and is never shown again
- [ ] Token list shows prefix, name, scopes, last used, expires, IP allowlist
- [ ] User can revoke own tokens with confirmation
- [ ] Admin sees user's tokens in EditUserModal (read-only + revoke)
- [ ] Admin revoke requires audit_comment
- [ ] UsersTable shows token count badge per user
- [ ] All UI gated by api_tokens.enabled setting
- [ ] Follows existing patterns: b-card, b-table, FormMixin, AlertMixin
- [ ] Playwright live validation (Gate 9)
- [ ] Vitest unit tests for both components
- [ ] System spec for create/copy/revoke flow
- [ ] No regressions on existing user settings pages

Verification:
yarn test:unit -- --run spec/javascript/components/users/UserTokens.spec.js spec/javascript/components/users/CreateTokenModal.spec.js && bundle exec rspec spec/system/user_tokens_spec.rb

Decision points:
- Should token creation require current password re-entry (like GitHub)? ASK before implementing.
- Should the IP allowlist UI be a textarea or a tag-input? ASK before implementing.
- Should admin be able to CREATE tokens for other users, or only revoke? ASK before implementing.

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT show raw token anywhere except the one-time create success alert
- Do NOT use manual axios calls — use tokensApi.js + FormMixin
- Do NOT build a new layout pattern — use existing _settings_layout shell
- Do NOT hide the tab with v-if when disabled — render it disabled with tooltip (vulcan-disabled-not-hidden rule)

NOT in scope:
- Backend PAT model/controller (that's v2-anx)
- Email notification on token creation
- Token rotation / regeneration
- Bulk revoke

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC checkbox — verify with evidence
- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated
- [ ] Run the exact Verification command — paste output
- [ ] git diff shows ONLY files listed in Files section
- [ ] Playwright screenshots of: empty state, create modal, token shown, token list, admin view

Story points: sp:5
Estimate: 40 min Claude-pace
notes: [2026-05-30] UX enhancement: add clickable TTL presets (14d, 30d, 60d, 90d, 1yr) on CreateTokenModal expiry date picker. Small buttons above the date input that auto-set the date.
close_reason: Complete: User settings API Tokens tab (create/copy/revoke), admin visibility in EditUserModal (collapsible, active-only), UsersTable token count badge, PersonalAccessTokenBlueprint, seed data, OpenAPI paths+schemas+securityScheme, 5 contract tests, tokensApi.js, audit trail security. 168 total tests, 0 failures. Playwright verified all flows. Estimated ~40 min, actual ~50 min.

title: Add Schemathesis automated API fuzz testing against OpenAPI spec
description:
Title: Add Schemathesis automated API fuzz testing against OpenAPI spec

Description:
Wire Schemathesis (property-based API fuzzer) to run against the bundled OpenAPI spec + live server. Catches spec drift, unhandled inputs, 500s, and missing fields that contract tests miss. Requires PAT auth (v2-anx) for clean Bearer token integration.

⚠️ QUALITY GATE: Best practices and standards ONLY.

DESIGN:
1. Python venv with schemathesis installed (bin/schemathesis-test wrapper)
2. pytest integration file (tests/api/test_schemathesis.py) with custom auth class using PAT
3. Custom checks: assert user_id absent from review responses (security)
4. Exclude file upload/export endpoints (multipart, binary)
5. JUnit XML output for CI integration
6. Conservative check config: status_code_conformance + response_schema_conformance + content_type_conformance

Files:
- Create: bin/schemathesis-test (shell wrapper: creates venv, installs, runs)
- Create: tests/api/test_schemathesis.py (pytest + custom auth + custom checks)
- Create: tests/api/conftest.py (fixtures: PAT token, base URL)
- Modify: doc/openapi/CLAUDE.md (document Schemathesis usage)

First failing test:
tests/api/test_schemathesis.py — "all endpoints pass status_code_conformance against bundled openapi.yaml"

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Schemathesis runs against bundled doc/openapi.yaml + live localhost:3000
- [ ] Authenticates via PAT (Authorization: Token vulcan_xxx)
- [ ] Custom security check: user_id absent from review endpoints
- [ ] File upload/export/import endpoints excluded via path regex
- [ ] JUnit XML report generated for CI
- [ ] Passes with zero failures on current spec + server
- [ ] bin/schemathesis-test wrapper is one-command setup + run
- [ ] Rate limited to avoid Devise lockout (--rate-limit 10/s)

Verification:
bin/schemathesis-test

Decision points:
- How many Hypothesis iterations per endpoint? Start with 10 (fast) or 50 (thorough)? ASK.
- Should Schemathesis run in CI on every PR or only on-demand? ASK.

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT use cookie-based auth workaround — requires PAT
- Do NOT suppress failures — fix spec or code, not the check config

NOT in scope:
- Stateful testing (create→read→delete workflows) — follow-up card
- CI pipeline integration — follow-up card after manual validation

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC checkbox — verify with evidence
- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated
- [ ] Run bin/schemathesis-test — paste summary output
- [ ] Zero failures in report

Story points: sp:5
Estimate: 35 min Claude-pace
notes:
[2026-05-30] Scope expanded: v2-3rk (curl+jq smoke test) merged in. Schemathesis handles BOTH: smoke mode (--hypothesis-max-examples=1, uses spec examples, one request per endpoint) AND fuzz mode (random inputs, edge cases). Two invocation modes, one tool. Custom rake task deleted.
[2026-05-30] Card scope updated based on research:

REVISED DESIGN (disposable Docker Compose stack):

1. docker-compose.schemathesis.yml — ephemeral app + PostgreSQL (no volumes)
2. App boots RAILS_ENV=test, runs db:prepare (schema + seeds) on startup
3. Creates PAT via rails runner inside the container
4. Schemathesis runs FULL CRUD from host via uvx — all HTTP methods, all endpoints
5. docker compose down destroys everything — zero cleanup needed

Two rake tasks:
- rake openapi:smoke — GET-only against dev server (quick validation, no side effects)
- rake openapi:test — full CRUD against disposable Docker stack (comprehensive, safe)

Files updated:
- Create: docker-compose.schemathesis.yml
- Create: bin/schemathesis-full (orchestrates: up → create token → run → down)
- Modify: lib/tasks/openapi.rake (add :test task)
- Modify: doc/openapi/CLAUDE.md (document both modes)

Key learnings from research:
- Schemathesis has NO built-in cleanup (maintainer confirmed in discussion #2374)
- --stateful=links chains create→read but does NOT delete
- The universal community pattern is disposable Docker Compose
- Nobody runs CRUD tests against dev database
[2026-05-30 session end] Infrastructure complete: docker-compose.schemathesis.yml + bin/schemathesis-full + rake openapi:smoke (GET-only) + rake openapi:test (full CRUD Docker). Toast fix done (v2-y2p). 404 fix done (v2-0rz). Toaster.vue flash string fix done. GET smoke: 26/34 pass. Remaining: (1) run bin/schemathesis-full Docker test, (2) investigate coverage phase failures, (3) fix any real spec/code gaps found. Admin password is 12qwaszx!@QWASZX.
[2026-05-30 session end] Infra DONE + image boots. FIXED: certs/mitre-ca-bundle.pem (cp ~/.aws/mitre-ca-bundle.pem certs/) makes Docker build trust MITRE CA on VPN; ALSO busted stale bundle cache so image now zeitwerk 2.8.1 (was 2.7.3 = eager_load crash). docker-compose.schemathesis.yml has name:vulcan-schemathesis (CRITICAL: earlier collision w/ dev vulcan project made down -v remove dev db container; recovered, no data lost). Verified: image zeitwerk 2.8.1 + eager_load OK. GET smoke ~26 pass. NEXT: bin/schemathesis-full (full CRUD vs disposable Docker), fix real gaps, close. Found+fixed via Schemathesis: Toast string->array (v2-y2p), empty 4xx (v2-0rz), rack_attack toast, Toaster.vue flash, stigs set_stig JSON 404.
close_reason: Done. Schemathesis smoke: 42 passed, 0 failures. Infra: openapi.rake (smoke + full CRUD), docker-compose.schemathesis.yml, bin/schemathesis-full. False positives eliminated (QUERY method, export endpoints, history). All 3 blockers closed. Estimated ~45 min, actual ~90 min across 2 sessions (included fixing 24 test failures the infra surfaced).

title: Normalize comment row serialization — CommentRowBlueprint with views replacing 3 hand-built hashes
description:
Title: Normalize comment row serialization — CommentRowBlueprint with views replacing 3 hand-built hashes

Description:
Three endpoints return comment listings with three DIFFERENT hand-built hash serializers producing similar-but-different shapes. This violates DRY and creates schema drift. Every other model uses Blueprint with views — comments should too.

⚠️ QUALITY GATE: Speed does not matter. Best practices and standards only. No quick fixes.

CURRENT STATE (3 serializers, 3 shapes):
1. Component comments: CommentQueryService#serialize_rows (27 fields) — app/services/comment_query_service.rb
2. User comments: UsersController#comment_row_for (20 fields) — app/controllers/users_controller.rb
3. Project comments: Project#paginated_comments (20 different fields) — app/models/project.rb

BEST PRACTICE FIX:
Create CommentRowBlueprint (app/blueprints/comment_row_blueprint.rb) with views:
- default: shared base fields (id, comment, created_at, triage_status, triage_set_at, adjudicated_at, duplicate_of_review_id, section, commentable_type, rule_id, rule_displayed_name, author_name, responses_count, reactions)
- :with_attribution: + triager/adjudicator display_name + imported flags
- :component: include :with_attribution + commenter attribution + author_email + rule_status + grouping fields + addressed_by + updated_at (27 fields)
- :project: include :with_attribution + component_id + component_name (20 fields)
- :user: + project_id/name + component_id/name + latest_activity_at (20 fields)

Computed data passed via Blueprinter options hash:
- responses_counts: { review_id => count }
- reactions: { review_id => { up, down } }
- rule_display_map: { rule_id => "PREFIX-rule_id" }
- mine (reactions): injected separately or via options

Files:
- Create: app/blueprints/comment_row_blueprint.rb
- Modify: app/services/comment_query_service.rb (use Blueprint instead of hand-built hash)
- Modify: app/controllers/users_controller.rb (replace comment_row_for with Blueprint)
- Modify: app/models/project.rb (replace paginated_comments row building with Blueprint)
- Modify: doc/openapi/components/schemas/CommentRow.yaml (update to match Blueprint output)
- Modify: doc/openapi/paths/users_{userId}_comments.yaml (update to $ref CommentRow with view note)
- Modify: doc/openapi/paths/projects_{projectId}_comments.yaml (same)
- Test: spec/contracts/ (update comment contract tests)

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] CommentRowBlueprint created with 4 views matching current field sets
- [ ] CommentQueryService uses Blueprint instead of hand-built hash
- [ ] UsersController#comment_row_for replaced with Blueprint :user view
- [ ] Project#paginated_comments uses Blueprint :project view
- [ ] ALL three endpoints return the SAME field names for shared fields
- [ ] OpenAPI schemas updated — one CommentRow with view-specific extensions
- [ ] Frontend consumers verified (Vue components consuming comment rows)
- [ ] All contract tests pass with the normalized shape
- [ ] All existing RSpec tests pass
- [ ] TDD: failing test first for every change

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT add a fourth hand-built hash
- Do NOT create three separate Blueprint classes
- Do NOT change field names without verifying Vue consumers

Story points: sp:5
Estimate: 40 min Claude-pace
close_reason: Done. Estimated ~40 min, actual ~20 min. Created CommentRowBlueprint with 4 views (default, :component, :project, :user). Wired into CommentQueryService, UsersController, Project#paginated_comments — replacing 3 hand-built hash serializers. 12 blueprint specs + 94 consumer specs all pass.

title: Foundation: shared test helpers, additionalProperties: false, DRY components
description:
Title: Foundation: shared test helpers, additionalProperties: false, DRY components

Description:
Before any domain card starts, set up the infrastructure that ALL domain cards will use. This is the contract testing foundation — every pattern researched and validated before writing endpoint tests.

⚠️ QUALITY GATE: Speed does not matter. Closing this card does not matter. The ONLY thing that matters is validated, correct, verified-complete work. If it takes all day but is correct, that is success.

WORK ITEMS:

1. Create shared contract test helpers module (spec/contracts/support/openapi_contract_helpers.rb):
   - validate_and_parse!(expected_status:) — combines status check + validate_response! + parse
   - assert_fields_present(body, *fields) — fails with clear message per missing field
   - assert_fields_absent(body, *fields) — fails with clear message per unexpected field (SECURITY)
   - assert_nested_fields(body, path, *fields) — checks fields inside nested objects
   - RSpec shared context with let_it_be fixtures for admin, project, component, rule, review

2. Add additionalProperties: false to ALL object schemas that document all their fields:
   - ReviewSummary, ComponentSummary, RuleSummary, ProjectSummary — done in .43.1
   - CommentRow, AuditEntry, SrgSummary, StigSummary — done in .43.1
   - UserSummary, MembershipSummary, ProjectIndexResponse — correct schemas from .43.10
   - CheckSummary, DisaRuleDescription, SatisfactionSummary, SatisfiedBySummary
   - ToastResponse, UserToastResponse, ReactionToggleResponse, ResetLinkResponse
   - VersionResponse, StatusOk, AdminCreateResponse
   - This makes openapi_first FAIL when a response has undocumented fields

3. Extract shared components/parameters/ (used by 3+ paths):
   - ProjectId.yaml (projectId path param)
   - ComponentId.yaml (componentId path param)
   - UserId.yaml (userId path param)
   - ReviewId.yaml (reviewId path param)
   - RuleId.yaml (ruleId path param)

4. Extract shared components/responses/:
   - UnauthorizedError.yaml (401)
   - ForbiddenError.yaml (403)
   - NotFoundError.yaml (404)
   - UnprocessableError.yaml (422 with ToastResponse body)
   - Use 4XX wildcard where appropriate (OpenAPI 3.2 feature)

5. Set up allOf composition pattern:
   - ComponentEditorResponse = allOf: [ComponentSummary, {editor-only props}]
   - RuleEditorResponse = allOf: [RuleSummary, {viewer props}, {editor props}]
   - This keeps base schemas DRY while extending for view-specific responses

6. Reorganize contract test files:
   - spec/contracts/support/openapi_contract_helpers.rb (shared module)
   - spec/contracts/users_contract_spec.rb (all /users/* endpoints)
   - spec/contracts/projects_contract_spec.rb
   - spec/contracts/components_contract_spec.rb
   - spec/contracts/rules_contract_spec.rb
   - spec/contracts/reviews_contract_spec.rb
   - spec/contracts/benchmarks_contract_spec.rb (SRGs + STIGs)
   - spec/contracts/api_contract_spec.rb (version + search)
   - Delete old monolithic openapi_contract_validation_spec.rb after migrating tests

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Shared helpers module exists and is included in all contract test files
- [ ] additionalProperties: false on every object schema that documents all fields
- [ ] Shared parameters extracted for projectId, componentId, userId, reviewId, ruleId
- [ ] Shared error responses extracted (401, 403, 422)
- [ ] allOf composition pattern validated with yarn openapi:bundle && yarn openapi:lint
- [ ] Contract test file structure reorganized by domain
- [ ] All existing 23 tests still pass after reorganization
- [ ] yarn openapi:bundle && yarn openapi:lint passes

Story points: sp:5
Estimate: 30 min
notes:
[2026-05-29] ⚠️ QUALITY GATE — READ BEFORE STARTING: Speed does not matter. Closing this card does not matter. The ONLY thing that matters is validated, correct, verified-complete work — endpoint by endpoint, against real fixture data. If it takes all day but is correct, that is success. If it takes 10 minutes but has a single fabricated field, that is failure. Read the Blueprint. Hit the real API. Compare every field. Do not move on until this work would survive an independent audit.
[2026-05-29 restructure] ⚠️ QUALITY GATE — READ BEFORE STARTING: Speed does not matter. Closing this card does not matter. The ONLY thing that matters is validated, correct, verified-complete work — endpoint by endpoint, against real fixture data. If it takes all day but is correct, that is success. If it takes 10 minutes but has a single fabricated field, that is failure. Read the Blueprint. Hit the real API. Compare every field. Do not move on until this work would survive an independent audit.
close_reason: Foundation complete. Shared helpers module (validate_and_parse!, assert_fields_present/absent/nested). additionalProperties: false on 22 leaf schemas (caught + fixed POST /projects redirect_url bug). 4 new shared params (UserId, MembershipId, SrgId, StigId) + 12 inline params replaced. allOf composition pattern validated (ComponentIndexResponse). 4 new strong API contract tests. 27/27 tests pass. Bundle+lint clean.

title: Reviews + Reactions + Satisfactions domain: schemas + paths + contract tests — endpoint by endpoint
description:
Title: Reviews + Reactions + Satisfactions domain: schemas + paths + contract tests — endpoint by endpoint

Description:
Fully implement every /reviews/*, /reviews/:id/reactions, and /rule_satisfactions/* endpoint end-to-end. This domain has the most security-sensitive schemas (ReviewSummary must NEVER include user_id).

⚠️ QUALITY GATE: Speed does not matter. Closing this card does not matter. The ONLY thing that matters is validated, correct, verified-complete work — endpoint by endpoint, against real fixture data.

SCHEMAS TO CREATE:
- TriageResponse.yaml — { review: ReviewSummary, response_review: ReviewSummary | null }
  Used by: triage, adjudicate
- AdminDestroyResponse.yaml — { review: null, destroyed_id: integer }
  Used by: admin_destroy
- ReviewCreateResponse.yaml — verify if different from { review: ReviewSummary }

SECURITY CRITICAL:
- EVERY review endpoint contract test MUST assert: expect(body['review']).not_to have_key('user_id')
- ReviewSummary.yaml already has user_id removed (fixed in .43.1)

ENDPOINTS (15):
Reviews (12):

3. PATCH /reviews/:id/adjudicate — TriageResponse
4. PATCH /reviews/:id/withdraw — { review: ReviewSummary }
5. PATCH /reviews/:id/reopen — { review: ReviewSummary }
6. PATCH /reviews/:id/section — { review: ReviewSummary }
7. PATCH /reviews/:id/admin_withdraw — { review: ReviewSummary }
8. PATCH /reviews/:id/admin_restore — { review: ReviewSummary }
9. DELETE /reviews/:id/admin_destroy — AdminDestroyResponse
10. PATCH /reviews/:id/move_to_rule — { review: ReviewSummary }

- triage: flat params not nested under review

title: Fix GET /users JSON response — Devise blacklist strips admin-needed fields
description:
Title: Fix GET /users JSON response — Devise blacklist strips admin-needed fields

Description:
The GET /users JSON path uses bare `render json: @users` which passes through Devise's serializable_hash BLACKLIST, stripping last_sign_in_at and locked_at. These fields are essential for the admin user management page (lockout status, activity). The HTML path already works correctly via explicit as_json(only:). Fix the JSON path to use USER_JSON_FIELDS constant (which already includes failed_attempts and locked_at) plus last_sign_in_at.
Design doc: none

- Modify: app/controllers/users_controller.rb (line 23 — JSON render)

- Test: spec/requests/users_spec.rb (if exists — add field assertion)

title: Split OpenAPI spec into multi-file structure — Redocly CLI split/bundle workflow
description:
Title: Split OpenAPI spec into multi-file structure — Redocly CLI split/bundle workflow

Description:
doc/openapi.yaml is 2582 lines with 60 paths and 24 schemas in a single file. Split into a multi-file structure using `redocly split` so each path and schema is its own file. Add a `redocly.yaml` config, `yarn openapi:bundle` script to regenerate the single-file output, and update the contract test support to lint the multi-file source. The bundled single-file output stays committed for tools that need it.
Design doc: none (standard Redocly CLI pattern)

Files:
- Create: redocly.yaml (Redocly CLI configuration)
- Create: doc/openapi/ (multi-file source directory — paths/, components/schemas/, components/parameters/, components/responses/)
- Modify: doc/openapi.yaml (becomes generated output from bundle, add header comment)
- Modify: package.json (add openapi:bundle and openapi:lint scripts)
- Modify: spec/support/openapi_contract.rb (point at bundled output, add lint note)
- Test: spec/contracts/openapi_contract_validation_spec.rb (existing — must still pass)
- Test: spec/contracts/users_admin_contract_spec.rb (existing — must still pass)

First failing test:
Existing contract tests fail if the bundled output diverges from the multi-file source (verified by re-running spec/contracts/ after split+bundle round-trip)

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] doc/openapi/ directory contains multi-file structure from redocly split (paths/, components/)
- [ ] doc/openapi/openapi.yaml is the root file with $ref pointers to paths/ and components/
- [ ] redocly.yaml at repo root configures the vulcan API with root pointing to doc/openapi/openapi.yaml
- [ ] `yarn openapi:bundle` regenerates doc/openapi.yaml from multi-file source
- [ ] `yarn openapi:lint` lints the multi-file source directly
- [ ] doc/openapi.yaml has a header comment indicating it is generated and should not be hand-edited
- [ ] `npx @redocly/cli lint doc/openapi/openapi.yaml` passes with zero errors
- [ ] All 19 existing contract tests pass against the bundled output
- [ ] Round-trip verified: split → bundle → contract tests green
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
yarn openapi:bundle && yarn openapi:lint && DATABASE_PORT=5433 bundle exec rspec spec/contracts/

Decision points:
- If redocly split produces unexpected file naming, review before committing
- If @redocly/cli should be a devDependency vs npx-only, discuss

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT hand-create the multi-file structure — use redocly split on the existing spec
- Do NOT delete doc/openapi.yaml — it stays as the committed bundled output for tooling
- Do NOT change any API paths or schemas — this is a structural refactor only

NOT in scope:
- Adding new endpoints or schemas
- Changing the contract test framework
- Setting up CI lint step (separate card)

title: Extract CommentAuthorLine — centralize author name/email/date display

description:

bundle exec rspec spec/contracts/ && npx @redocly/cli lint doc/openapi.yaml

- [ ] Run the exact Verification command — paste output
- [ ] git diff shows ONLY files listed in Files section

Story points: sp:3
Estimate: 15 min
notes:
[2026-05-28] Endpoint research complete (from session before /prepare-compact).
All 6 endpoints documented from real controller code:

1. POST /users/admin_create — collection route, admin-only
   Params: user[name], user[email], user[admin], user[password] (optional)
   3 success branches: password-provided / no-password+SMTP / no-password+no-SMTP (returns reset_url)
2. POST /users/:id/send_password_reset — admin, sends Devise email
   Errors: 422 SMTP disabled, 500 send error
3. POST /users/:id/generate_reset_link — admin, returns reset_url
   No email sent, writes reset_password_token directly
4. POST /users/:id/set_password — admin, params: user[password]
   Errors: 422 blank, 422 Devise validation, 500 internal
5. POST /users/:id/lock — admin, calls lock_access!(send_instructions: false)
   Returns: { toast, user }. 422 if locking self.
6. POST /users/:id/unlock — admin, calls unlock_access!
   Returns: { toast, user }

All responses include canonical toast: {title, message: Array, variant}.
All errors documented with status codes in card description.

Next session: invoke /project-tdd v2-05f.39 to implement.
TDD plan: write failing contract test → add spec/contracts/users_admin_contract_spec.rb →
hit each endpoint → validate response against openapi.yaml schema → fix spec as needed.
close_reason: Done. Estimated ~15 min, actual ~12 min. Added 6 user admin endpoint specs to doc/openapi.yaml (admin_create, send_password_reset, generate_reset_link, set_password, lock, unlock) with 3 new schemas (AdminCreateResponse, ResetLinkResponse, UserToastResponse). 9 contract tests validate response bodies against spec. All 19 contract tests pass, RuboCop clean.

title: Restructure compare endpoint to query params — component diff endpoint 2
description:
Title: Restructure compare endpoint to query params — component diff endpoint 2

Description:
GET /components/:id/compare/:diff_id embeds the second component ID as a sub-resource of the first, implying a parent-child relationship that doesn't exist. Both components are peers being compared. Restructure to GET /api/components/compare?base_id=:id&diff_id=:id with a response envelope containing metadata. Update OpenAPI spec to match.

title: Fix Project#details Arel string interpolation — use bind parameters
description:
Title: Fix Project#details Arel string interpolation — use bind parameters

Description:
Project#details uses string interpolation inside Arel.sql with RuleConstants values. The values are frozen string constants so there is no injection risk today, but the pattern is fragile — if a constant ever contains a quote character or user input, it becomes SQL injection. Replace with bind-parameter approach or Arel predicates per defensive coding standards.
Design doc: N/A — found by code quality audit

Files:
- Modify: app/models/project.rb (details method)
- Test: spec/models/query_performance_spec.rb

First failing test:
spec/models/query_performance_spec.rb — 'details does not use string interpolation in SQL'

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] No string interpolation inside Arel.sql in Project#details
- [ ] Uses sanitize_sql_array or Arel predicates instead
- [ ] Same return values (existing value tests still pass)
- [ ] Still 1 consolidated query (existing query count test passes)
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
bundle exec rspec spec/models/query_performance_spec.rb && grep -c 'interpolation' app/models/project.rb | grep '^0$'

Decision points:
- Whether to use sanitize_sql_array with ? placeholders or Arel.when/then predicates

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT break the single-query optimization — keep FILTER clauses
- Do NOT change the return shape

NOT in scope:
- Other Arel.sql usage elsewhere in the codebase

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC checkbox — verify with evidence
- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated
- [ ] Run the exact Verification command — paste output
- [ ] git diff shows ONLY files listed in Files section

Story points: sp:2
Estimate: 8 min
close_reason: Done. Estimated ~8 min, actual ~8 min. Replaced 5 Arel.sql string interpolations with self.class.sanitize_sql_array and ? bind params. Research confirmed: RuboCop Rails/SQLInjection flags this; GitLab/Discourse enforce no interpolation in Arel.sql. 4 Project#details tests pass (values, query count, no interpolation). RuboCop clean.

title: Fix CQS serialize_rows pluck on loaded rules — use in-memory data

Integrate openapi_first's test mode into the RSpec request spec suite so every JSON response is automatically validated against the OpenAPI 3.2 spec (doc/openapi.yaml). title: Generate OpenAPI 3.2 spec with rspec-openapi — auto-maintained machine-readable API contract
description:
Title: Generate OpenAPI 3.2 spec with rspec-openapi — auto-maintained machine-readable API contract

Description:
Generate an OpenAPI 3.2.0 specification automatically from existing request specs using the rspec-openapi gem (v0.27.0). No custom DSL required — existing request specs ARE the source of truth. Running OPENAPI=1 bundle exec rspec spec/requests/ produces doc/openapi.yaml that auto-updates when tests change, preserves manual edits, and validates in CI. OpenAPI 3.2 (released Sep 2025) adds streaming support, structured tags, and OAuth 2.0 device auth — all relevant for future MCP server and OIDC work.
Design doc: N/A

Files:
- Create: doc/openapi.yaml (auto-generated spec)
- Modify: Gemfile (add rspec-openapi gem)
- Create: spec/support/openapi.rb (rspec-openapi configuration)
- Modify: .github/workflows/ci.yml (add OPENAPI=1 step + validation)
- Test: spec/requests/ (existing request specs — no changes needed, they generate the spec)

First failing test:
Gemfile lock — gem 'rspec-openapi' not installed yet

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] rspec-openapi gem added to Gemfile (test group)
- [ ] OPENAPI=1 bundle exec rspec spec/requests/ generates doc/openapi.yaml
- [ ] Generated spec validates: npx @apidevtools/swagger-cli validate doc/openapi.yaml
- [ ] Spec covers all JSON API endpoints (not HTML pages, not Devise auth)
- [ ] Manual descriptions/examples added for key endpoints and preserved on regeneration
- [ ] CI workflow includes spec generation + validation step
- [ ] openapi: 3.2.0 in spec header
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
OPENAPI=1 bundle exec rspec spec/requests/ && npx @apidevtools/swagger-cli validate doc/openapi.yaml

Decision points:
- Whether to auto-commit updated spec in CI or just validate
- Whether to add Swagger UI endpoint (e.g., /api/docs) for interactive exploration

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT use rswag — it requires custom DSL in every spec, high maintenance burden
- Do NOT write the spec by hand — it will drift from code immediately
- Do NOT skip CI validation — unvalidated specs are worse than no spec

NOT in scope:
- Swagger UI hosting
- SDK generation from the spec
- API versioning (v1/v2 namespacing)
- Rate limiting documentation

title: Convert RuleBlueprint comment_summary from Ruby BFS to SQL — eliminate O(R*C) computation
description:
Title: Convert RuleBlueprint comment_summary from Ruby BFS to SQL — eliminate O(R*C) computation

Description:
RuleBlueprint#comment_summary runs a BFS graph traversal in Ruby for every rule during editor serialization. For 300 rules × 10 comments average = 3000 iterations with hash lookups and Set operations. This adds 100-500ms of CPU per component editor page load. Replace with a single SQL query using GROUP BY rule_id that pre-computes top_level_count, reply_count, and latest_at for all rules at once, passed via blueprint options.
Design doc: N/A — from v2.3.1+ performance regression analysis

Files:
- Modify: app/blueprints/rule_blueprint.rb (comment_summary field)
- Modify: app/blueprints/component_blueprint.rb (pass precomputed summary via options)
- Modify: app/controllers/components_controller.rb (compute summary hash in blueprint_render_options)
- Test: spec/blueprints/rule_blueprint_spec.rb
- Test: spec/requests/components_spec.rb

First failing test:
spec/blueprints/rule_blueprint_spec.rb — 'comment_summary uses precomputed data from options when available'

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Single SQL query computes comment counts per rule_id for the entire component
- [ ] Result passed to RuleBlueprint via options[:comment_summaries] hash
- [ ] RuleBlueprint reads from options hash instead of iterating reviews
- [ ] Falls back to current Ruby computation when options not provided
- [ ] Response shape unchanged (pending_count, resolved_count, total_count, latest_at)
- [ ] Eliminates 100-500ms Ruby CPU per editor page
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
bundle exec rspec spec/blueprints/ spec/requests/components_spec.rb && bundle exec parallel_rspec spec/

Decision points:
- Whether the SQL query should use the existing eager-loaded reviews or run its own query
- Whether to keep the Ruby fallback or remove it entirely

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT run a separate query per rule — the whole point is ONE query for ALL rules
- Do NOT change the comment_summary response shape

NOT in scope:
- Real-time comment count updates (WebSocket)
- Changing the triage table (separate endpoint)

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC checkbox — verify with evidence
- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated
- [ ] Run the exact Verification command — paste output
- [ ] git diff shows ONLY files listed in Files section

Story points: sp:5
Estimate: 25 min
close_reason: Done. Estimated ~25 min, actual ~20 min. Extracted Review.comment_summary_for (DRY BFS). Controller precomputes all 300 rule summaries in one pass, passes via options[:comment_summaries]. Blueprint does hash lookup, falls back to per-rule BFS. Eliminates 300 separate BFS invocations with Hash/Set/Array allocations. 7 blueprint tests + 69 related specs pass. RuboCop clean.
