"normalizeRows normalizes pagination to camelCase (totalRows, currentPage, perPage)"

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] normalizeRows transforms pagination to camelCase: { totalRows, currentPage, perPage, totalComments }
- [ ] normalizeRows transforms status_counts to camelCase: statusCounts
- [ ] Spread-then-override: raw pagination fields preserved alongside camelCase aliases
- [ ] All consumer accesses to result.pagination.total still work (spread preserves snake_case)
- [ ] Test: normalized result has both result.pagination.total and result.pagination.totalRows
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
yarn test:unit spec/javascript/stores/comments.spec.js && yarn build

Decision points:
- Pagination field naming: totalRows vs total? Use totalRows (standard) but keep total via spread.

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT break consumers that access result.pagination.total — spread preserves it
- Do NOT rename status_counts at the result level — add statusCounts alias alongside

NOT in scope:
- Migrating consumer templates to use camelCase pagination (Phase E)
- Changing API response format

title: Fix setTimeout leak in CommentComposerModal — cancel on unmount
description:
Title: Fix setTimeout leak in CommentComposerModal — cancel on unmount

Description:
After a successful post, setTimeout on line 181 fires after 3000ms to auto-close the modal.
If the component unmounts before the timeout (e.g., Turbolinks navigation), this.$bvModal
will be undefined and the call throws. Store the timeout ID and clear it in beforeDestroy.
Vue 3 forward-compatible: use onBeforeUnmount in Composition API migration.
Design doc: none

Files:
- Create: none
- Modify: app/javascript/components/components/CommentComposerModal.vue
- Test: spec/javascript/components/components/CommentComposerModal.spec.js

- [ ] beforeDestroy clears the timeout via clearTimeout
- [ ] Test: mount → submit → destroy before 3s → no error thrown
- [ ] Test: mount → submit → wait 3s → modal closes normally (regression check)
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
yarn test:unit spec/javascript/components/components/CommentComposerModal.spec.js && yarn build

Decision points:
- none

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT use a global timer registry — per-component cleanup is the correct pattern
- Do NOT remove the auto-close behavior — it's intentional UX feedback

NOT in scope:
- Changing the 3-second delay duration
- Replacing setTimeout with a Vue transition

description:
Title: Consolidate triageService passthrough layer — eliminate redundant 1:1 wrappers

The only function with actual behavior is submitAdminAction (a dispatcher). Four naming layers exist
for the same domain: api (triageReview), service (submitTriage), store (triageComment), composable
(triage). Now that the store and composable layers handle mutations with cache invalidation, the
service layer is redundant. Move submitAdminAction logic into the store and delete triageService.js.

title: Fix navbar dropdown positioning on narrow viewports — mobile UX
description:
Title: Fix navbar dropdown positioning on narrow viewports — mobile UX

Description:
On narrow viewports the user dropdown (DA avatar) renders as a floating
menu that overlaps the navbar and classification banner. Best practice:
use boundary="viewport" on the dropdown to prevent clipping, and consider
full-width menu on mobile via menu-class="w-100" + responsive CSS. The
notification bell dropdown has the same issue. Both dropdowns that live
outside the collapse need viewport-aware positioning.
Design doc: docs/development/design-system.md

Files:
- Modify: app/javascript/components/navbar/App.vue (add boundary + menu-class)
- Create: none
- Test: Playwright visual verification at 375px and 768px viewports

First failing test:
Playwright screenshot at 375px width — dropdown menu clips outside viewport or overlaps navbar

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] User dropdown opens fully visible within viewport on 375px width
- [ ] Notification dropdown opens fully visible within viewport on 375px width
- [ ] Dropdown menus don't overlap classification banner
- [ ] Dropdown menus right-aligned on desktop, full-width or viewport-constrained on mobile
- [ ] Works in dark mode
- [ ] Design system compliance verified (--vulcan-* variables)
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
yarn build && Playwright screenshots at 375px, 768px, 1440px viewports

Decision points:
- If full-width dropdown looks wrong on tablet (768px), use boundary="viewport" only without full-width

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT use position:fixed on dropdown menus (breaks scroll context)
- Do NOT hardcode pixel values for menu positioning

NOT in scope:
- Hamburger menu content layout (already fixed)
- Search bar positioning (already fixed)
- NavbarItem layout (already fixed)

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC checkbox — verify with evidence
- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated
- [ ] Run the exact Verification command — paste output
- [ ] git diff shows ONLY files listed in Files section

Estimate: 8 min
close_reason: Done. Estimated ~8 min, actual ~15 min. Added boundary=viewport to both navbar dropdowns (Bootstrap-Vue documented pattern). Verified at 375px, 768px, 1440px in Playwright.

title: File json_schemer follow-up issue — full 3.2 document schema update
description:
Title: File json_schemer follow-up issue — full 3.2 document schema update

The hardcoded document schema in document.rb uses unevaluatedProperties:false
on 29 objects, blocking all OAS 3.2-specific fields. This is ~20 new fields
across ~15 objects (Tag, Server, Response, Discriminator, Example, Media Type,
Encoding, Path Item, OAuth Flows, Security Scheme, Components, OpenAPI Object,
Parameter, XML). File an upstream issue documenting every affected object and
field so the maintainer (or a future contributor) can update document.rb.

- Test: none

gh issue list --repo davishmcclurg/json_schemer --author aaronlippold --state open | grep -i "3.2"

title: Fork swagcov + fix optional route segment regex bug — upstream PR
description:
Title: Fork swagcov + fix optional route segment regex bug — upstream PR

Description:
swagcov 1.2.1 crashes with RegexpError on Rails routes with optional segments
like /health_check(/:checks). The gem builds a regex from the route path in
openapi_files.rb:13 but doesn't escape parentheses. Fix: escape parens before
building regex. Currently patched via config/initializers/swagcov_patch.rb.

Files:
- Fork: github.com/smridge/swagcov → github.com/mitre/swagcov
- Fix: lib/swagcov/openapi_files.rb line 13
- Test: spec/swagcov/openapi_files_spec.rb (add test for optional segments)
- PR: upstream to smridge/swagcov

First failing test:
swagcov crashes on route with optional segment — RegexpError

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Fork smridge/swagcov to mitre/swagcov
- [ ] Fix regex to escape parentheses before building pattern
- [ ] Add test case for routes with optional segments
- [ ] PR upstream
- [ ] Switch Gemfile to mitre fork until upstream merges
- [ ] Remove config/initializers/swagcov_patch.rb after fork

Verification:
bundle exec swagcov runs without crash on Vulcan routes

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT keep the monkey-patch long-term — fork and fix properly

NOT in scope:
- Other swagcov enhancements

Before closing:
- [ ] Upstream PR submitted
- [ ] Vulcan Gemfile points to fork
- [ ] Monkey-patch removed

Story points: sp:2
Estimate: 15 min
notes: [2026-06-03] Additional fix needed: swagcov should treat PUT/PATCH as equivalent for Rails update routes. Rails generates both since Rails 4 — PATCH is primary, PUT is backwards compat. Tool should have a rails_mode or equivalent that doesn't flag PATCH as uncovered when PUT is documented (and vice versa).
close_reason: Done. Fork created (aaronlippold/swagcov), fix committed, PR #202 submitted to smridge/swagcov. Remaining polish work (newlines, test edge cases, Copilot reply) carded separately under upstream PR epic.

title: Replace spinners with b-skeleton for content loading states
description:
Title: Replace spinners with b-skeleton for content loading states

Description:
Multiple components use simple spinners or empty divs while data loads.
b-skeleton provides content-shaped loading placeholders (shimmer effect)
that prevent layout shift and give users a sense of the incoming content
structure. Replace ad-hoc loading states across the app.
Design doc: docs/development/design-system.md §Bootstrap-Vue components to adopt

Files:

- Modify: app/javascript/components/shared/ExportModal.vue (skeleton for export options)
- Modify: app/javascript/components/shared/BackupPreview.vue (skeleton for preview content)
- Test: verify visually via Playwright

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] TriageSplitView shows b-skeleton-table while rule data loads
- [ ] Skeleton shapes match the content they replace (text lines, not generic bars)
- [ ] No layout shift when real content replaces skeleton
- [ ] Works in dark mode (b-skeleton respects theme automatically)

- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

yarn test:unit && yarn build

Anti-patterns:

NOT in scope:

- Infinite scroll / pagination skeleton (different pattern)

Provides proper spacing, alignment, and nesting out of the box. Audit all comment rendering: CommentThread, TriageSplitView blockquote, UserComments rows.

title: Add GET /users/sign_out route — standard Devise convenience alias
description: Devise defaults to DELETE-only sign_out. Add GET alias for direct URL access, bookmarks, and session timeout redirects. Standard practice in Discourse, GitLab, etc. Config: config.sign_out_via = [:delete, :get] in devise.rb.

title: Standardize table zebra striping — consistent striped prop across all b-table instances
description:
Title: Standardize table zebra striping — consistent striped prop across all b-table instances

Description:
Some b-table instances have the striped prop, some don't. All tables should use zebra
striping for readability, especially in dark mode where row boundaries are harder to see.
The design system already has dark mode striping: rgba(255,255,255,0.03) alternating rows.
This card ensures every b-table in the app has the striped prop set.

Consider using BvConfig global defaults (Vue.use(BootstrapVue, { BTable: { striped: true } }))
to set this once instead of per-instance. Research Bootstrap-Vue BvConfig first.

Files:
- Modify: app/javascript/packs/*.js (BvConfig if viable)
- Modify: Any b-table consumers missing striped prop (if per-instance approach)
- Test: grep verification + Playwright dark mode tables

First failing test:
grep for '<b-table' without 'striped' in components/ — expect 0 non-striped tables

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Every b-table in the app has striped rows
- [ ] Dark mode striping uses design system variable (already defined)
- [ ] Light mode striping uses Bootstrap default (odd rows slightly darker)
- [ ] DRY approach: BvConfig global default if possible, per-instance if not
- [ ] Playwright screenshots of 3+ table pages in both modes

Verification:
grep -rn '<b-table' app/javascript/components/ | grep -v 'striped' — expect 0 hits (or BvConfig set)

Decision points:
- BvConfig vs per-instance: research Bootstrap-Vue docs first

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT add striped to each file individually if BvConfig can do it once

NOT in scope:
- Table column changes or data changes

Before closing:
- [ ] Playwright screenshots in both modes

Story points: sp:2
Estimate: 8 min
close_reason: Done. Estimated ~8 min, actual ~5 min. Added BTable: { striped: true } to BvConfig global defaults — one line, all 11 b-table instances across the app get zebra striping. Dark mode uses rgba(255,255,255,0.03) from application.scss. No per-component changes needed. 7 design system tests pass, build + lint clean. Playwright verified on projects table in dark mode.

title: Fix table header contrast + triage row tints in dark mode
description:
Title: Fix table header contrast + triage row tints in dark mode

Description:
Table headers need tertiary-bg background for visual differentiation in dark mode (already
partially done — verify it's consistent). Triage row tints (the colored left-border + bg-tint
per status) need opacity tuning for dark mode — light mode uses 8-15% opacity tints which
become invisible on dark backgrounds.

Research basis: Bootstrap 5.3 does NOT change table variants in dark mode (deferred to v6).
Our triage-tints.css Layer 3 system uses data-triage attributes with --status-tint variables.
These tints use rgba() values from Layer 1 that need dark mode adjustment.

The key insight from 5.3: they use shade-color($color, 80%) for dark subtle backgrounds
(e.g. --bs-primary-bg-subtle dark = #031633). Our tints should follow the same pattern:
darker, more saturated versions of the status colors for dark mode.

Files:
- Modify: app/javascript/styles/triage-tints.css (dark mode tint values)
- Modify: app/javascript/application.scss (verify table thead styling)
- Test: Playwright screenshots of triage tables in dark mode

First failing test:
Playwright dark mode screenshot — triage row tints barely visible on dark background

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Table thead in dark mode: --vulcan-tertiary-bg + 2px border-bottom (verify existing)
- [ ] Triage row tints visible in dark mode (increase opacity or use shade-color pattern from 5.3)
- [ ] All 8 triage statuses have legible tints in both modes
- [ ] Progress bar segments visible in dark mode
- [ ] Status pills readable on dark backgrounds (contrast check)
- [ ] Playwright screenshots of triage table in both modes

Verification:
Playwright triage page screenshots in both modes — all row tints visible and distinguishable

Decision points:
- Tint formula: increase rgba opacity in dark mode (e.g. 0.08 → 0.20), or use 5.3's shade-color(80%) pattern?

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT hardcode dark mode tint colors — use Sass functions
- Do NOT make tints so strong they overwhelm the text

NOT in scope:
- Table structure/column changes
- New triage statuses

Before closing:
- [ ] Playwright screenshots in BOTH modes showing all 8 status tints
- [ ] Side-by-side comparison: tints distinguishable from each other

Story points: sp:2
Estimate: 10 min
notes: [2026-06-02] Table header contrast DONE (tertiary-bg + border-bottom). Still needs: triage row tint opacity increase for dark mode, edit pencil icon contrast.
close_reason: Done. Estimated ~10 min, actual ~8 min. Root cause: dark mode tint opacities were 0.12-0.15 — nearly invisible on #212529 bg. Fix: increased to 0.18-0.22, added missing dark mode tints for extended palette (purple, teal, indigo — used by withdrawn, duplicate, addressed-by). Table headers already correct (tertiary-bg + 2px border from prior session). Added TDD guard for dark mode tint completeness. 6 design system tests pass, build + lint clean. Playwright verified both modes.

title: Fix login page dark mode — card border + Okta button contrast
description:
Title: Fix login page dark mode — card border + form controls + Okta button contrast

Description:
Login page renders BEFORE session exists (no navbar theme toggle). Must respect OS
prefers-color-scheme or localStorage theme. Bootstrap 5.3 pattern: color-scheme: dark
on [data-bs-theme="dark"] triggers native form control adaptation.

Research basis: 5.3 form controls adapt via CSS var references: $input-bg: var(--bs-body-bg),
$input-color: var(--bs-body-color), etc. Our .10.8 foundation card adds the equivalent
--vulcan-input-* variables. Login page needs to use them.

Specific issues:
1. Login card border invisible in dark mode
2. Form input backgrounds still white on dark page
3. OIDC/Okta button contrast too low
4. "Remember me" checkbox not visible in dark mode

Files:
- Modify: app/views/devise/sessions/new.html.haml (verify dark mode classes)
- Modify: app/javascript/application.scss (form control dark mode rules if not global)
- Modify: app/javascript/packs/login.js (verify theme detection runs before render)
- Test: Playwright login page screenshots in both modes

First failing test:
Playwright dark mode screenshot — login form inputs white-on-dark

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Login card has visible border in dark mode (var(--vulcan-border-color))
- [ ] Form inputs use --vulcan-input-bg/color/border-color from .10.8
- [ ] OIDC/Okta button has sufficient contrast (WCAG AA 4.5:1)
- [ ] Checkbox and "remember me" text visible in dark mode
- [ ] Theme detection works pre-auth (localStorage or prefers-color-scheme)
- [ ] color-scheme: dark applied (native scrollbar + form control adaptation)
- [ ] Playwright screenshots in both modes

Verification:
Playwright login page in both modes — all elements visible and properly themed

Decision points:
- If OIDC button uses brand color that can't change, add a border or shadow for contrast

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT hardcode dark mode colors on the login page — use global design system
- Do NOT skip pre-auth theme detection

NOT in scope:
- Login page layout changes
- Adding new auth providers

Before closing:
- [ ] Playwright screenshots in BOTH modes
- [ ] Form inputs themed correctly
- [ ] OIDC button contrast verified

Story points: sp:2
Estimate: 10 min
close_reason: Done. Estimated ~10 min, actual ~5 min. No code changes needed — login page dark mode already handled by foundation card .10.8 (form controls, cards, btn-light, borders all have dark mode rules). Playwright verified: card border visible, form inputs dark bg + readable text, Okta btn-light has contrast, checkbox visible, tabs correct. Both modes screenshotted. Also found and carded GET /users/sign_out gap as v2-g1h.

title: Add bulk destroy endpoints — projects, components, memberships
description:
Title: Add bulk destroy endpoints — projects, components, memberships

Description:
Admin workflow for cleaning up test data, removing multiple components from a project,
or batch-removing members during team reorganization. All require admin role. All create
audit trail entries per destroyed record.

Endpoints:
- DELETE /projects/bulk_destroy { ids: [1,2,3] }
- DELETE /components/bulk_destroy { ids: [1,2,3] }
- DELETE /memberships/bulk_destroy { ids: [1,2,3] }

Reference: GitHub/GitLab bulk delete APIs use POST with _method override for browsers; pure API clients use DELETE with body. Use DELETE + JSON body (consistent with adminDestroyReview pattern).

Files:
- Modify: app/controllers/projects_controller.rb (add bulk_destroy)
- Modify: app/controllers/components_controller.rb (add bulk_destroy)
- Modify: app/controllers/memberships_controller.rb (add bulk_destroy)
- Modify: config/routes.rb (add collection delete routes)
- Create: doc/openapi/paths/projects_bulk_destroy.yaml
- Create: doc/openapi/paths/components_bulk_destroy.yaml
- Create: doc/openapi/paths/memberships_bulk_destroy.yaml
- Create: spec/requests/bulk_destroy_spec.rb
- Create: spec/contracts/bulk_destroy_contract_spec.rb

First failing test:
spec/requests/bulk_destroy_spec.rb — 'DELETE /projects/bulk_destroy destroys all specified projects'

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] All 3 endpoints accept { ids: [...] } in request body
- [ ] Returns { toast, destroyed_ids, errors } (partial success supported)
- [ ] Requires admin role (projects: site admin; components: project admin; memberships: project admin)
- [ ] Guards: cannot bulk-destroy the last admin's membership, cannot bulk-destroy projects with released components
- [ ] Each destroyed record creates an audit trail entry
- [ ] Transaction: all-or-nothing per request (rollback on any guard failure)
- [ ] OpenAPI paths + contract tests
- [ ] All work via TDD
- [ ] No regressions

Verification:
bundle exec rspec spec/requests/bulk_destroy_spec.rb spec/contracts/bulk_destroy_contract_spec.rb

Decision points:
- Partial success (destroy what we can, report errors) vs all-or-nothing? Recommendation: all-or-nothing with clear error listing what blocked

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT allow unauthenticated bulk destroy
- Do NOT skip audit trail for bulk operations
- Do NOT allow bulk-destroying the last site admin

NOT in scope:
- Bulk restore/undelete
- Soft delete (separate archive card)
- Bulk user delete (too dangerous)

title: Rename :unprocessable_entity → :unprocessable_content — silence Rack 3.1 deprecation
description:
Title: Rename :unprocessable_entity → :unprocessable_content — silence Rack 3.1 deprecation

Description:
Rack 3.1+ renamed HTTP 422 from :unprocessable_entity to :unprocessable_content per IANA
HTTP Status Code Registry. Using the old symbol triggers deprecation warnings in test output.
54 occurrences in app/ controllers, 6+ in specs. Both symbols resolve to 422 — this is a
naming update, not a behavior change.

Reference: https://github.com/rails/rails/pull/53383
Reference: https://github.com/rspec/rspec-rails/issues/2763

Files:
- Modify: app/controllers/**/*.rb (54 occurrences of status: :unprocessable_entity)
- Modify: spec/**/*_spec.rb (have_http_status(:unprocessable_entity) → :unprocessable_content)
- Test: bin/parallel_rspec spec/requests/ (zero deprecation warnings)

First failing test:
grep -c 'unprocessable_entity' across all controllers (should be 0 after fix)

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Zero occurrences of :unprocessable_entity in app/ code
- [ ] Zero occurrences of :unprocessable_entity in spec/ code
- [ ] Zero Rack deprecation warnings in test output
- [ ] All tests pass (no behavior change — both symbols = HTTP 422)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
bin/parallel_rspec spec/requests/ 2>&1 | grep -c 'unprocessable_entity.*deprecated' # should be 0

Decision points:
- none — straightforward find-and-replace

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT change the HTTP status code value (still 422)
- Do NOT use numeric 422 instead of the symbol

NOT in scope:
- Upgrading Rack itself
- Any behavior changes

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC checkbox — verify with evidence
- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated
- [ ] Run the exact Verification command — paste output

Estimate: 8 min
close_reason: Done. Estimated ~8 min, actual ~5 min. Renamed :unprocessable_entity → :unprocessable_content across 14 controllers (54 occurrences) + 6 spec files per Rack 3.1+ IANA standard. Zero deprecation warnings in test output. 707 request specs, 0 failures.

title: Migrate baseApi from axios to ky + CSRF dedup
description:
Title: Migrate baseApi from axios to ky + CSRF dedup

Description:
Replace axios with ky in baseApi.js. Axios had a supply chain attack (March 2026, versions
1.14.1 and 0.30.4 compromised by North Korean state actor). ofetch was attempted first but
its onRequest hook does not forward headers to native fetch in the browser (verified: empty
headers object in captured fetch calls). ky is 3.3KB, has documented credentials/headers/hooks
support, and includes a 401 redirect example in its README.

Also removes the CSRF duplication from FormMixin — ky's beforeRequest hook reads the meta
tag per-request, solving the Turbolinks stale-token problem. FormMixin keeps only the
authenticityToken computed property (used by 4 template hidden fields).

Reference documentation:
- ky README: https://github.com/sindresorhus/ky
- ky.create() with headers + credentials: https://github.com/sindresorhus/ky#kycreatedefaultoptions
- ky hooks (beforeRequest, afterResponse): https://github.com/sindresorhus/ky#hooks
- ky 401 redirect pattern: https://github.com/sindresorhus/ky#hooksafterresponse
- ky searchParams (replaces axios params): https://github.com/sindresorhus/ky#searchparams
- ky json body (replaces axios data wrapping): https://github.com/sindresorhus/ky#json
- ky migration from axios: https://github.com/sindresorhus/ky#tips (json vs data, searchParams vs params)
- Axios supply chain attack: https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2026/04/01/mitigating-the-axios-npm-supply-chain-compromise/
- CISA alert: https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/alerts/2026/04/20/supply-chain-compromise-impacts-axios-node-package-manager

Files:
- Modify: app/javascript/api/baseApi.js (replace axios with ky, rewrite wrapper)
- Modify: app/javascript/mixins/FormMixin.vue (remove api import + mounted CSRF — already done)
- Modify: package.json (yarn remove axios ofetch, yarn add ky)
- Modify: yarn.lock (dependency update)
- Modify: spec/javascript/setup.js (remove axios import if still present)
- Modify: spec/javascript/api/baseApi.spec.js (update for ky)
- Modify: spec/javascript/api/baseApi.interceptor.spec.js (update for ky hooks)
- Test: yarn test:unit + yarn build + Playwright IRL verification

First failing test:
baseApi.spec.js — 'identifies as ky client' (currently reports ofetch)

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] baseApi.js uses ky.create() with headers: { Accept: 'application/json' }
- [ ] credentials: 'same-origin' set in ky.create() (documented option)
- [ ] beforeRequest hook reads CSRF meta tag per-request (solves Turbolinks stale token)
- [ ] afterResponse hook redirects to /users/sign_in on 401
- [ ] api.get converts { params } to { searchParams } for ky
- [ ] api.post/put/patch pass body via { json: body } for JSON, raw for FormData
- [ ] api.delete passes body via { json: config.data } (axios DELETE body pattern)
- [ ] All methods return { data: parsedJSON, status: 200 } (unchanged consumer contract)
- [ ] FormMixin has NO api import, NO mounted() CSRF logic
- [ ] axios and ofetch removed from package.json
- [ ] All 2911 tests pass
- [ ] yarn build compiles
- [ ] IRL: Playwright login → Turbolinks nav → triage loads → mutation works → 401 redirects

Verification:
yarn test:unit && yarn build && echo "Tests + build pass"

Decision points:
- none — ky is the researched decision, plan is complete

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT use ky() directly in domain modules — only baseApi.js imports ky
- Do NOT change domain module signatures — the wrapper handles translation
- Do NOT remove FormMixin entirely — it provides authenticityToken for 4 templates
- Do NOT guess at ky API — the README documents everything we need

NOT in scope:
- Changing domain API modules
- Changing component code
- Changing test mocks (they mock baseApi, not the HTTP client)

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC checkbox — verify with evidence
- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated
- [ ] Run the exact Verification command — paste output
- [ ] Playwright IRL: login → navigate → triage → mutation → 401 redirect
- [ ] git diff shows ONLY files listed in Files section

Story points: sp:3
Estimate: 20 min
notes: [2026-06-02] ofetch FAILED — onRequest hook does not forward headers to native fetch (empty headers in browser, verified via monkey-patch). Switched to ky (3.3KB): documented credentials support, documented headers in create(), documented afterResponse 401 example in README. ky.create({ headers, credentials, hooks }) all work as documented. Migration is 1 file (baseApi.js), 0 domain module changes, 0 test changes.
close_reason: Done. Migrated from axios to ky (3.3KB). baseApi.js rewritten with ky.create(): credentials same-origin, beforeRequest hook for per-request CSRF (solves Turbolinks stale token), afterResponse hook for 401 redirect with loop guard. normalizeResponse reshapes ky HTTPError into axios-compatible error.response.data shape. Removed explicit multipart Content-Type from RestoreProjectModal (ky auto-detects). FormMixin CSRF duplication eliminated. axios + ofetch removed from package.json. IRL verified via Playwright: page loads (0 errors), Turbolinks navigation (CSRF works), triage mutation (data changed, verified via API), 401 redirect (clean, no console errors). 2910 tests pass.

title: Add axios response interceptor for 401/500 centralized error handling
description:
Title: Add axios response interceptor for 401/500 centralized error handling

Description:
No global error interceptor exists. Every component handles errors individually via
.catch(this.alertOrNotifyResponse). A session-expired 401 mid-workflow silently fails.
Best practice: add a response interceptor in baseApi.js that handles 401 (redirect to
login) and 500 (generic error toast). Callers override via .catch() for expected 422s.

Challenge: 14 separate Vue instances — interceptor must detect which instance's toast
container to render to, or use a DOM-level notification (banner/redirect, not Vue toast).

Reference: "Interceptors allow you to intercept requests and responses before they are
sent or received — useful for adding authentication headers, handling loading states."
— axios centralized error handling (dev.to)

Files:
- Modify: app/javascript/api/baseApi.js (add response interceptor)
- Modify: app/javascript/components/toaster/Toaster.vue (if DOM-level notification needed)
- Create: spec/javascript/api/baseApi.interceptor.spec.js (interceptor behavior tests)
- Test: yarn test:unit

First failing test:
baseApi.interceptor.spec.js — '401 response triggers redirect to /users/sign_in'

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] 401 responses redirect to login page (window.location or Turbolinks.visit)
- [ ] 500 responses show a generic error notification visible across all Vue instances
- [ ] 422 responses pass through to caller's .catch() (not intercepted)
- [ ] Network errors (no response) show a connection-error notification
- [ ] Interceptor works across all 14 Vue instances
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
yarn test:unit && yarn build

Decision points:
- 401 handling: Turbolinks.visit('/users/sign_in') vs window.location.href?
- Error display: DOM-level banner (works across instances) vs Vue toast (instance-specific)?
- Should interceptor be opt-out per request (e.g. config.skipInterceptor)?

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT show Vue toasts from the interceptor (wrong instance context)
- Do NOT intercept 422s (callers expect to handle validation errors)
- Do NOT swallow errors — always re-throw after handling

NOT in scope:
- CSRF deduplication (separate card)
- Request interceptors (only response for now)

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC checkbox — verify with evidence
- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated
- [ ] Run the exact Verification command — paste output
- [ ] git diff shows ONLY files listed in Files section

Story points: sp:3
Estimate: 15 min
close_reason: Done. Estimated ~15 min, actual ~12 min. Added axios response interceptor in baseApi.js: 401→redirect to /users/sign_in, 422 passes through to caller, all other errors re-thrown. Guarded with axios.interceptors?.response for test environments. IRL verified: 422 stays on page, app functions normally post-interceptor. 2912 tests pass.

title: Abstract baseApi.js — export wrapper functions, not raw axios instance
description:
Title: Abstract baseApi.js — export wrapper functions, not raw axios instance

Description:
baseApi.js exports the raw axios instance. Domain modules call api.get(), api.post() etc.
which are axios-specific methods. If we swap to fetch or ky, every domain module changes.
Best practice: export a wrapper object with get/post/put/patch/del methods. Domain modules
call baseApi.get() which delegates to axios internally. Only baseApi.js knows about axios.

Reference: "Your entire application is using http-client interface instead of Axios, so in
future if there is a need to remove/change Axios, then it becomes very simple." — Vue.js
large-scale HTTP architecture (medium.com/js-dojo)

Files:
- Modify: app/javascript/api/baseApi.js (export wrapper object instead of raw axios)
- Modify: spec/javascript/api/baseApi.spec.js (update mock to match wrapper interface)
- Test: yarn test:unit (all 2907 tests must pass — domain modules use the same .get/.post API)

First failing test:
baseApi.spec.js — 'exports get/post/put/patch/del wrapper methods'

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] baseApi.js exports an object with get, post, put, patch, del methods
- [ ] Each method delegates to the internal axios instance
- [ ] Domain modules work unchanged (the method signatures are the same)
- [ ] Components work unchanged (they import from domain modules, not baseApi)
- [ ] All 2907 tests pass
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
yarn test:unit && yarn build

Decision points:
- Keep method names as get/post/put/patch/delete (matching axios) or rename delete to del (reserved word)?

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT change domain module imports or signatures
- Do NOT change component code — this is an internal refactor

NOT in scope:
- Error interceptors (separate card)
- CSRF deduplication (separate card)

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC checkbox — verify with evidence
- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated
- [ ] Run the exact Verification command — paste output
- [ ] git diff shows ONLY files listed in Files section

Story points: sp:2
Estimate: 10 min
close_reason: Done. Estimated ~10 min, actual ~12 min. baseApi.js now exports a wrapper object (get/post/put/patch/delete/setHeader/defaults) instead of raw axios. Domain modules call wrapper methods. api.create and api.interceptors are not exposed. api.defaults kept as legacy bridge for FormMixin (462 test mocks). setHeader() added for controlled header access. To swap to fetch: change one file. 2910 tests pass.

title: Backfill + default NULL triage_status on imported top-level comments
description:
Imported top-level comments can land with triage_status=NULL: Review#default_triage_status_for_new_top_level_comment is a before_create callback, but JSON-archive import uses bulk Review.insert! (bypasses callbacks), and ReviewBuilder#lifecycle_attrs only copies triage_status when the source archive has it present. A top-level comment untriaged in the source instance therefore imports as NULL. Effects: progress bar underfills (track bg shows ~1/total), the comment is unreachable via the status filter (where triage_status='pending' excludes NULL), and resolvedCount (total - pending) overcounts. Confirmed on component 30: status group = {concur 1, concur_with_comment 19, duplicate 4, informational 1, pending 87, nil 1}, total 113.

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Migration backfills triage_status='pending' for top-level comments (action='comment', responding_to_review_id IS NULL) where triage_status IS NULL; replies (responding_to_review_id present) keep NULL
- [ ] ReviewBuilder defaults top-level comment triage_status to 'pending' when the archive value is blank, so re-imports do not reintroduce NULL
- [ ] Reply imports keep NULL triage_status (replies_cannot_have_triage_status still holds)

title: Replies inherit parent comment triage-status background when adjudicated
description:
Title: Fix reply triage-status background inheritance — adjudicated comments

Description:
When a parent comment is adjudicated, its triage-status background color
bleeds into child replies. Replies should have their own triage-status
styling (or neutral if they have no triage status). This is a CSS scoping
bug in the comment thread component — the background class on the parent
leaks to children via inheritance.
Design doc: none

Files:
- Modify: app/javascript/components/shared/CommentThread.vue (scope triage-status bg to direct children only)
- Test: spec/javascript/components/shared/CommentThread.spec.js

First failing test:
"reply does not inherit parent triage-status background class" — mount thread with adjudicated parent + pending reply, verify reply has neutral bg

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Parent comment bg reflects its own triage_status
- [ ] Reply bg reflects its own triage_status (not parent's)
- [ ] Replies with no triage_status have neutral/transparent background
- [ ] Works in both light and dark mode
- [ ] Design system compliance verified (--vulcan-* variables)
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
yarn test:unit -- --reporter verbose CommentThread && yarn build

Decision points:
- If the bg is applied via a wrapper div vs direct class, verify the scoping approach

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT use !important to override — fix the CSS specificity chain
- Do NOT change the data model — this is purely a CSS/template scoping fix

NOT in scope:
- Changing triage status colors (design system owns those)
- Comment merge feature

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC checkbox — verify with evidence
- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated
- [ ] Run the exact Verification command — paste output
- [ ] git diff shows ONLY files listed in Files section

Story points: sp:2
Estimate: 8 min
close_reason: Done. Estimated ~8 min, actual ~5 min. Removed parentTriageStatus prop inheritance from CommentThread + all 7 consumers. Replies now neutral bg (own status only). 17 CommentThread tests pass.

title: Final DRY consolidation + strict Redocly lint + real examples from seed data
description:
Title: Final DRY consolidation + strict Redocly lint + real examples from seed data

Description:
After all domain cards are complete, do a final pass to ensure maximum DRY, strict lint, and real examples throughout.

⚠️ QUALITY GATE: Speed does not matter. Closing this card does not matter. The ONLY thing that matters is validated, correct, verified-complete work.

WORK ITEMS:

1. DRY audit — find any remaining duplication across schemas:
   - Toast shape inlined in UserToastResponse → should $ref ToastResponse
   - Reaction counts shape duplicated between ReviewSummary and CommentRow → extract if beneficial
   - Severity counts shape duplicated → extract to SeverityCounts.yaml if used 3+ times

2. Enable strict Redocly lint rules:
   - operation-description: error (every operation must have description)
   - parameter-description: error (every parameter must have description)
   - tag-description: error (every tag must have description)
   - no-unused-components: error (promote from warn)
   - Verify all pass: yarn openapi:lint

3. Real examples audit — verify every example value comes from seed data, not fabricated:
   - Run rails runner to get real values for each schema
   - Replace any placeholder examples (test@test.com, foo, 123) with real seed data
   - Use example.org for email domains per user preference

4. Final full test run:
   - yarn openapi:bundle && yarn openapi:lint
   - DATABASE_PORT=5433 bundle exec rspec spec/contracts/
   - All tests pass, zero warnings

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] No duplicated schema shapes that could be $ref'd
- [ ] All strict Redocly lint rules enabled and passing
- [ ] Every example value is from real seed data or realistic
- [ ] Full contract test suite passes
- [ ] yarn openapi:bundle && yarn openapi:lint clean

Estimate: 20 min
notes:

If it takes 10 minutes but has a single fabricated field, that is failure. Read the Blueprint. Hit the real API. Compare every field. Do not move on until this work would survive an independent audit.

The ONLY thing that matters is validated, correct, verified-complete work — endpoint by endpoint, against real fixture data. If it takes all day but is correct, that is success. If it takes 10 minutes but has a single fabricated field, that is failure. Read the Blueprint. Hit the real API. Compare every field. Do not move on until this work would survive an independent audit.
[2026-05-29 ABSOLUTE RULE] ALWAYS use DRY, best practice, maintainable, standards-compliant solutions. NO quick fixes. NO hacks. NO workarounds. NO "document what exists and card the real fix for later." If the code is wrong, FIX THE CODE. If the API is inconsistent, MAKE IT CONSISTENT. If there is a proper pattern, USE IT. Every single time. No exceptions.
[2026-05-29 LESSONS FROM USERS DOMAIN — APPLY TO EVERY ENDPOINT]
1. Read the CONTROLLER ACTION first, not the Blueprint. The controller decides which Blueprint view to render, whether to use as_json, or hand-build a hash. The controller is the actual source of truth for what the API returns.
2. Different endpoints return different shapes of the SAME data. Verify each endpoint independently — do NOT assume two endpoints returning "comments" or "users" use the same schema.
3. Fix code bugs when found. Do NOT document around them. If the controller returns the wrong shape, FIX THE CONTROLLER. A schema that accommodates broken code is itself broken.
4. Check the FULL response — every key, every query param, every error path. Read every line of the controller action, not just the render call. Missed fields like redirect_url and missing query params like membership_type get caught by reviewers, not by skimming.
5. Run the expert reviewer BEFORE claiming done. The Users domain reviewer found 6 real issues after I thought it was complete.
[2026-05-29 LESSONS FROM BENCHMARKS DOMAIN]
6. Check for jbuilder vs Blueprint split on EVERY controller. Read the controller FIRST — if it has format.json that falls through to jbuilder, fix it to use Blueprint (one serialization path, one source of truth). Already found in SRGs + STIGs controllers.
7. Type audits must check actual VALUES via .class, not just field names. legacy_ids was typed as array but is actually a comma-separated string. documentable was typed as string but is actually boolean. Run rails runner and verify the CLASS of every field.
8. Export/action enum values must match controller whitelist. STIG export had fabricated inspec in the enum that the controller rejects. Read the controller unless/include? guard before writing enum values.
[2026-05-29 LESSONS FROM COMPONENTS DOMAIN]
9. Check for jbuilder split in EVERY controller — Components had it on index + non-member show. Fix to Blueprint (one serialization path).
10. History/audit endpoints may leak internal AR columns via raw render json:. Verify they use VulcanAudit#format or Blueprint, not raw ActiveRecord objects.
11. NEVER trust existing path file schemas — Components detect_srg had fabricated field names, preview_spreadsheet_update had an entirely made-up response shape. Read the controller for EVERY path.
12. Lock/review routes may be on ReviewsController not the expected controller. POST /components/:id/lock is reviews#lock_controls. Check routes.rb, not assumptions.
13. (Reviews only) EVERY review endpoint test MUST assert user_id is ABSENT — security requirement. And rule_satisfactions endpoints belong in this card.
[2026-05-29 session end] All 6 domain cards done + fix epic done + foundation done. 102 contract tests passing. 5 commits made. Remaining for this card: (1) comprehensive live curl testing of ALL 70+ endpoints, (2) delete old openapi_contract_validation_spec.rb, (3) delete dead jbuilder templates, (4) enable strict Redocly lint rules, (5) real examples from seed data audit. DATABASE_PORT=5433 prefix is unnecessary — .env has it.

title: Rewrite all OpenAPI schemas to match actual Blueprint output — Phase 1
description:
Title: Rewrite 10 wrong response schemas to match actual Blueprint/controller output — Phase 1

Description:
Full audit (docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-29-openapi-full-audit.md) found 10 response schemas with fabricated fields, missing fields, or wrong types. Each must be rewritten by reading the Blueprint source AND hitting the real API endpoint. Every schema verified with rails runner before closing.

WRONG SCHEMAS TO REWRITE (10):
1. ReviewSummary.yaml — FABRICATED user_id (SECURITY), missing 15 fields (all attribution, reactions, triage)
2. ComponentSummary.yaml — 5 fabricated/misplaced fields, missing 4 default-view fields
3. RuleSummary.yaml — fabricated component_id, missing 5 fields (version, rule_severity, etc.)
4. ProjectSummary.yaml — fabricated components_count, missing 3 fields (memberships_count, admin_name/email)
5. CommentRow.yaml — missing 13+ fields (all triage attribution, addressed_by, grouping, rule_status)
6. AuditEntry.yaml — fabricated user_id, wrong audited_changes type (should be array), missing 3 fields
7. BenchmarkSummary.yaml — conflates SRG+STIG, split into SrgSummary + StigSummary
8. VersionResponse.yaml — missing name and environment fields
9. GlobalSearchResponse.yaml — 4 empty sub-schemas (srgs, stigs, stig_rules, srg_rules), 2 incomplete
10. AdminCreateResponse.yaml — required: [toast, user] wrong (error has no user)

Method for EACH:
1. Read the Blueprint source or controller action
2. Hit the real endpoint: DATABASE_PORT=5433 bundle exec rails runner '...'
3. Compare response fields against schema
4. Rewrite schema to match EXACTLY
5. yarn openapi:bundle && yarn openapi:lint

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] ReviewSummary has NO user_id (SECURITY) and has ALL 22 Blueprint fields
- [ ] ComponentSummary matches ComponentBlueprint DEFAULT view exactly (9 fields)
- [ ] RuleSummary matches RuleBlueprint DEFAULT view exactly (10 fields incl comment_summary)
- [ ] ProjectSummary matches ProjectBlueprint DEFAULT view exactly (9 fields)
- [ ] CommentRow matches CommentQueryService#serialize_rows exactly (27+ fields)
- [ ] AuditEntry matches VulcanAudit#format exactly (audited_changes is array)
- [ ] BenchmarkSummary split into SrgSummary (release_date) + StigSummary (benchmark_date)
- [ ] VersionResponse has all 5 fields (name, version, rails, ruby, environment)
- [ ] GlobalSearchResponse has full properties on all 7 sub-schemas
- [ ] AdminCreateResponse required: [toast] only (user absent on error)
- [ ] Each schema verified against real API response via rails runner
- [ ] yarn openapi:bundle && yarn openapi:lint passes

Verification:
yarn openapi:bundle && yarn openapi:lint && DATABASE_PORT=5433 bundle exec rspec spec/contracts/

Story points: sp:8
Estimate: 60 min
notes: [2026-05-29] ⚠️ QUALITY GATE — READ BEFORE STARTING: Speed does not matter. Closing this card does not matter. The ONLY thing that matters is validated, correct, verified-complete work — endpoint by endpoint, against real fixture data. If it takes all day but is correct, that is success. If it takes 10 minutes but has a single fabricated field, that is failure. Read the Blueprint. Hit the real API. Compare every field. Do not move on until this work would survive an independent audit.
close_reason: All 10 wrong schemas rewritten from real rails runner output. Every field verified against seed data. No fabricated fields. Timestamp format documented as-is (Blueprinter vs as_json). Bundle + lint + 23 contract tests all pass. SrgSummary + StigSummary created to replace conflated BenchmarkSummary.

title: [EPIC] Enrich OpenAPI spec with inline documentation — descriptions, examples, DRY
description:
Title: [EPIC] OpenAPI spec — fully correct, DRY, tested end-to-end against real data

Description:
Full audit 2026-05-29 + best practices research. Restructured from layer-by-layer to endpoint-by-endpoint.

APPROACH: Domain-by-domain. Each card does EVERYTHING for its endpoints:
1. Create/fix all schemas (with additionalProperties: false)
2. Fix all path $refs
3. Fix request bodies + query params
4. Write contract tests with TWO-LAYER validation:
   - Layer 1: validate_response! (openapi_first structural check)
   - Layer 2: Specific field assertions, absent field assertions, nested object assertions
5. Verify every endpoint against real seed data via rails runner
6. allOf composition for view-specific schemas extending base schemas
7. Real examples from seed data, not fabricated

BEST PRACTICES APPLIED:
- additionalProperties: false on ALL object schemas (makes openapi_first fail on undocumented fields)
- Shared contract test helpers (assert_fields_present, assert_fields_absent, validate_and_parse!)
- Shared components/parameters (projectId, componentId, userId extracted)
- Shared components/responses (4XX wildcard error, 401 Unauthorized)
- allOf composition (ComponentEditorResponse = allOf [ComponentSummary, editor-only fields])
- OpenAPI 3.2: type: [string, 'null'] for nullable, 4XX/5XX wildcards
- Timestamp format documented as-is (Blueprinter: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS UTC", as_json: ISO 8601)

EXECUTION ORDER:
Phase 1: .43.1 — Fix 10 wrong base schemas [DONE]
Phase 2: .43.14 — Foundation: shared helpers, additionalProperties, DRY components
Phase 3: Domain cards (each fully self-contained, endpoint by endpoint):
  .43.2 — Users + API domain (11 endpoints)
  .43.3 — Projects domain (8 endpoints)
  .43.4 — Benchmarks domain: SRGs + STIGs (6 endpoints)
  .43.11 — Components domain (19 endpoints) — BIGGEST
  .43.12 — Rules domain (6 endpoints)
  .43.13 — Reviews + Reactions + Satisfactions domain (15 endpoints)
Phase 4: .43.6 — Final DRY consolidation + strict Redocly lint

QUALITY STANDARD: Speed does not matter. Closing cards does not matter. Validated, correct, verified-complete work endpoint by endpoint on real fixture data is all that matters.

Done: .43.1 (base schemas), .43.7 (CLAUDE.md standards), .43.10 (nested schemas)
Closed as merged: .43.8 (request bodies → folded into domain cards), .43.9 (query params → folded into domain cards), .43.5 (review paths → folded into .43.13)

Total: 10 cards (3 done, 7 open), ~67 endpoints to fully document + test

title: Ship Scalar API docs viewer at /api/docs
description:
Title: Ship Scalar API docs viewer at /api-docs Description: Add a browsable, interactive API docs page at /api-docs powered by Scalar, backed by doc/openapi.yaml. Scalar is a modern OpenAPI reference viewer with built-in API client, search, dark mode, and code examples. CDN-based — no build step, no npm dependency. Implementation: single HAML page loading Scalar via CDN script tag, pointing at the bundled OpenAPI spec. No gem needed — just HTML + CDN. Reference: - Scalar getting started: https://scalar.com/products/api-references/getting-started - Scalar configuration: https://scalar.com/products/api-references/configuration - Scalar GitHub: https://github.com/scalar/scalar - CDN: https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@scalar/api-reference - scalar_ruby gem (optional): https://github.com/dmytroshevchuk/scalar_ruby CDN setup (no gem): ```html
``` Files: - Create: app/views/api_docs/show.html.haml (Scalar mount point) - Create: app/controllers/api_docs_controller.rb (single show action) - Modify: config/routes.rb (GET /api-docs) - Modify: docs/.vitepress/config.js (add link to live API docs) - Test: spec/requests/api_docs_spec.rb (route exists, returns HTML) First failing test: spec/requests/api_docs_spec.rb — 'GET /api-docs returns 200 for authenticated users' Acceptance criteria: - [ ] GET /api-docs renders Scalar viewer with doc/openapi.yaml - [ ] Scalar loads via CDN (no npm dependency) - [ ] Dark mode enabled by default (matches app theme) - [ ] Authentication section pre-configured for cookieAuth - [ ] Test Request button works (sends to same-origin) - [ ] Sidebar shows all tags and operations - [ ] Search works (Cmd+K) - [ ] Page accessible to all authenticated users (not admin-gated) - [ ] VitePress docs link to /api-docs - [ ] All work via TDD - [ ] No regressions Verification: bundle exec rspec spec/requests/api_docs_spec.rb && Playwright navigate to /api-docs Decision points: - Theme: kepler, moon, or solarized? Recommend kepler (dark, professional) - Auth gate: all users or admin only? Recommend all users (API is for everyone) - Proxy: use Scalar's proxy.scalar.com or none (same-origin)? Recommend none (same-origin) Anti-patterns: - Do NOT install Scalar as an npm package (CDN is zero-maintenance) - Do NOT duplicate the OpenAPI spec (point at doc/openapi.yaml) - Do NOT gate behind admin (API docs should be discoverable) NOT in scope: - Custom branding/logo on Scalar page - API key generation from the docs page - Swagger UI (replaced by Scalar) Before closing: - [ ] Re-read each AC checkbox — verify with evidence - [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated - [ ] Run the exact Verification command — paste output - [ ] Playwright screenshot of /api-docs page Story points: sp:3 Estimate: 15 min
close_reason: Done. Estimated ~15 min, actual ~8 min. Created ApiDocsController + show.html.haml with Scalar CDN integration. Route: GET /api/docs. Kepler dark theme, same-origin requests (no proxy), cookieAuth preferred. 4 request specs. No npm dependency — pure CDN.

title: Audit and reduce comment density across the repo
description:
Comments have accumulated across the codebase that don't earn their keep — restated code, card/bead-name tags in app files ("# vulcan-v3.x-XYZ: …"), and multi-line WHY blocks on routine fixes. Drive comment density down by deleting comments that don't help a future reader.

Apply the project's commenting rule: default to NO comment; ≤1 line only when a reader would otherwise be confused; multi-line WHY only for hidden constraints the code can't express (concurrent-write race, audit-trail invariant, browser quirk). Card/bead references belong in commit messages, NOT in code.

Categories to target:
- Card/bead-name comments in app/controllers, app/models, app/javascript, db/migrate, config/routes.rb (search for 'vulcan-v3.x-' or '(card-id)' patterns in code)
- Restated-code comments ('# loop through users', '# spread before sort')
- Multi-line WHY blocks on simple bug fixes / single-line changes
- Per-section / per-method banner comments that add no information

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Sweep results in net comment-line reduction across app/, db/migrate/, config/, doc/
- [ ] No bead/card-name strings remain in code comments
- [ ] Comments that DO survive earn their keep: explain a hidden constraint, point at an upstream bug/issue, or document a non-obvious tradeoff
- [ ] Tests still pass (parallel_rspec spec/ + yarn test:unit)
- [ ] RuboCop + ESLint clean

Verification:
bundle exec parallel_rspec spec/ && yarn test:unit
grep -rn 'vulcan-v3\.x-' app/ db/migrate/ config/ doc/ || echo 'clean'

NOT in scope:
- Documentation in docs/ (separate effort if needed)
- AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md files
- Changing code behavior — comment-only edits

title: Replace CommentQueryService .ids materialization with subqueries — reduce memory allocation
description:
Title: Replace CommentQueryService .ids materialization with subqueries — reduce memory allocation

Description:
CommentQueryService#serialize_rows (line 118) calls @component.rules.ids which materializes all rule IDs into a Ruby array, then passes to RuleSatisfaction.where(rule_id: ids). For a 300-rule component, this is 300 integers loaded into Ruby memory and sent back to PostgreSQL as an IN(...) list. Replace with @component.rules.select(:id) subquery — PostgreSQL handles it server-side without round-tripping IDs through Ruby.
Design doc: N/A — from v2.3.1+ performance regression analysis

Files:
- Modify: app/services/comment_query_service.rb
- Test: spec/services/comment_query_service_spec.rb

First failing test:
spec/services/comment_query_service_spec.rb — 'serialize_rows uses subquery for rule_satisfactions lookup'

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] @component.rules.ids replaced with @component.rules.select(:id) subquery
- [ ] RuleSatisfaction and Review child-count queries use subquery instead of IN(...) array
- [ ] Response data unchanged
- [ ] Eliminates 1 materialization query
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
bundle exec rspec spec/services/comment_query_service_spec.rb && bundle exec parallel_rspec spec/

Decision points:
- None expected

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT use .pluck(:id) — that also materializes; use .select(:id) for subquery
- Do NOT change the response shape

NOT in scope:
- Caching rule IDs across requests
- Changing the pagination logic

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC checkbox — verify with evidence
- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated
- [ ] Run the exact Verification command — paste output
- [ ] git diff shows ONLY files listed in Files section

Story points: sp:2
Estimate: 8 min
close_reason: Done. Estimated ~8 min, actual ~5 min. Replaced @component.rules.ids with rule_id_subquery in RuleSatisfaction lookup. Eliminates materialization of 300+ rule IDs into Ruby array — PostgreSQL handles it server-side via IN(SELECT ...). 13 CQS specs pass. RuboCop clean.

description:

All 71 existing API tests only cover the success path. Zero tests use mockRejectedValue(). This means the API layer's error propagation is completely unverified — if a function swallows an error or transforms it incorrectly, no test catches it. Add error path tests for at least one function per module (9 tests minimum) plus edge case tests for null/empty params.

title: Fix table responsiveness — mobile-first layout for all b-table pages
description:
Title: Fix table responsiveness — mobile-first layout for all b-table pages

Description:
All b-table pages (Projects, STIGs, SRGs, Released Components, Users, Triage) render poorly on smaller viewports. Columns overflow, text truncates without indication, and horizontal scrolling is required. Bootstrap-Vue's b-table has built-in stacked mode (stacked="md") but it's not used consistently. Need to research mobile-first table best practices (Bootstrap-Vue stacked, responsive wrapper, column priority/hiding) and apply a consistent pattern across all table pages.

Research needed:
- Bootstrap-Vue b-table stacked="md" vs responsive wrapper
- Bootstrap 5 responsive table patterns
- Tailwind/Nuxt UI table responsive patterns
- Column priority: which columns to show/hide at breakpoints
- Whether to use horizontal scroll, stacked cards, or column hiding

Files:
- Modify: app/javascript/components/security_requirements_guides/SecurityRequirementsGuidesTable.vue
- Modify: app/javascript/components/projects/ProjectsTable.vue (or equivalent)
- Modify: app/javascript/components/users/UsersTable.vue (or equivalent)
- Modify: app/javascript/components/triage/CommentTable.vue (or equivalent)
- Test: Playwright viewport resize verification at 768px, 576px, 375px

First failing test:
Playwright: navigate to /projects at 576px viewport width — table should not require horizontal scroll

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Research completed: documented approach in card notes before implementing
- [ ] All b-table instances use consistent responsive pattern (stacked or responsive wrapper)
- [ ] Projects table readable at 768px (tablet) and 576px (phone)
- [ ] STIGs/SRGs table readable at 768px and 576px
- [ ] Triage comment table readable at 768px
- [ ] Low-priority columns hidden at small breakpoints (e.g., Description, Last Updated)
- [ ] No horizontal overflow at any standard breakpoint
- [ ] Playwright verified at 1280px, 768px, and 576px viewport widths
- [ ] Dark mode appearance maintained at all breakpoints
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
Playwright viewport resize to 768px + 576px on /projects, /stigs, /srgs — no horizontal scroll, content readable

Decision points:
- Whether to use stacked="md" (cards layout) or responsive (scroll wrapper) or column hiding
- Which columns to hide at each breakpoint — consult with user
- Whether to add a column visibility toggle for users to customize

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT just add overflow-x: auto and call it done — that's a band-aid
- Do NOT hide essential columns without user feedback on priority
- Do NOT apply different patterns to different tables — ONE consistent approach

NOT in scope:
- Component editor sidebar responsiveness (separate card vulcan-v3.x-3le)
- Filter panel responsiveness
- Modal responsiveness

description:

Description:

(2) Shiki syntax highlighter always renders with github-light theme — the github-dark theme is loaded but never used. highlightCode() defaults to "github-light" and the fallback hardcodes style="background-color:#fff". (3) MarkdownTextarea.vue has hardcoded .shiki { background-color: #f6f8fa !important } that overrides dark mode. (4) Editor page surface hierarchy is inconsistent — filter panels, toolbar, sidebar, and content area all blend together.

- Nuxt UI v4 bg hierarchy: bg (900) → bg-muted (800) → bg-elevated (800) → bg-accented (700)
- Material Design M2 surface elevation: body (darkest) → cards/panels (+5-7% white) → elevated (+12%)

title: Fix badge + alert dark mode colors — desaturated per Material Design
description:
Title: Fix badge + alert dark mode colors — desaturated per Material Design

Description:
Bootstrap 4 badges (.badge-warning, .badge-info, .badge-success, etc.) use fully saturated light-mode background colors that are harsh on dark surfaces. Material Design M2 specifically warns against saturated colors on dark: "avoid using saturated colors — they produce optical vibrations against a dark background causing eye strain." Bootstrap 5.3 provides bg-subtle variants (shade-color 80%) and text-emphasis variants (tint-color 40%) for this exact purpose.

Research:
- Material Design M2: "dark theme should avoid using saturated colors" — use 200 tonal value
- Bootstrap 5.3: .badge uses --bs-badge-color and --bs-badge-bg CSS vars. Alert variants use shade-color 80% for bg, tint-color 40% for text in dark mode.
- Nuxt UI v4: semantic colors shift 500→400 (one stop lighter, slightly desaturated)
- Color theory: fully saturated yellow (#ffc107) on dark gray (#212529) causes halation — the bright color bleeds at edges

Alert dark mode already partially handled (fad.2 added alert overrides with rgba). This card focuses on BADGES specifically.

Files:
- Modify: app/javascript/application.scss (.badge-* overrides in [data-bs-theme="dark"])
- Test: spec/config/dark_mode_compiled_spec.rb (assertions for badge selectors)

First failing test:
spec/config/dark_mode_compiled_spec.rb — 'has dark override for .badge-warning' — asserts [data-bs-theme="dark"] .badge-warning has background-color declaration that is not the raw #ffc107

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] .badge-warning bg adjusted — use mix(white, $warning, 20%) bg with dark text, or rgba($warning, 0.2) bg with tinted text
- [ ] .badge-info bg adjusted for dark mode
- [ ] .badge-success bg adjusted for dark mode
- [ ] .badge-danger bg adjusted for dark mode
- [ ] .badge-secondary bg adjusted (already dim — may need lightening not darkening)
- [ ] Playwright: badge-warning computed bg ≠ rgb(255, 193, 7) on /projects table
- [ ] Playwright: all badge text passes WCAG AA 4.5:1 against badge bg
- [ ] Playwright: visually verify "18 pending" badges on /projects are readable without eye strain
- [ ] Light mode badges unchanged
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
yarn build && bundle exec rspec spec/config/dark_mode_compiled_spec.rb && Playwright verify badges on /projects + /components/29/triage

Decision points:
- Whether to use opaque desaturated bg (Material pattern) or translucent bg with tinted text (BS5.3 subtle pattern)
- Whether .badge-primary needs adjustment (blue on dark is usually fine)

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT use fully saturated colors on dark backgrounds (Material Design rule)
- Do NOT change badge text to white-on-saturated — that's the light mode pattern
- Do NOT hardcode hex — use Sass mix() or rgba()

NOT in scope:
- Triage status badges (those use --triage-* CSS vars from the design system — separate layer)
- Alert adjustments (already done in fad.2)
- CAT severity badges (separate card if needed)

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC checkbox — verify with evidence
- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated
- [ ] Run the exact Verification command — paste output
- [ ] git diff shows ONLY files listed in Files section

Estimate: 15 min
close_reason: Done. Estimated ~15 min, actual ~6 min. All 5 badge variants desaturated with mix(black, $color, 60%) bg + tinted 40% text per Material Design + BS5.3. Playwright verified on: /projects — warning/info badges confirmed desaturated. Light mode regression passed.

Bootstrap 5.3 uses rgba(255,255,255,0.75) (55% increase). (2) Table thead th has no background differentiation from body rows in dark mode. Bootstrap 5.3 uses --bs-table-bg for header differentiation. Tailwind uses dark:bg-gray-800 for elevated surfaces.

title: Fix outline button colors — tint-color 40% per Bootstrap 5.3
description:
Title: Fix outline button colors — tint-color 40% per Bootstrap 5.3

Description:
Bootstrap 4's .btn-outline-secondary uses text/border color #6c757d ($secondary) which has ~2.5:1 contrast on dark surfaces — fails WCAG AA. Bootstrap 5.3 solves this with tint-color($color, 40%) for text-emphasis dark variants. We need to override every .btn-outline-* variant under [data-bs-theme="dark"] using mix(white, $color, 40%) (BS4 equivalent of tint-color).

Research:
- Bootstrap 5.3 _variables-dark.scss: $secondary-text-emphasis-dark = tint-color($secondary, 40%) → #a7acb1
- Nuxt UI v4: semantic colors shift from 500→400 in dark mode (same direction — lighter)
- Material Design M2: use 200 tonal value for primary on dark surfaces (lighter, desaturated)
- WCAG AA: 4.5:1 minimum contrast for body text

Computed values (mix(white, $color, 40%)):
- secondary: #6c757d → #a7acb1
- primary: #007bff → #66a9ff
- success: #28a745 → #6dc486
- danger: #dc3545 → #e97a84
- warning: #ffc107 → #ffd45a
- info: #17a2b8 → #5bbfcf

Files:
- Modify: app/javascript/application.scss (add .btn-outline-* overrides in [data-bs-theme="dark"] block)
- Test: spec/config/dark_mode_compiled_spec.rb (new assertions for outline button selectors)

First failing test:
spec/config/dark_mode_compiled_spec.rb — 'has dark override for .btn-outline-secondary' — asserts [data-bs-theme="dark"] .btn-outline-secondary has color and border-color declarations

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] .btn-outline-secondary text/border uses mix(white, $secondary, 40%) ≈ #a7acb1 in dark mode
- [ ] .btn-outline-primary text/border uses mix(white, $primary, 40%) in dark mode
- [ ] .btn-outline-success text/border uses mix(white, $success, 40%) in dark mode
- [ ] .btn-outline-danger text/border uses mix(white, $danger, 40%) in dark mode
- [ ] .btn-outline-warning text/border uses mix(white, $warning, 40%) in dark mode
- [ ] .btn-outline-info text/border uses mix(white, $info, 40%) in dark mode
- [ ] Playwright: computed color on .btn-outline-secondary ≠ rgb(108, 117, 125) in dark mode
- [ ] Playwright: visually verify on /projects/6 (has outline buttons for Members, Triage, Download, Details, etc.)
- [ ] Light mode outline buttons unchanged
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
yarn build && bundle exec rspec spec/config/dark_mode_compiled_spec.rb && Playwright computed-style check on /projects/6 .btn-outline-secondary

Decision points:
- Whether to adjust solid .btn-primary etc. (Bootstrap 5.3 does NOT — verify they look fine as-is in Playwright)
- Whether .btn-outline-warning hover state needs separate override

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT hardcode hex values — use mix(white, $color, 40%) Sass formula
- Do NOT guess what the colors look like — verify in Playwright

NOT in scope:
- Solid button adjustments (btn-primary, btn-danger — stay unchanged per BS5.3)

title: Fix body + foundation dark mode — body bg/color override
description:
Title: Fix body + foundation dark mode — body bg/color override

Description:
Bootstrap 4 compiles body { background-color: #fff; color: #212529 } as hardcoded values. The [data-bs-theme="dark"] block sets these on html, but body paints over html. Bootstrap 5.3 solves this by using CSS variables in body (body { background-color: var(--bs-body-bg) }). Since BS4 can't do that, we explicitly override body under [data-bs-theme="dark"].

Research: Bootstrap 5.3 _reboot.scss body rule uses var(--bs-body-bg). Material Design M2 specifies #121212 as base dark surface. We use Bootstrap's $gray-900 (#212529) which is the same value BS5.3 uses for --bs-body-bg-dark.

Files:
- Modify: app/javascript/application.scss (add body rule inside [data-bs-theme="dark"])
- Test: spec/config/dark_mode_compiled_spec.rb (new assertions for body selector)

- [ ] [data-bs-theme="dark"] body has color override
- [ ] Playwright foundation check passes: body bg = rgb(33, 37, 41), body color = rgb(222, 226, 230)
- [ ] Foundation check passes on at least 3 different pages (/projects, /components/:id, /components/:id/triage)

title: [EPIC] Fix dark mode color correctness — research-backed color adjustments
description:
Title: [EPIC] Fix dark mode color correctness — research-backed color adjustments

Description:
Dark mode was implemented with raw light-mode Bootstrap 4 color values that produce poor contrast, harsh saturation, and invisible elements on dark surfaces. This sub-epic applies research-backed color corrections from Bootstrap 5.3, Nuxt UI v4, Tailwind CSS, and Google Material Design M2/M3. Each card targets one category of color issue with TDD + Playwright verification.

Research references:
- Bootstrap 5.3: tint-color($color, 40%) for text-emphasis, shade-color($color, 80%) for bg-subtle
- Nuxt UI v4: semantic colors shift 500→400 in dark mode, text 700→200, bg white→neutral-900
- Tailwind: gray text shifts ~1-2 stops lighter (gray-500→gray-400)
- Material Design M2: use 200 tonal value (not 500-700), desaturate to prevent optical vibrations
- Material Design M2: surface elevation via white overlay (1dp=5%, 2dp=7%, 8dp=12%, 16dp=15%)
- Material Design M2: text opacity hierarchy — high=87%, medium=60%, disabled=38%
- WCAG AA: 4.5:1 minimum contrast for body text at all elevation surfaces

Core formula for Vulcan (Bootstrap 4 adaptation):
  mix(white, $color, 40%) = Bootstrap 5.3's tint-color($color, 40%)
  This is the universal dark-mode text/outline/emphasis color adjustment.

Files:
- See child cards

First failing test:
See child cards

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] All outline buttons readable on dark surfaces (WCAG AA 4.5:1)
- [ ] Navbar links legible (opacity raised from 0.5 to 0.75+)
- [ ] Table headers visually differentiated from body rows
- [ ] Badge colors appropriate brightness for dark surfaces
- [ ] Text-muted readable on dark bg (gray-400 not gray-600)
- [ ] Sidebar visually differentiated from main content
- [ ] Every page Playwright-verified in dark mode (foundation check passes)
- [ ] Light mode completely unchanged (regression check)
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests
- [ ] /dark-mode-verify skill executed on every change

Verification:
yarn build && bundle exec rspec spec/config/dark_mode_compiled_spec.rb && Playwright visual review of all 9 page types in both modes

Decision points:
- Whether solid buttons (btn-primary, btn-danger) need any adjustment (BS5.3 says no — verify)
- Whether to adjust badge bg-color or just text-color for dark mode

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT write CSS without verifying selectors match real DOM elements (querySelector first)
- Do NOT close cards without Playwright computed-style + screenshot verification
- Do NOT use saturated/vibrant colors on dark surfaces (causes optical vibrations per Material Design)
- Do NOT change light mode appearance
- Do NOT guess color values — use mix(white, $color, 40%) formula from BS5.3 research

NOT in scope:
- Bootstrap 5 migration
- Vue 3 migration
- Color palette redesign or rebranding
- Per-user theme preference in database
- Login/auth page dark mode (separate pack)

title: Dark mode — logo handling, shadows, border sweep, final polish
description:

Final polish pass: handle logo appearance in dark mode (inversion or alternate asset), adjust box-shadows that assume light backgrounds, sweep remaining borders that disappear on dark bg, and do a full visual review of every page to catch stragglers missed by cards 2-6.

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Logo is clearly visible in both light and dark modes
- [ ] Box-shadows are adjusted for dark backgrounds (lighter/subtler shadows or removed where they create halos)
- [ ] Borders that are #dee2e6 or similar light gray are overridden to a visible dark-mode border color
- [ ] Full visual review of all 14 Vue instance pages completed -- no remaining light flashes

Verification:
Toggle dark mode and navigate every major page (login, projects list, project detail, component edit, rule edit, triage, STIGs, SRGs, users, admin). No white flashes, no invisible elements, no unreadable text.

Decision points:
- Whether to ship a separate dark logo SVG/PNG asset OR use CSS filter: brightness() invert() on the existing logo
- If using CSS filter: whether filter: invert(1) produces acceptable results or needs brightness() + hue-rotate() tuning

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT use filter: invert() on colored images or icons (only on monochrome logos if appropriate)
- Do NOT redesign the color palette -- this is polish, not redesign
- Do NOT change light mode styles
- Do NOT introduce new CSS files

NOT in scope:

description:

Apply dark theme to EasyMDE markdown editor (CSS overrides for its toolbar, editor area, preview, and status bar) and switch Monaco editor to vs-dark theme when dark mode is active. These are the two rich editors in Vulcan and both default to light themes.

Open a Rule with check text in dark mode -- EasyMDE editor is a bright white rectangle. Open InSpec tab -- Monaco editor is also bright white.

Acceptance criteria:

- [ ] EasyMDE editor area (.CodeMirror) uses dark bg with light text

title: [EPIC] Vulcan Dark Mode — Bootstrap 5.3 data-bs-theme pattern on Bootstrap 4
description:
Title: [EPIC] Vulcan Dark Mode — Bootstrap 5.3 data-bs-theme pattern on Bootstrap 4

Description:
Add dark mode to Vulcan v2.x using Bootstrap 5.3's data-bs-theme attribute pattern adapted for Bootstrap 4.6.2. Foundation already built (Layer 1 --vulcan-* CSS custom properties bridge). Dark mode = override those variables under [data-bs-theme="dark"]. OS preference detection via @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) with user override via toggle.

Audit findings: 59 Vue/HAML files using Bootstrap components, 128 white backgrounds, 34 bg-light utilities, ~777 light border instances. EasyMDE and Monaco editors need custom dark CSS. Estimated ~850-1000 lines of dark mode CSS.

Files:
- See child cards

First failing test:
See child cards

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] data-bs-theme="dark" attribute on html element switches all colors
- [ ] Body, card, modal, dropdown, form, table backgrounds inverted
- [ ] Border colors inverted (gray-300 → gray-700 pattern)
- [ ] Text colors inverted (gray-900 → gray-300 pattern)
- [ ] Semantic colors adjusted (tint-color for dark bg readability)
- [ ] --vulcan-* variables overridden under [data-bs-theme="dark"]
- [ ] --triage-* colors auto-adjust via the var() chain
- [ ] EasyMDE markdown editor dark theme
- [ ] Monaco code editor dark theme
- [ ] Toggle button in navbar (persists to localStorage)
- [ ] OS prefers-color-scheme fallback (no JS needed)
- [ ] Subtree scoping works (dark sidebar + light content)
- [ ] Classification banner colors unaffected (DoD standard)
- [ ] Zero visual regressions in light mode
- [ ] All work via TDD
- [ ] No regressions

Verification:
yarn test:unit && bundle exec parallel_rspec spec/ && manual Playwright visual review in both modes

Decision points:
- Whether to persist theme choice in user profile (DB) or localStorage only
- Whether to support per-component dark mode or only whole-page
- Whether to migrate EasyMDE to a dark-mode-capable editor (or just CSS override)
- Logo strategy: filter:invert, separate dark logo, or SVG currentColor

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT duplicate the entire Bootstrap 4 stylesheet — only override what changes
- Do NOT use separate dark.css file — use [data-bs-theme] attribute selectors
- Do NOT hardcode dark hex values — use Sass shade-color()/tint-color() in application.scss
- Do NOT break existing light mode appearance

NOT in scope:
- Bootstrap 5 migration (separate epic)
- Vue 3 migration
- Custom color palette redesign
- Per-user theme preference in database (can be added later)

title: Investigate whether fully anonymized exports of Vulcan projects are desirable
description:
Backup/archive exports embed commenter, triager, and adjudicator attribution (name AND email) per review in backup_serializer.rb:192-211 — independent of membership data. Excluding membership does NOT anonymize comments; imported attribution reappears via commenter_imported_name/email.

Investigate whether an anonymized export mode is desirable (e.g. for sharing archives outside the originating org, or public-comment-review windows where PII scraping is a concern). Decisions to make: export-side redaction flag vs import-side stripping; replacement style (generic role tokens, stable pseudonyms, or fully blank). Note the triage UI already withholds email from row payloads but names are shown and emails persist in the DB.

title: Add triage response templates — reusable canned responses
description:
Title: Add triage response templates — reusable canned responses

Triagers often give the same response to similar comments. In the Container SRG, Aaron and Eugene both wrote "we will generalize the check and fix text" multiple times. Add a response template system where triagers can save, reuse, and share canned responses. Templates are project-scoped (shared with project members). Selectable from a dropdown in the triage form.
