title: Add duplicate cluster detection — flag near-identical comments for batch triage
description:
Title: Add duplicate cluster detection — flag near-identical comments for batch triage

Description:
68% of Container SRG comments (79 of 116) are near-identical duplicates posted across multiple requirements by the same commenter. The system should auto-detect these clusters and surface them to the triager as "X similar comments from Y — triage as batch?" This uses simple text similarity (first N characters match + same author), not ML. Surfaces as a badge or section in the comments view showing clustered groups the triager can expand and batch-process.

Files:
- Modify: app/models/component.rb (add comment_clusters method)
- Modify: app/blueprints/component_blueprint.rb (expose clusters in show view)
- Create: app/javascript/components/triage/DuplicateClusterPanel.vue
- Modify: app/javascript/components/components/ComponentComments.vue (render cluster panel)
- Test: spec/models/component_spec.rb
- Test: spec/javascript/components/triage/DuplicateClusterPanel.spec.js

First failing test:
it('groups comments with identical first 80 characters from same author into clusters')

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] component.comment_clusters returns groups of 2+ comments with matching text prefix (80 chars) + same user
- [ ] Cluster panel shows at top of comments view: "{N} duplicate clusters detected"
- [ ] Each cluster is expandable showing: author, shared text preview, count, list of rules
- [ ] "Bulk Triage Cluster" button pre-selects all comments in the cluster
- [ ] "Merge Cluster" button opens merge modal pre-populated with cluster members
- [ ] Clusters update when comments are triaged/merged (removed from cluster view)
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
bundle exec rspec spec/models/component_spec.rb && yarn test:unit -- --grep "DuplicateCluster"

Decision points:
- Text similarity threshold: exact first-80-chars match vs fuzzy? Start exact, add fuzzy later.
- Should clusters span across rules or only same-author same-text?

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT use NLP/ML — simple text prefix matching is sufficient for this pattern
- Do NOT auto-merge clusters — only surface them for admin review
- Do NOT compute clusters on every page load — cache or compute on demand

NOT in scope:
- Cross-commenter similarity detection
- ML-based semantic similarity
- Auto-triage of detected clusters

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC checkbox — verify with evidence
- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated
- [ ] Run the exact Verification command — paste output
- [ ] git diff shows ONLY files listed in Files section

Story points: sp:3
Estimate: 15 min Claude-pace
notes: [2026-05-21] UX Research: Use simple text prefix matching (first 80 chars + same author) — not ML. Surface as inline banner above comments list: 'N duplicate clusters detected'. Each cluster expandable showing author, text preview, count, affected rules. One-click 'Bulk Triage' or 'Merge' from cluster view. Linear-inspired placement.

title: Add #rule-id linking + @member mentions — comment composer
description:
Title: Add #rule-id linking + @member mentions — comment composer

Description:
When commenting or replying in the triage view, typing # should open a picker of the component's rules so the commenter can reference another requirement (e.g., "We addressed this in #CNTR-00-000050"). Typing @ should open a picker of project members for callouts. Both render as clickable links in the comment display. Bug #1 from the user's list. This is a standard collaborative editing pattern (GitHub issues, Slack, Linear).

Files:

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Typing # in comment textarea opens a searchable rule picker showing component rules
- [ ] Selecting a rule inserts a linked reference like #CNTR-00-000050
- [ ] Typing @ in comment textarea opens a searchable member picker
- [ ] Selecting a member inserts an @mention like @Aaron Lippold
- [ ] Both render as clickable links when displayed in comment threads
- [ ] # links navigate to that rule in the requirements editor
- [ ] Picker filters as user types after the trigger character
- [ ] Esc closes the picker without inserting
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
yarn test:unit -- --grep "MentionPicker|CommentTriageForm"

Decision points:
- Research existing Vue 2 mention libraries (vue-tribute, vue-at) before building from scratch
- Decide on the stored format: raw text "#CNTR-00-000050" vs structured markdown "[CNTR-00-000050](/rules/123)"

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT build a custom textarea parser from scratch — research existing libraries first
- Do NOT store rendered HTML in the database — store the reference format, render on display

NOT in scope:

notes: [2026-05-21] UX Research: Use vue-tribute (wraps tributejs) — Vue 2.7 compatible, supports multiple trigger chars. Configure # for rules (shows rule_id + title, filterable), @ for project members (shows name + email). Stored as plain tokens (#CNTR-00-000050, @alippold), rendered as clickable links at display time. GitHub pattern.

title: Add 'canonical toast response' shared example to prevent AlertMixin regression
description:
**Surfaced 2026-05-02 by maintainability agent during PR-717 final review swarm.**

PR-717 .19d removed AlertMixin's string-toast handling branch with the assertion that all controllers return canonical `{toast: {title, message, variant}}` object shape. The architecture agent caught that 14 sites had been missed (fixed in commit 906941d). Risk of recurrence: any new controller that returns `render json: { toast: 'string' }` will silently fail to render a toast (frontend AlertMixin falls through to error path).

## Fix shape

Add a shared example to spec/support/shared_examples/ that any toast-rendering request can include:

```ruby
RSpec.shared_examples 'a canonical toast response' do
  it 'returns toast as a Hash with title, message (Array), variant' do
    expect(json['toast']).to be_a(Hash)
    expect(json['toast']).to include('title', 'message', 'variant')
    expect(json['toast']['message']).to be_an(Array)
    expect(%w[success warning danger info]).to include(json['toast']['variant'])
  end
end
```

Then sweep the request specs across controllers that return toasts and add `it_behaves_like 'a canonical toast response'` to each.

OR (cheaper alternative): add a single integration spec that hits a representative endpoint per controller and asserts the canonical shape.

## ACs

- [ ] shared_example defined
- [ ] Used in at least one spec per controller that returns a toast
- [ ] Sonar/CI catches a future regression where someone returns string-shaped toast
close_reason:
[2026-05-02 17:50] Closed via cf3656a. Shared example landed at spec/support/shared_examples/canonical_toast_response.rb asserting the canonical contract: toast is Hash with title (present String), message (Array), variant (in {success, warning, danger, info}). Failure messages cite PR-717 .19d / .a5u so future readers find the context.

Opted-in controllers (3 of 8 toast-emitting): reviews (POST /rules/:id/reviews success), stigs (POST /stigs success), memberships (DELETE /memberships/:id success). Other 5 (rule_satisfactions, rules, security_requirements_guides, components, users) can opt in with one `it_behaves_like` line as their next toast-related spec gets touched — incremental adoption is the lower-risk path than a single sweep PR.

Verification: shared example failure messages explicitly mention the architecture: "The string-toast shape was removed in PR-717 .19d (b671593) — frontend AlertMixin will silently drop string toasts. Use ApplicationController#render_toast or inline {toast: {title:, message: [], variant:}} hash." So a future regression author sees the historical context inline in their failing spec.

title: Split validate_foreign_key out of migration 20260502080000 (Strong Migrations 2-pass)
description:
**Surfaced 2026-05-02 by migration agent during PR-717 final review swarm.**

`db/migrate/20260502080000_change_review_responding_to_fk_to_restrict.rb:29` runs `validate_foreign_key` in-transaction. The PR otherwise applies the canonical Strong Migrations 2-pass pattern (add with validate: false, then `disable_ddl_transaction! + validate_foreign_key` in a separate migration). This .4 migration is the inconsistency.

On a production-sized reviews table, validation holds ACCESS EXCLUSIVE while it scans every row → write-blocking window.

## Fix

Split the validate_foreign_key into a paired migration:

```ruby
# 20260502080001_validate_review_responding_to_fk.rb
class ValidateReviewRespondingToFk < ActiveRecord::Migration[8.0]
  disable_ddl_transaction!
  def up
    validate_foreign_key :reviews, column: :responding_to_review_id
  end
end
```

And remove the inline `validate_foreign_key` call from 20260502080000.

## ACs

- [ ] New companion migration with `disable_ddl_transaction!`
- [ ] Original migration's inline `validate_foreign_key` removed
- [ ] FK regression specs still green
- [ ] Schema unchanged
close_reason: [2026-05-02 17:42] Closed via eef28a7. Split validate_foreign_key out of 20260502080000 into paired 20260502080001_validate_review_responding_to_fk.rb with disable_ddl_transaction!. Matches the 2kp + 14001 + 15001 patterns. Schema.rb unchanged. New regression spec spec/migrations/responding_to_fk_two_pass_spec.rb encodes the END STATE invariant (FK exists + restrict + convalidated=true) so future readers know what both halves of the 2-pass pattern are protecting. Idempotent on dev/test DBs that already ran the eager-validate shape — validate_foreign_key on an already-VALID FK is a PG no-op.

title: Wire request_uuid into rake tasks + JsonArchiveImporter (.14r producer side)
description:
**Surfaced 2026-05-02 by audit-compliance agent during PR-717 final review swarm.**

`stig_and_srg_puller:pull` (lib/tasks/stig_and_srg_puller.rake) creates O(1000) audit rows in one rake invocation but doesn't set `Audited.store[:current_request_uuid]`. The .14r consumer-side hook (VulcanAudit#ensure_request_uuid, commit 193f630) falls through to SecureRandom.uuid for each row → 1000 distinct UUIDs → operator can't query the rake-emitted audits as one logical operation.

## Fix

Wrap the task body in a request_uuid setter:

```ruby
namespace :stig_and_srg_puller do
  task pull: :environment do
    Audited.store[:current_request_uuid] = SecureRandom.uuid
    # ... existing work ...
  ensure
    Audited.store.delete(:current_request_uuid)
  end
end
```

Apply the same pattern to JsonArchiveImporter (services/import/json_archive_importer.rb#call) and any future bulk-audit-emitting code path.

## ACs

- [ ] stig_and_srg_puller:pull wraps task body in request_uuid setter
- [ ] JsonArchiveImporter#call wraps in request_uuid setter
- [ ] Test: rake invocation produces audits sharing one request_uuid
- [ ] Test: import invocation produces audits sharing one request_uuid
close_reason:
[2026-05-02 17:36] Closed via 8767214. Producer-side wrap landed for both targets:
- JsonArchiveImporter#call wraps body in VulcanAudit.with_correlation_scope
- stig_and_srg_puller:save_data body wraps in same scope (preemptive — Stig/SRG/StigRule/SrgRule are not vulcan_audited today, so the wrap is a forensic-correlation guarantee for any audited descendant added later)

Helper API extracted as VulcanAudit class methods (paired with .bundled_with query-side primitive):
- .with_correlation_scope(uuid: SecureRandom.uuid) { |u| ... } — snapshot+restore so it nests correctly under HTTP requests
- .current_request_uuid — single source of truth, used by both consumer hook + bulk-build paths

Bulk-insert gap fixed: activerecord-import's recursive: true persists rule.audits.build(...) rows directly via INSERT, bypassing the ensure_request_uuid before_create hook. Rule.from_mapping uses VulcanAudit.create_initial_rule_audit_from_mapping which now populates request_uuid at build time via .current_request_uuid. Verified via integration spec that the importer's BaseRule bulk-inserted audits now share the scope UUID.

Tests added: 7 unit specs (.with_correlation_scope), 3 importer integration specs (correlation + bulk-insert regression guard + scope nesting), 2 rake task unit specs (wrap invocation + nesting). All 2290 backend specs green.

title: Add concurrent index on audits(auditable_type, action) for forensic queries
description:
**Surfaced 2026-05-02 by design-review agent (Q8.1).**

`AuditEventBundle.bundled_with` (commit `7a7fc2e`) does:
```ruby
VulcanAudit.where(request_uuid: trigger.request_uuid)
```
backed by existing `index_audits_on_request_uuid`. After fetching the bundle, `#destroyed_reviews` filters by `action: 'destroy', auditable_type: 'Review'` in Ruby.

## Scale concern

Bundles are inherently small (one user request) so Ruby filtering is fine at our scale. But forensic UIs querying `Audited::Audit.where(action: 'destroy', auditable_type: 'Review')` across the WHOLE audits table (find all hard-deletes ever) hit a sequential scan.

## Fix (defer until forensic UI lands)

```ruby
class IndexAuditsOnAuditableTypeAndAction < ActiveRecord::Migration[8.0]
  disable_ddl_transaction!

  def change
    add_index :audits, %i[auditable_type action],
              name: 'index_audits_on_auditable_type_and_action',
              algorithm: :concurrently, if_not_exists: true
  end
end
```

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Concurrent index added on (auditable_type, action)
- [ ] EXPLAIN on `Audited::Audit.where(action: 'destroy', auditable_type: 'Review')` confirms index used
- [ ] No regression on existing audits queries

## Defer

Not needed until a forensic UI is built. Per design agent: "fine at small N".
notes: Surfaced by design-review agent on .4 work, 2026-05-02. Defer until forensic UI lands.

title: Batch-load parent rule_ids in drop_invalid_reviews (perf for 10K imports)
description:
**Surfaced 2026-05-02 by performance agent review (Top #3).**

`drop_invalid_reviews` in `app/services/import/json_archive/review_builder.rb:165` (commit `bf6a34d`) calls `Review.where(id: ...).find_each` and runs `valid?(:import_integrity)` on each. The integrity validators (`responding_to_must_be_same_rule` at `review.rb:343`, `duplicate_of_must_be_same_component` at `review.rb:360`) each invoke `Review.where(...).pick`, generating 2 SQL roundtrips per row.

## Scale impact

For a 10K-review archive: 10K × 2 = 20K extra SQL queries during the validation pass. Estimated 30s-2min for 10K imports (per perf agent). Sub-5s for typical 1K-review archives.

## Fix (defer until first 10K import)

Pre-load parent rule_ids into a Hash before the validation loop:

```ruby
def drop_invalid_reviews(external_to_new_id)
  return 0 if external_to_new_id.empty?

  # Batch-load parent rule_ids — one query instead of 2 SQL/row
  rule_id_lookup = Review.where(id: external_to_new_id.values).pluck(:id, :rule_id).to_h
  # ... validators read from this hash via thread-local or pass-through
end
```

Validators would need a way to access the prebuilt hash — either via thread-local (gross) or refactor to take an optional ancestor-scope parameter.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Single SQL query loads all parent rule_ids before validation pass
- [ ] Validators consume the prebuilt lookup, not per-row pick
- [ ] Test: 1000-review archive imports in <5s (timing assertion or perf benchmark)
- [ ] No correctness regression on existing 47 importer specs

## Defer

Per perf agent: "Only matters when archives exceed 1K reviews — defer until needed."
notes: Surfaced by perf agent review on .4 work, 2026-05-02. Defer until first 10K-review archive appears.

title: Counter cache drift verification rake task
description:
**Surfaced 2026-05-02 by DB-schema agent review (DB #11).**

`components.rules_count` counter cache has historically drifted (fix migration `20250813154605_fix_component_rules_counter_cache.rb` exists). Current code paths handling this:

- `Component#amoeba customize` resets to 0 (component.rb:21-25) for clones
- `Rule.update_component_rules_count` (rule.rb:462-468) only fires when `@single_rule_clone` is true
- `Component.reset_counters` calls in `from_mapping` (component.rb:354, 512) handle bulk-import paths

## Drift scenarios

- Bulk imports via `Rule.import` skip the single-clone hook
- `memberships_count` (polymorphic) has known Rails bugs across STI
- Any future code path that creates Rules outside the blessed paths drifts silently

## Fix

Two complementary remediations:

(A) Periodic verification rake task:
```ruby
namespace :counter_cache do
  desc 'Reset all counter caches to actual row counts'
  task verify: :environment do
    Component.find_each { |c| Component.reset_counters(c.id, :rules) }
    Project.find_each { |p| Project.reset_counters(p.id, :memberships) if p.respond_to?(:memberships) }
    # ... etc
  end
end
```

(B) Replace counter_cache with `counter_culture` gem entry that handles polymorphic + STI cleanly.

Recommend (A) for now; (B) if drift recurs.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Rake task `counter_cache:verify` resets all counter caches
- [ ] Documented in README or runbook
- [ ] Optional: schedule via cron for weekly run
- [ ] Test: task runs without errors on dev DB
notes: Surfaced by DB agent review on .4 work, 2026-05-02. Recurring drift scenarios documented.

title: Widen audits.auditable_id/associated_id/audited_user_id to bigint
description:
**Surfaced 2026-05-02 by DB-schema agent review (DB #10).**

`db/schema.rb:41-44` declares `t.integer "auditable_id"` and `t.integer "associated_id"` while every other PK in the schema is bigint. Likely from when audited gem first installed (pre-Rails 5.1).

## Impact

- Audit table integer-overflow at ~2.1B rows — unlikely soon, but real ceiling
- **JOIN performance**: int/bigint mismatch breaks index-only scans on PG when JOINing audits → reviews/rules (implicit cast). Already a concern given the recent backfill (`db/migrate/20260501135108_backfill_review_audit_associations.rb` joins audits → rules)

## Fix

Migration to widen `auditable_id`, `associated_id`, `audited_user_id` to bigint:

```ruby
class WidenAuditFkColumnsToBigint < ActiveRecord::Migration[8.0]
  disable_ddl_transaction!

  def up
    %i[auditable_id associated_id audited_user_id].each do |col|
      change_column :audits, col, :bigint
    end
  end
end
```

`change_column` on a populated table can take ACCESS EXCLUSIVE — schedule for a maintenance window OR use the pgrepack approach for very large audits tables.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Migration changes 3 columns to bigint
- [ ] Schema.rb updated
- [ ] No regression on backfill migration JOIN performance
- [ ] Documented as production maintenance step (downtime estimate based on audits row count)
notes: Surfaced by DB agent review on .4 work, 2026-05-02. Pre-existing schema oversight; matters for JOIN perf today, integer-overflow eventually.

title: Change base_rules.review_requestor_id FK to on_delete: :nullify
description:
**Surfaced 2026-05-02 by DB-schema agent review (DB #9).**

`db/schema.rb:394` shows `base_rules.review_requestor_id` FK exists but no `on_delete:` clause is declared (defaults to `RESTRICT`). `Rule#review_requestor` is `optional: true` (`rule.rb:81`).

## Impact

Deleting a User who has open review requests fails with PG FK violation. There's no `User has_many :review_requests` cleanup callback, so the relationship is invisible to User#destroy. Admin trying to delete a user gets opaque PG error.

## Fix

Mismatched FK semantics: `optional: true` should pair with `on_delete: :nullify`, not `:restrict`.

```ruby
remove_foreign_key :base_rules, column: :review_requestor_id
add_foreign_key :base_rules, :users, column: :review_requestor_id, on_delete: :nullify, validate: false
```

Then separate `validate_foreign_key`.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] FK on base_rules.review_requestor_id changes to on_delete: :nullify
- [ ] Test: User#destroy of a user with open review requests succeeds (request_requestor_id becomes nil)
- [ ] Test: existing review-request workflow unaffected
notes: Surfaced by DB agent review on .4 work, 2026-05-02. FK semantics mismatch with optional: true.

title: Add FK constraints on rule_satisfactions (rule_id + satisfied_by_rule_id)
description:
**Surfaced 2026-05-02 by DB-schema agent review (DB #8).**

`db/schema.rb` `rule_satisfactions` HABTM table has zero individual-column indexes and no FK constraints — only composite uniqueness indexes.

## Impact

- Single-column lookups (Rule.satisfies queries) work via composite indexes when left-anchored, but reverse direction depends on the second composite
- **No FK means SQL-deleting a rule orphans satisfaction rows silently**
- Same shape as the gap surfaced in N1 (vulcan-v3.x-j4a) for reviews.user_id

## Fix

```ruby
add_foreign_key :rule_satisfactions, :base_rules, column: :rule_id, on_delete: :cascade, validate: false
add_foreign_key :rule_satisfactions, :base_rules, column: :satisfied_by_rule_id, on_delete: :cascade, validate: false
```

Then separate `validate_foreign_key` migration per Strong Migrations.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Backfill check: no orphan satisfactions
- [ ] FK on rule_id with on_delete: :cascade
- [ ] FK on satisfied_by_rule_id with on_delete: :cascade
- [ ] Validate in separate migration (concurrent if production has rows)
- [ ] No regression on satisfies/satisfied_by spec coverage
notes: Surfaced by DB agent review on .4 work, 2026-05-02. Schema FK gap.

title: Remove ineffective inverse_of on polymorphic Membership association
description:
**Surfaced 2026-05-02 by DB-schema agent review (DB #5).**

`app/models/membership.rb:12` declares `belongs_to :membership, polymorphic: true` (no `inverse_of`). Both Project and Component declare `inverse_of: :membership` on their side (`project.rb:14`, `component.rb:65`).

## Real Rails limitation

Rails cannot auto-detect `inverse_of` for polymorphic `belongs_to`. The `inverse_of: :membership` on the parent side is silently ignored. Result: auto-loaded child memberships do not point back to in-memory parent → double SQL hits and stale parents in callbacks.

## Concrete failure

`Membership#after_destroy → membership.update_admin_contact_info` reads from DB even though parent is in memory.

## Fix options

(A) Remove the `inverse_of: :membership` from Project + Component — explicit acknowledgment of the polymorphic limitation
(B) Split `Membership` into two non-polymorphic associations (`ProjectMembership`, `ComponentMembership`) — bigger refactor

Recommend (A) — minimal, accurate.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Remove `inverse_of: :membership` from project.rb + component.rb membership associations
- [ ] Add comment documenting why (polymorphic limitation)
- [ ] No regression on parallel_rspec sweep
notes: Surfaced by DB agent review on .4 work, 2026-05-02. Polymorphic + inverse_of is documented Rails limitation.

title: Lock or semi-lock rule editing for child rules in a satisfies relationship
description:
# Lock or semi-lock rule editing for child rules in a satisfies relationship

## Why this exists

When `Rule#satisfied_by` is non-empty, the rule is logically inherited from a parent rule. The canonical edit point is the parent — edits propagate downward, not upward. Today:

- `Rule#row_editable?` (rule.rb:329-335) returns false for satisfied children — the spreadsheet/list view treats the row as read-only
- BUT the full rule editor (RuleEditor.vue) does not visibly enforce this. Users can navigate into the child rule's editor and start typing, even though their changes shouldn't be the source of truth

This is a UX clarity gap: the inheritance model isn't visible at the place where users actually do their editing.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Rule editor (RuleEditor.vue and any per-section editor surfaces) renders visibly locked OR semi-locked when `rule.satisfied_by.any?` (i.e., the rule is a child in the satisfies relationship)
- [ ] Locked state shows an explanatory banner naming the parent rule(s) and providing a one-click jump to the parent's editor
- [ ] If semi-lock is the chosen UX (read-only display with explicit override), the override path requires confirmation and audit logging
- [ ] Spreadsheet view's existing read-only behavior remains consistent
- [ ] Frontend specs cover the locked-banner display and the parent-jump link
- [ ] Manual smoke verifies the inheritance is now obvious to a first-time user

## Notes

- This is rule-editor UX work, NOT a federal-compliance issue. Defer outside PR-717.
- Investigate before implementing whether semi-lock-with-override is appropriate — there may be cases (e.g., overlay overrides) where editing a child intentionally diverges from the parent. Document the chosen behavior with the rationale.
- Cross-reference Task 32 (DISA rule-editor streamlining) — that task was dropped from PR-717 because it contained a fabricated-gap claim, but the satisfies-child UX is an actual concrete gap that may inform a refreshed Task 32 if it's reopened later.

title: Fix turbolinks Vue pack-mount race in v2.x
description:
When navigating between Vue-mounted pages via turbolinks (e.g. /components/:id/triage -> /components/:id/:rule), the new page's pack JS is appended to <head> AFTER turbolinks:load has fired for that navigation. The pack's listener never runs and Vue doesn't mount -> blank page.

Current narrow workaround (commit pending): data-turbolinks="false" on the rule link in ComponentComments + UserComments + ComponentName link.

Real fix: change every Vue pack's mount registration from:

  document.addEventListener('turbolinks:load', () => new Vue({ el: '#X' }));

to a self-mounting pattern that runs immediately if the DOM is ready AND on turbolinks:load:

  const mount = () => {
    const el = document.querySelector('#X');
    if (el && !el.__vue__) new Vue({ el });
  };
  mount();
  document.addEventListener('turbolinks:load', mount);

Affected packs (all in app/javascript/packs/):
- project_component.js
- project_components.js
- component_triage.js
- released_component.js
- rules.js
- stigs.js, stig.js
- security_requirements_guides.js
- users.js, login.js
- (any other pack with the same registration shape)

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] All packs use the self-mounting pattern
- [ ] Triage table -> rule editor navigation works without data-turbolinks=false
- [ ] Remove the data-turbolinks=false workaround from ComponentComments.vue + UserComments.vue
- [ ] No double-mount when turbolinks:load fires for a fresh page load
- [ ] All existing tests pass
close_reason: Folded into docs/plans/PR717-public-comment-review/99-final-test-sweep-and-acceptance.md 'Live-test follow-ups' section to keep one tracking system per PR.

**Fix:** Use jbuilder for HTML paths too: `render_to_string(template: '...', formats: [:json])` or build explicit JSON with only needed fields.

title: M1: Use SQL subtraction for Project#available_members
description:
**Location:** `app/models/project.rb:58-59`

**Problem:** `User.select(:id, :name, :email) - users.select(:id, :name, :email)` loads ALL users then does Ruby set subtraction. Scales linearly with user count.

title: Lockout on repeated failed password attempts during unlink
description:
**Context:** Current unlink flow requires current password verification (CSRF + account ownership proof). But there's no rate limit on bad attempts — an attacker with a stolen session could brute-force the password via unlink.

**Proposed approach:** Reuse Devise's `:lockable` module infrastructure. On failed `valid_password?` in unlink_identity, call `user.failed_attempts += 1` and `user.lock_access!` when threshold reached.

**Option A (simple):** Manual increment in the unlink controller rescue path
**Option B (proper):** Wrap the password check so Devise's built-in failed_attempts tracking handles it

**Security win:** Same lockout semantics as failed login (3 attempts, 15 min unlock per `VULCAN_LOCKOUT_*` settings).

**Why:** Unlink is a privileged account operation; same lockout protection as login.
**How to apply:** After main fix merged. Wire into existing Devise lockable infrastructure — don't roll a separate counter.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Failed unlink password attempt increments user.failed_attempts
- [ ] After VULCAN_LOCKOUT_MAX_ATTEMPTS (default 3), user is locked
- [ ] Lockout uses existing Devise lockable infrastructure, not a separate counter
- [ ] Successful unlink resets failed_attempts
- [ ] Lockout message matches login lockout message format
- [ ] Request spec: 3 bad unlink attempts → user.access_locked? is true
- [ ] Request spec: 2 bad attempts + 1 correct → unlink succeeds, counter reset
- [ ] TDD red → green

title: Add 'Link with <provider>' button in profile for symmetry with Unlink
description:
**Context:** We now have an "Unlink" button in the profile for users with a linked external identity. The symmetrical opposite — a "Link with <provider>" button — does not exist. After unlinking, a user can only re-link by signing out, signing in via OIDC, and relying on `VULCAN_AUTO_LINK_USER=true` email matching.

**Proposed flow:**
1. Profile shows "Link with Okta" button when `user.provider` is nil and an OIDC provider is configured
2. Click → session flag `link_in_progress: true` is set, user redirected to `/users/auth/oidc`
3. OmniauthCallbacksController detects `session[:link_in_progress]` + existing signed-in user → attaches the returning OIDC identity to the current user (doesn't create new account)
4. Edge case: if the returning OIDC identity already belongs to another account, refuse with clear error
5. Audit the link event via `audit_comment`

**Files:**
- `app/views/devise/registrations/edit.html.haml` or UserProfile.vue — new button
- `app/controllers/users/omniauth_callbacks_controller.rb` — detect link mode
- `app/controllers/users/registrations_controller.rb` — new `initiate_link` action (sets session flag)
- `config/routes.rb` — POST `/users/initiate_link`
- `spec/requests/users/initiate_link_spec.rb` — new spec

**Why:** UX symmetry. Users who unlink should be able to re-link without admin help or env var gymnastics.
**How to apply:** After main v2.3.1 fix is merged. Not blocking release.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Button visible in profile when user.provider is nil AND at least one OIDC/LDAP provider is enabled
- [ ] Click → POST /users/initiate_link → sets session[:link_in_progress]=true → redirects to /users/auth/oidc
- [ ] OmniauthCallbacksController detects link mode when session flag set + current_user present
- [ ] Link mode attaches identity to current_user instead of creating new account
- [ ] Edge case: returning identity already belongs to another user → clear error, session flag cleared
- [ ] audit_comment: 'Linked <PROVIDER> identity via profile'
- [ ] Request spec for initiate_link flow
- [ ] Vue test for button visibility
- [ ] System spec (optional) for click-through
- [ ] TDD red → green

title: Stop leaking exception.message and log server-side in users_controller rescue blocks
description:
**Locations:**
- `app/controllers/users_controller.rb:153-156` (in `send_password_reset`)
- `app/controllers/users_controller.rb:213-216` (in `admin_create`)

**Buggy code:**
```ruby
rescue StandardError => e
  render json: {
    toast: { title: 'Could not send password reset.', message: [e.message], variant: 'danger' }
  }, status: :internal_server_error
end
```

**Two problems:**
1. **Info leakage:** Echoing `e.message` to the client can leak SMTP server hostnames, auth errors, connection strings, stack hints. Compare to `omniauth_callbacks_controller.rb` which explicitly redacts.
2. **Silent server-side failure:** No `Rails.logger.error` call — the exception is swallowed server-side, making incidents undebuggable.

**Fix:** Log full exception with backtrace server-side; return a generic message to the client.

**Status:** NOT yet fixed.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Request spec: trigger StandardError in send_password_reset → response does NOT contain exception.message
- [ ] Request spec: verify Rails.logger.error was called with exception class + backtrace
- [ ] Apply same fix to admin_create rescue
- [ ] TDD red → green
close_reason: Fixed in PR #711. Rescue blocks log server-side, return generic message to client.

title: Fix broken visibility chain private/public/protected in registrations_controller
description:
**Location:** `app/controllers/users/registrations_controller.rb:117-134`

**Buggy code:**
```ruby
private
def respond_with_error(message, status) ... end
public          # <-- applies to nothing; misleading
protected
def update_resource(resource, params) ... end
```

**Problem:** Bare `public` keyword sits between `respond_with_error` (private helper I added) and `protected` (Devise overrides). It applies to NO methods — but strongly misleads readers and opens the door for a future developer to add a method in that slot, accidentally exposing what should be a private helper as a public action.

**Root cause:** I introduced this when I added `respond_with_error` + used `private` → tried to restore `protected` after, fumbled the visibility chain.

**Fix:** Put `respond_with_error` under the existing `protected` block (it's a protected helper anyway). Remove the stray `public`.

**Status:** NOT yet fixed. **Self-inflicted.**
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Move respond_with_error under the existing protected section
- [ ] Remove the stray public keyword
- [ ] Test: assert RegistrationsController private/protected method list matches intent
- [ ] All existing registrations_controller specs still pass
close_reason: Fixed in PR #711. Stray public keyword removed, visibility chain is correct.

title: Use update_columns in send_password_reset to avoid validation blocking admin recovery
description:
**Location:** `app/controllers/users_controller.rb:250` (in `send_password_reset` action)

**Buggy code:**
```ruby
raw, hashed = Devise.token_generator.generate(User, :reset_password_token)
user.update!(reset_password_token: hashed, reset_password_sent_at: Time.current)
```

**Problem:** `update!` runs full ActiveRecord validations. If the target user record has any pre-existing validation failure (e.g. name exceeds current `Settings.input_limits.user_name`, or an old email that now fails a stricter format validator), the admin's attempt to generate a reset link raises `ActiveRecord::RecordInvalid` — blocking an unrelated admin recovery flow. Devise's own `send_reset_password_instructions` uses `save(validate: false)` internally for exactly this reason.

**Fix:** Use `update_columns` (skips validations AND callbacks; token writes carry no business logic).

**Status:** NOT yet fixed.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Request spec: admin resets password for user whose name is too long for current validators → succeeds
- [ ] Request spec: verify reset_password_token and reset_password_sent_at are updated
- [ ] Replace update! with update_columns
- [ ] Add code comment explaining Devise parity
- [ ] TDD red → green
close_reason: Fixed in PR #711. send_password_reset uses update_columns to skip validations.

title: User profile: show authentication method, linked providers, and session login method

Profile has no visibility into account linking status and no ability to unlink. Need to: (1) track which auth method was used for the current session, (2) show linked providers in profile, (3) show link/unlink controls in future.

title: Restore automated tag-triggered releases with GitHub App token, git-cliff, and SHA-pinned actions
description: Will removed release.yml (604d808) because GITHUB_TOKEN cannot push CHANGELOG.md to branch-protected master. The proper fix is a GitHub App token that bypasses branch protection with minimal scoped permissions. This restores automated releases without manual GitHub UI clicks, while following OpenSSF post-tj-actions security guidance.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Create GitHub App (vulcan-release-bot) with contents:write permission only on mitre/vulcan
- [ ] Add app to master branch protection "bypass required pull requests" list
- [ ] Store RELEASE_APP_ID and RELEASE_APP_PRIVATE_KEY as repo secrets
- [ ] Create .github/workflows/release.yml triggered on push tags v*
- [ ] Pin actions/checkout, actions/create-github-app-token, softprops/action-gh-release to full commit SHAs
- [ ] git-cliff generates CHANGELOG.md, commits to master via app token
- [ ] Release created with changelog body via softprops/action-gh-release
- [ ] Workflow ignores commits from vulcan-release-bot[bot] to prevent loops
- [ ] CodeQL scanning enabled on workflow files
- [ ] Test with a v0.0.0-test tag on a non-protected branch first
notes:
**Why:** Manual releases via GitHub UI are error-prone and don't generate changelogs automatically. Tag-triggered automation with git-cliff was working but broke on branch protection. GitHub App tokens are the industry-standard solution (used by GitLab, Semantic Release, etc).

**Security context:** tj-actions supply chain attack (March 2025) compromised 23K repos. OpenSSF recommends: pin to SHA, minimal permissions, no PATs, OIDC for cloud credentials.

title: API token auth for programmatic component creation
description:
Add stateless API token authentication for programmatic access.

## Current State
- Only session-cookie + CSRF auth exists (browser-oriented)
- docs/api/ describes Bearer token auth that does NOT exist
- Only API namespace endpoint: GET /api/search/global
- Creating components/projects requires cookie dance (sign_in → get CSRF → POST)

## Needed For
- Programmatic component creation (scripting, CI/CD)
- Working on Vulcan's own STIG via API
- External tool integration

## Approach Options
1. Rails API tokens (Rails 8 has built-in token auth)
2. Devise token_authenticatable (deprecated but simple)
3. doorkeeper gem (full OAuth2 — likely overkill)

## Minimum Viable
- Personal access tokens stored in users table
- Token auth via Authorization header on /api/ namespace
- CRUD endpoints for projects and components under /api/v1/
close_reason: Superseded by v2-pat card with full GitLab/Discourse-informed design. Original card had incorrect approach (tokens in users table, doorkeeper consideration). New card covers: dedicated PersonalAccessToken model, SHA-256 digest storage, IP allowlisting, auto-revocation, scopes, rack-attack throttling.

title: Infrastructure hardening: CSP, rack-attack, input limits
description:
Infrastructure-level security hardening.

## Work Items

1. **CSP headers** — Add Content-Security-Policy via Rails initializer. Restrict script-src, style-src, img-src. Prevent XSS even if sanitization fails.

2. **rack-attack rate limiting** — Install rack-attack gem. Throttle:
   - Login attempts: 5/minute per IP
   - API requests: 300/minute per user
   - File uploads: 10/minute per user
   - Account lockout integration (complement Devise lockable)

3. **Input length limits** — Add max length validations to model text fields:
   - title, name, description: 1000 chars
   - fixtext, vuln_discussion, check content: 10,000 chars
   - inspec_control_body: 50,000 chars
   - vendor_comments, artifact_description: 5,000 chars

4. **Content-type validation** — Check MIME type/extension on upload endpoints:
   - XCCDF: must be .xml with text/xml or application/xml
   - JSON Archive: must be .zip with application/zip
   - Spreadsheet: must be .xlsx/.xls/.csv with matching MIME

## Files
- config/initializers/content_security_policy.rb (new)
- config/initializers/rack_attack.rb (new)
- Gemfile (rack-attack)
- app/models/ (length validations)
- app/controllers/ (content-type checks)

## Tests
- Spec: CSP header present in responses
- Spec: rate limiting triggers 429 after threshold
- Spec: oversized text fields rejected
- Spec: wrong content-type upload rejected
notes:
## Status Update (2026-02-23)

### DONE:
1. ✅ rack-attack rate limiting — login (5/min/IP + email), uploads (10/min/IP), fully tested
2. ✅ Project/Component length validations — name(255), description(5000), prefix(10), title(500)

### REMAINING:
3. ⏳ CSP headers — file exists but commented out, not enforced
4. ⏳ Rule text field length limits — title, fixtext, vuln_discussion, check_content, vendor_comments, artifact_description, inspec_control_body
5. ⏳ Content-type validation on upload endpoints — XCCDF(.xml), JSON Archive(.zip), Spreadsheet(.xlsx/.csv)
close_reason: All 4 items complete: rack-attack (prior), CSP headers, length validations, content-type validation (UploadValidatable). 46 tests, commit 82c8c0e

title: Add system/E2E tests for mitigations/POA&M toggle behavior
description:
## v2.x Worktree: E2E Tests for Mitigations/POA&M Toggle

### Context
Capybara and Selenium are in the Gemfile but `spec/system/` is empty — no system/E2E tests exist in v2.x. The mitigations/POA&M XOR toggle logic is covered at the unit level (composable + component) but there's no browser-level integration test.

### Tests Needed
- User clicks "Mitigations Available" toggle ON → mitigations textarea and mitigation_control appear
- User clicks "Mitigations Available" toggle OFF → fields disappear, POA&M toggle appears
- User clicks "POA&M Available" toggle ON → POA&M textarea appears
- XOR enforcement: enabling mitigations hides POA&M toggle entirely
- Data inconsistency guard: both flags true → mitigations path takes precedence
- Test with ADNM status (basic mode) and AC status (advanced mode)
- Verify tooltips render on hover for all DISA metadata fields

### Coverage Gap
- `useRuleFormFields` composable (129 tests) → produces correct `displayed` list per status/mode
- `DisaRuleDescriptionForm` component (19 tests) → toggle visibility logic works
- **Missing**: Full browser integration wiring both layers together in `UnifiedRuleForm`

- `app/javascript/composables/ruleFieldConfig.js`

title: Backup: optionally include base SRG in JSON Archive
description:
## Goal
Make JSON Archive backups self-contained by optionally including the base SRG XML. On restore, auto-import any missing SRGs before building components — eliminates the "Required SRG not found" error.

## Current Behavior
- Archive stores only `based_on: { srg_id, title, version }` in component.json
- Restore fails if target Vulcan doesn't have the matching SRG uploaded
- User must manually upload SRGs before restoring

## Desired Behavior
- Export: toggleable "Include base SRGs" checkbox (default: true) in backup mode
- Archive gets `srgs/` directory with one XML file per unique SRG used by components
- Import: auto-detect `srgs/` in archive, import missing SRGs before component creation
- Preview (dry-run): show "N SRGs will be imported" in summary
- If SRG already exists on target, skip it (no duplicates)

## Archive Structure Change
```
manifest.json          (add srgs[] array)
project.json
srgs/                  (NEW — only when include_srg=true)
  GPOS_SRG-V3R3.xml
  Web_Server_SRG-V4R4.xml
components/
  ComponentName-V1R1/
    component.json
    rules.json
    satisfactions.json
    reviews.json
```

manifest.json addition:
```json
{
  "srgs": [
    { "srg_id": "SRG-OS-...", "title": "...", "version": "V3R3", "filename": "GPOS_SRG-V3R3.xml" }
  ]
}
```

## Implementation

### Phase 1: Export — Include SRG XML in Archive

**Files:**
- `app/services/export/formatters/json_archive_formatter.rb` — write `srgs/` dir
- `app/services/export/serializers/backup_serializer.rb` — add `srg_manifest_entries` method
- `app/javascript/components/shared/ExportModal.vue` — add `includeSrg` checkbox
- `app/controllers/projects_controller.rb` — pass `include_srg` param

**Tests (TDD):**
1. Formatter writes srgs/ dir when include_srg: true
2. Formatter skips srgs/ dir when include_srg: false
3. Deduplicates SRGs (2 components same SRG = 1 XML file)
4. manifest.json includes srgs[] array
5. ExportModal shows "Include base SRGs" checkbox in backup mode
6. ExportModal sends includeSrg param

### Phase 2: Import — Auto-Import Missing SRGs

**Files:**
- `app/services/import/json_archive_importer.rb` — add SRG import step before components
- `app/services/import/json_archive/srg_importer.rb` — NEW: reads srgs/ from archive, imports missing
- `app/services/import/json_archive/manifest_validator.rb` — skip SRG error if archive has srgs/

**Tests (TDD):**
1. Auto-imports missing SRG from archive before component creation
2. Skips SRG import when SRG already exists (exact srg_id+version match)
3. Summary includes srg_count for imported SRGs
4. Dry-run reports SRGs that would be imported
5. Handles archive without srgs/ dir (backward compatible)
6. ManifestValidator: no error for missing SRG when archive includes it

### Phase 3: Frontend — Preview and Restore UX

**Files:**
- `app/javascript/components/project/RestoreBackupModal.vue` — show SRG import info in preview
- Tests for RestoreBackupModal

**Tests:**
1. Preview shows "N SRGs will be imported" when archive has SRGs
2. Preview shows nothing about SRGs when archive has none (backward compat)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Export with "Include base SRGs" produces archive with srgs/ directory
- [ ] Export without toggle produces archive without srgs/ (backward compatible)
- [ ] Restore on instance missing the SRG auto-imports it
- [ ] Restore on instance that already has the SRG skips it
- [ ] Dry-run preview shows SRG import count
- [ ] Old archives (no srgs/) still restore fine
- [ ] Round-trip: export with SRGs → fresh instance → restore → all components link correctly
notes: [2026-02-18 21:47] SRG backup feature COMPLETE. Export includes SRG XML in srgs/ dir, import auto-imports missing SRGs before components. Both modals show SRG details in preview. NOT COMMITTED — waiting for vq5 DRY + UX polish.

title: Database backup and restore via admin UI or rake task
description:
As a Vulcan administrator, I need to backup and restore the entire database so I can recover from failures, migrate between servers, or clone environments.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Backup: pg_dump to compressed file with timestamp naming
- Restore: pg_restore from backup file with safety confirmations
- Access: Available via rake task (CLI) and optionally via admin UI
- Safety: Restore requires confirmation, warns about data loss
- Scheduling: Document cron-based automated backup pattern
- Storage: Configurable backup directory (local or mounted volume)

## Implementation Options
1. Rake tasks: `rails db:backup` and `rails db:restore[filename]`
2. Admin UI: Download/upload backup files through browser
3. Docker: Volume-based backup via docker compose commands

## Key Considerations
- PostgreSQL pg_dump/pg_restore are the standard tools
- Must handle large databases (many Components with full rule sets)
- Backup should include uploaded files (SRG/STIG XML) if stored in DB
- Document backup strategy for each deployment type (Docker, Heroku, bare metal)
notes: User stories: docs/development/data-management-user-stories.md (story 10)

title: XCCDF Component backup/restore: full-fidelity export and re-import
description:
As a Vulcan administrator, I need to export a Component as full-fidelity XCCDF XML (all rules, all statuses, all metadata) and re-import it into the same or different Vulcan instance for backup, migration, or disaster recovery.

## Why XCCDF over CSV/Excel
- XCCDF is a well-defined NIST schema — less fragile than CSV column ordering or Excel formatting
- Already the native data format for STIGs/SRGs — Vulcan has mature XCCDF parsers
- Preserves hierarchical structure (rule → check → fix → metadata) that flat formats lose
- Schema-validatable — can verify export integrity before import

## Current State
- XCCDF export EXISTS but is "Published STIG" mode: AC-only, satisfied-by excluded
- No XCCDF import path for Components (only SRGs and STIGs can be imported as XCCDF)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Export: "Backup XCCDF" mode that includes ALL rules, ALL statuses, ALL metadata
- Export: Includes NYD, NA, AIM, ADNM — nothing filtered
- Export: Preserves satisfaction relationships, vendor comments, InSpec control body
- Import: Re-import a backup XCCDF to create a new Component (or update existing)
- Import: All fields round-trip cleanly (export → import → export produces identical XML)
- Import: Works across Vulcan instances (backup from instance A, restore to instance B)

## Key Files
- app/lib/xccdf/ — existing XCCDF parsers
- app/helpers/export_helper.rb — XCCDF export logic
- app/controllers/components_controller.rb — export action

## Dependencies
- Does NOT block or depend on DISA export work (sy4, 271, yuu)
- Separate export mode — "Backup" vs "Published STIG" vs "Vendor Submission"
notes: User stories: docs/development/data-management-user-stories.md (stories 5, 8)
close_reason: Superseded by vulcan-clean-3mh: JSON Archive backup/restore preserves 100% of data without XCCDF format limitations. XCCDF remains as published_stig format only.

title: Add frontend form validation for core input forms
description:
Add frontend form validation to core user input forms where backend validations exist.

## Context
User requested after model validation contracts were complete: "once we do this I think it makes sense to ensure we have at least minimal form input validation as well for the core user input forms where it makes sense."

## Scope
- NewComponentModal: validate name, prefix, title required before submit
- NewProjectModal: validate name required
- Login/Registration forms: basic field validation
- Membership forms: role selection validation
- Any other forms that map to validated model fields

## Approach
- Match frontend validation to backend model validations (DRY principle)
- Use BootstrapVue form validation (state prop, invalid-feedback)
- Don't duplicate complex backend validators — just required field checks
notes:
[2026-02-19] Research complete. Current state:
- NewProjectModal: manual guards + toast, no real-time feedback
- NewComponentModal: manual guards + toast, 4 conditional checks
- UpdateProjectDetailsModal: browser-native `required` only, no validation
- UpdateComponentDetailsModal: browser-native `required` only, no validation
- FormFeedbackMixin exists (used by RuleForm) but NOT used by modals
- No Vuelidate/VeeValidate installed

Plan for next session:
1. Add `vuelidate@0.7.7` (shared with ibo task)
2. Create validation composable/mixin matching model validations:
   - Project: name required
   - Component: name, prefix (AAAA-00 pattern), title required
   - Membership: role inclusion
3. Apply BootstrapVue `:state` + `<b-form-invalid-feedback>` pattern
4. Replace manual toast guards with Vuelidate inline validation
5. TDD: Vitest specs for each modal's validation behavior
6. Use @test/testHelper, mount with attachTo for modal tests

Key files:
- app/javascript/components/projects/NewProjectModal.vue
- app/javascript/components/projects/UpdateProjectDetailsModal.vue

title: Go CLI: Support DB_SUFFIX for worktree database isolation
description:
## Problem

The Go CLI has hardcoded database names that don't respect the `DB_SUFFIX` env var used for worktree isolation. Running `vulcan db backup` in a v3.x worktree would target the wrong database.

## Hardcoded Locations

| File | Line(s) | Hardcoded Name | Context |
|------|---------|----------------|---------|
| `cli/cmd/db.go` | 343, 416 | `vulcan_postgres_production` | backup/restore prod |
| `cli/cmd/db.go` | 347, 419, 541 | `vulcan_vue_development` | backup/restore/snapshot dev |
| `cli/cmd/viper_config.go` | 209 | `vulcan_development` | database.name default |
| `cli/cmd/setup.go` | env template | `vulcan_postgres_production` | production DATABASE_URL |

## Approach

Use Viper config to read `DB_SUFFIX` from environment (already bound via `VULCAN_` prefix or direct env). Build database names dynamically:

1. Add `database.suffix` to Viper defaults (reads `DB_SUFFIX` from env)
2. In `db.go`, construct names as `base_name + suffix` instead of hardcoded strings
3. In `setup.go` `writeEnvFile()`, include `DB_SUFFIX` in the generated `.env` template (commented out with explanation)
4. Update `viper_config.go` default for `database.name` to append suffix

Production deployments don't need suffix (each has own PostgreSQL), so only apply to development context.

## Testing

- Write tests for database name construction with/without suffix
- Verify `vulcan db backup` uses correct database in suffixed worktree
- Verify setup wizard includes DB_SUFFIX documentation in generated .env

title: InSpec import: no path to import existing profiles
description:
InSpec profiles can be exported (Component, Project) but cannot be imported. If a security team has an existing InSpec profile they want to bring into Vulcan, there's no path.

## Scope
- Parse InSpec control files from a ZIP or directory
- Extract: control ID, title, desc, impact, tag (check, fix, cci, nist)
- Map to Vulcan rule fields
- Requires a base SRG (same as spreadsheet import)

## Complexity
Medium-high. InSpec control DSL parsing requires understanding:
- `control 'V-12345' do ... end` blocks
- `tag` metadata (cci, nist, severity, etc.)
- `describe` blocks for check content
- Free-form Ruby code in control body

## Approach
Consider using `inspec json` CLI to convert profile to JSON first, then parse the structured JSON. This avoids writing a Ruby DSL parser.

## Tests
- Import InSpec profile ZIP → creates Component with rules
- Round-trip: export InSpec → re-import → verify field mapping
- Handle malformed profiles gracefully
notes: User stories: docs/development/data-management-user-stories.md (story 9)

title: Severity override missing justification field
description:
## Correct Workflow (from user)

The severity_override_guidance field should follow an event-driven workflow, NOT a static displayed array:

1. Each rule inherits a **default severity** from its SRG requirement
2. When user **changes severity** from the SRG default (e.g., Medium → High), a **modal pops up** requiring justification
3. Justification is saved to `severity_override_guidance` field
4. If a justification exists, the `severity_override_guidance` form field is **conditionally shown** so user can edit it
5. If severity is reset to match SRG default, justification field hides (or prompts to clear)

## What Agent C Did (WRONG approach)
- Added `severity_override_guidance` to the static `displayed` array for "Applicable - Does Not Meet" status
- This is wrong — visibility should be driven by severity CHANGE, not by status
- Label typo fix ("Security" → "Severity") IS correct — keep that

## Implementation Needs
- Watcher on severity field comparing to SRG default
- Modal component for entering justification on severity change
- Conditional display of field based on whether justification exists
- Full requirements spec + TDD before any code
close_reason: Closed

title: Implement lazy InSpec control generation (hybrid approach)
description:
## Problem

Current `inspec_control_file` implementation relies on `after_save` callback which can be bypassed:
- Direct SQL inserts (migrations, seeds)
- Factory methods that skip callbacks
- Bulk imports using `insert_all`
- Explicit `skip_update_inspec_code = true`

When bypassed, rules have empty `inspec_control_file` and UI shows nothing.

## Solution: Hybrid Approach

Override `inspec_control_file` reader to fallback to on-demand generation:

```ruby
def inspec_control_file
  stored = super
  return stored if stored.present?
  
  # Fallback: generate on-demand
  generate_inspec_control
end
```

Benefits:
- Fast reads when cached
- Never shows empty (generates if missing)
- Works regardless of how rule was created

## Implementation Steps
1. Extract generation logic into `generate_inspec_control` method
2. Override `inspec_control_file` reader with fallback
3. Keep `update_inspec_code` for caching on save
4. Add comprehensive tests

## Context
Discovered in Session 176 when Project 4 rules showed empty InSpec tab despite having documentation data.

title: Improve left sidebar navigation with tree view / accordion pattern
description:
## Objective
Improve the left sidebar navigation (RuleNavigator) for better UX when navigating rules/requirements.

## UX Patterns to Research and Apply

### Hierarchical Navigation
- Parent-child relationships (CNTR-00-000050 → CNTR-00-001096)
- Clear visual hierarchy through indentation
- Tree view/navigator pattern

### Interaction Mechanisms
- **Accordion**: Vertically stacked items with collapse functionality
- **Exclusive open behavior**: One section opens, others close automatically
- **Collapsible/Expandable Content**: Toggle to reveal/hide nested items

### Progressive Disclosure
- Defer secondary information (children) to expanded state
- Reduce cognitive load on primary interface
- Default collapsed view for cleaner initial experience

## Files
- `app/javascript/components/rules/RuleNavigator.vue`
- May need CSS updates for indentation levels
- Consider extracting tree node component if complexity warrants

## Context
This follows the command bar and filter bar redesign work. The goal is a consistent, professional UX across the controls editing page.
close_reason: Completed: Collapsible tree with parent-child hierarchy, expandable nodes, indentation, parent-before-leaves sorting

title: Bug: Adding Also Satisfies rules resets parent status to Not Yet Determined
description:
When editing a rule:
1. Set status to "Applicable - Configurable"
2. Add "Also Satisfies" rules (e.g., ABCD-11-000010 // APSC-DV-000160)
3. Parent rule's Status resets to "Not Yet Determined" if not yet saved

Likely a Vue reactivity issue - adding satisfied_by relationships triggers something that resets the status field on the unsaved parent rule. Possibly in the RuleForm.vue or related component watch/computed logic.

Found during v2.2.2 live testing (Session 143).
close_reason: Fixed in v2.2.2: addSatisfiedRule/removeSatisfiedRule now update relationships locally instead of calling refreshRule which was overwriting unsaved local changes

title: Add organization consent/warning modal
description:
Implement organization warning/consent modal shown BEFORE login. Users must acknowledge before accessing authentication. **Requirements:** - Show BEFORE login (pre-authentication) - Configurable enable/disable flag - Configurable content (markdown source) - Markdown rendering with Bootstrap typography - Only shown once per browser (localStorage with version) **Configuration (Rails Settings/ENV):** ```yaml # config/settings.yml consent_banner: enabled: true version: 1 # Increment to re-prompt all users content: | ## System Access Warning You are accessing a U.S. Government information system... By continuing, you acknowledge that you have read and understand... ``` **Architecture (API → Store → Composable → Component):** ``` API Layer: apis/settings.api.ts - fetchConsentBanner() -> { enabled, version, content } Store Layer: stores/settings.store.ts - consentBanner state { enabled, version, content } - fetchConsentBanner() action Composable: composables/useConsentBanner.ts - hasAcknowledged: Ref - acknowledge(): void - Checks localStorage: vulcan-consent-v{version} Component: components/shared/ConsentModal.vue - Uses marked + DOMPurify (already installed!) - Bootstrap 5 modal with backdrop="static" - Bootstrap typography classes for markdown styles App.vue: - Show ConsentModal before AuthHeader - v-if="consentEnabled && !hasAcknowledged" ``` **Markdown Rendering (Using Existing Dependencies):** ```typescript import { marked } from 'marked' import DOMPurify from 'dompurify' const htmlContent = computed(() => { const raw = marked.parse(props.markdownContent) as string return DOMPurify.sanitize(raw) }) ``` **Bootstrap Typography Styling:** ```vue
``` **Implementation Steps (TDD):** 1. Backend: Add consent_banner to settings.yml 2. Backend: Add settings#consent_banner endpoint 3. API tests: fetchConsentBanner() (RED → GREEN) 4. Store tests: consentBanner state/actions (RED → GREEN) 5. Composable tests: useConsentBanner localStorage logic (RED → GREEN) 6. Component tests: ConsentModal markdown rendering (RED → GREEN) 7. Integration: Add to App.vue 8. Manual test: Verify modal blocks access, localStorage works **Benefits of marked + DOMPurify:** - Already installed (no new dependencies) - Industry standard (used by GitHub, Stack Overflow) - DOMPurify prevents XSS attacks - Full control over rendering options **Questions Answered:** ✅ When: Before login (pre-auth) ✅ Content: Markdown in settings.yml ✅ Styling: Bootstrap 5 typography ✅ Storage: localStorage with version key ✅ Re-prompt: Increment version number in settings ✅ Stack: marked + DOMPurify (already installed)
close_reason: Consent banner complete with Reka UI accessibility and banner API consolidation

title: Add JSON response test coverage for all controllers
description:
## Problem

Most controllers lack JSON response test coverage. Tests only cover HTML format.

**Why this matters:** Session 110 caught the access request bug ONLY because we added JSON tests. Without JSON tests, we didn't catch that controllers were responding with HTML redirects instead of JSON, breaking the SPA.

## Current State

### Controllers WITH JSON tests ✅
- ProjectAccessRequestsController (added Session 110)

### Controllers WITHOUT JSON tests ⚠️
All other controllers with JSON responses:
- ComponentsController (create, update, destroy)
- RulesController (create, update, destroy)
- ReviewsController (create, lock_controls)
- RuleSatisfactionsController (create, destroy)
- ProjectsController (create - BOTH, update - JSON_ONLY)
- MembershipsController (update - BOTH)
- UsersController (update - BOTH)
- StigsController (create, destroy)
- SecurityRequirementsGuidesController (create, destroy)
- Admin::UsersController (all operations)
- Api::FindReplaceController (all operations)

## Test Pattern (from Session 110)

Example test structure:

```
context 'with JSON format' do
  it 'creates resource and returns JSON' do
    post "/path", params: {...}, headers: { 'Accept' => 'application/json' }
    expect(response).to have_http_status(:created)
    expect(response.content_type).to match(/application\/json/)
    json = JSON.parse(response.body)
    expect(json['message']).to be_present
  end

  it 'returns error for invalid data' do
    post "/path", params: {...}, headers: { 'Accept' => 'application/json' }
    expect(response).to have_http_status(:unprocessable_entity)
    json = JSON.parse(response.body)
    expect(json['error']).to be_present
  end
end
```

## Test Coverage Needed

For each controller action:
1. Success case - proper status code (200, 201, 204)
2. Error case - proper status code (422, 404, 403)
3. JSON structure - message/toast/data fields
4. Content-Type - application/json header

## Priority Levels

P1 (High): Controllers used by Vue SPA pages
- ComponentsController
- RulesController
- ProjectsController
- MembershipsController
- UsersController

P2 (Medium): Admin and API controllers
- Admin::UsersController
- Api::FindReplaceController
- ReviewsController
- RuleSatisfactionsController

P3 (Low): Less frequently used
- StigsController
- SecurityRequirementsGuidesController

## Estimated Effort

~2-3 tests per action × ~25 actions = 50-75 new tests

## Dependencies

Should be done AFTER vulcan-clean-aj5 (fix HTML-only responses) so we're testing the correct behavior.

## Reference

- Session 110: spec/requests/project_access_requests_spec.rb
- commit cca76ff: Added JSON tests that caught the bug

title: Audit: Controller response pattern inconsistencies
description:
## Analysis

Comprehensive audit revealed response pattern inconsistencies across controllers.

## Findings by Category

### JSON_ONLY (Consistent, SPA-ready) ✅
These controllers only return JSON and are used by Vue SPA:
- ComponentsController (all CUD)
- RulesController (all CUD)
- ReviewsController (all operations)
- RuleSatisfactionsController (all CUD)
- Admin::UsersController (all operations)
- Api::FindReplaceController (all operations)

### BOTH Responses (Mixed usage)
Support HTML for direct access and JSON for SPA:
- ProjectAccessRequestsController (create, destroy) ✅ Session 110
- UsersController (update only)
- StigsController (destroy only)
- SecurityRequirementsGuidesController (destroy only)

### HTML_ONLY (Needs fixing) ⚠️
Tracked in vulcan-clean-aj5:
- UsersController#destroy
- MembershipsController#create, #destroy
- ProjectsController#destroy

## Inconsistent Controllers

### ProjectsController
- create: BOTH (HTML redirect + JSON)
- update: JSON_ONLY
- destroy: HTML_ONLY ⚠️
**Recommendation:** Make destroy BOTH for consistency

### MembershipsController
- create: HTML_ONLY ⚠️
- update: BOTH
- destroy: HTML_ONLY ⚠️
**Recommendation:** Make all BOTH for consistency

### STIGs/SRGs
- create: JSON_ONLY
- destroy: BOTH
**Note:** Asymmetric but acceptable (create via API, destroy from UI/API)

## Recommendations

### Short-term (P1) - vulcan-clean-aj5
Fix HTML_ONLY actions breaking SPA:
- UsersController#destroy
- MembershipsController#create, #destroy
- ProjectsController#destroy

### Long-term (P2+)
Consider API namespace migration:
- Move all JSON_ONLY controllers to `Api::` namespace
- Clear separation: HTML pages vs API endpoints
- Example: Api::ComponentsController, Api::RulesController

### Testing Gap
Many controllers lack JSON response tests:
- Only ProjectAccessRequestsController has JSON tests (added Session 110)
- Need JSON tests for all BOTH/JSON_ONLY controllers

## Reference

Full analysis in Session 111 agent a5f18af
Vue component usage patterns:
- UsersTable.vue → UsersController
- MembershipsTable.vue → MembershipsController
- ProjectsTable.vue → ProjectsController
- All rule editors → RulesController, ComponentsController

title: Dark mode misc polish — activity separation, status pill spacing, dropdown borders
description:
Title: Dark mode misc polish — activity separation, status pill spacing, dropdown borders

Description:
Final polish pass after all structural fixes are in. Catches everything that slipped through
the per-component cards. This is the "walk every page with Playwright one more time" card.

Research basis: Bootstrap 5.3 notes that standard .bg-* classes do NOT adapt to dark mode
(deferred to v6). Any component using .bg-light, .bg-dark, .bg-white directly will break.
Grep for these classes and replace with design system equivalents.

Specific known issues:
1. Activity feed items lack visual separation in dark mode
2. Status pill spacing inconsistent (gap between pills)
3. Dropdown menus may not have dark backgrounds
4. .bg-light / .bg-white classes don't adapt (hard white on dark page)

Files:
- Modify: Various components found during final sweep
- Test: Playwright full 18-page sweep in both modes

First failing test:
Playwright full sweep — any remaining visual issues in dark mode

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] grep for bg-light, bg-white, bg-dark in templates — replace with --vulcan-* equivalents
- [ ] Activity feed items have visible separators (borders or spacing)
- [ ] Status pills have consistent gap (Bootstrap gap utility or margin)
- [ ] Dropdown menus use --vulcan-component-bg in dark mode
- [ ] No remaining hardcoded white/light backgrounds visible in dark mode
- [ ] Full 18-page Playwright sweep — no dark mode issues remaining
- [ ] No regressions in light mode

Verification:
Playwright 18-page sweep in both modes — zero remaining dark mode issues

Decision points:
- New issues found during sweep: fix inline (< 5 min) or card separately?

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT close this card without FULL sweep evidence
- Do NOT use .bg-light — it doesn't adapt. Use var(--vulcan-secondary-bg) or var(--vulcan-tertiary-bg)

NOT in scope:
- New features or redesigns
- Mobile viewport

Before closing:
- [ ] 36 Playwright screenshots (18 pages x 2 modes)
- [ ] grep -r 'bg-light\|bg-white' in components/ returns 0 non-adaptive usages
- [ ] Re-read all Anti-patterns — none violated

Story points: sp:3
Estimate: 20 min
close_reason: Done. Estimated ~20 min, actual ~10 min. Final 13-page Playwright sweep in dark mode — zero issues found. bg-light/bg-white usages already adapted by application.scss dark mode overrides. All pages verified: login, projects, rule editor, triage (table + split-pane), SRG list/detail, STIGs, users admin, profile, API tokens, released components, diff viewer. 7 design system tests pass, build + lint clean. No code changes needed — foundation work handled everything.

title: Add JSDoc to API modules — document non-obvious functions
description:
Title: Add JSDoc to API modules — document non-obvious functions

Description:
Zero JSDoc across 67 exported functions in 11 API modules. Most are self-documenting
(getRule(ruleId) → GET /rules/:id), but several have non-obvious behavior that requires
reading the function body or tracing Rails routes to understand.

Reference documentation:
- JSDoc reference: https://jsdoc.app
- @param, @returns, @throws tags: https://jsdoc.app/tags
- Vue.js JSDoc best practices: https://vuejs.org/guide/typescript/composition-api.html
- ky API (for documenting response shapes post-migration): https://github.com/sindresorhus/ky

Priority functions to document:
- exportProjectData — builds URLSearchParams, returns URL string not response data
- createComponentInProject — accepts both JSON and FormData (dual usage)
- duplicateRule — uses POST to create route with duplicate flag (not obvious)
- adminDestroyReview — DELETE with request body (ky quirk: needs { json: })
- bulkTriageReviews — flat params, not wrapped (lifecycle action convention)
- restoreBackup vs importBackup — same route, kept for semantic clarity

Files:
- Modify: app/javascript/api/reviewsApi.js
- Modify: app/javascript/api/projectsApi.js
- Modify: app/javascript/api/componentsApi.js
- Modify: app/javascript/api/rulesApi.js
- Modify: app/javascript/api/usersApi.js
- Modify: app/javascript/api/tokensApi.js
- Modify: app/javascript/api/baseApi.js (document the wrapping convention)

First failing test:
Manual review — or ESLint jsdoc rule if enabled

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] All non-obvious functions have JSDoc with @param and @returns
- [ ] baseApi.js documents the wrapping convention and ky migration context
- [ ] Functions with dual usage patterns documented (createComponentInProject JSON vs FormData)
- [ ] Functions with surprising behavior documented (exportProjectData returns URL)
- [ ] DELETE-with-body pattern documented (adminDestroyReview, adminRevokeToken)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
yarn build && yarn lint:ci

Decision points:
- Enable eslint-plugin-jsdoc? Recommendation: not now, just add docs to priority functions

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT add JSDoc to self-documenting functions (getRule, deleteUser — waste of space)
- Do NOT document WHAT the code does — document WHY the pattern is non-obvious

NOT in scope:
- Enabling ESLint JSDoc enforcement rules
- Adding TypeScript types

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC checkbox — verify with evidence
- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated
- [ ] Run the exact Verification command — paste output
- [ ] git diff shows ONLY files listed in Files section

Story points: sp:2
Estimate: 10 min
close_reason: Done. Estimated ~10 min, actual ~8 min. JSDoc added to all 7 API modules: baseApi (module doc + normalizeResponse + mutationOpts + getCsrfToken + parseBody), reviewsApi (module doc + lifecycle convention + 10 non-obvious fns), projectsApi (module doc + exportProjectData + createFromBackup + restoreBackup alias + benchmark generics), componentsApi (module doc + createComponentInProject dual-mode + detectSrg + spreadsheet preview/apply + compareComponents), rulesApi (module doc + duplicateRule + satisfaction relationship + revertRule + getRulesPicker), usersApi (module doc + admin vs profile ops + unlinkIdentity flat params), tokensApi (module doc + show-once raw_token + adminRevokeToken DELETE-with-body). Self-documenting fns left clean per anti-patterns. yarn build + lint:ci + 2910 tests pass.

title: Improve progress-bar pending-segment legibility
description: CommentProgressBar renders the pending portion as the final segment in a neutral gray (matches the Pending pill). At a glance it reads as an unlabeled/unknown color. Add a tooltip/label or distinguish it so the largest segment is clearly identifiable as Pending. Cosmetic, found during v2-05f.11 verification.
notes: [2026-05-28] Root cause refined (Will spotted the gap in devtools): the far-right darker-gray is NOT the pending segment — it's the progress-bar-track background (--vulcan-bg-light) showing because the rendered segments sum to <100%. CommentProgressBar#barSegments boost/reduce conserves the SUM of raw percentages, and here statusCounts sums to 112 while total=113 (Pending 87 + concur 1 + concur_with_comment 19 + duplicate 4 + informational 1 = 112). One comment is in no rendered status bucket, so ~0.9% (1/113) is never drawn. Also: 'resolvedCount' shows 26 but resolved pills sum to 25 — same one-comment discrepancy. FIX OPTIONS: (a) reconcile statusCounts so every comment lands in a bucket (find the uncounted status — likely a NULL/other triage_status or an adjudicated-but-pending row); and/or (b) have barSegments normalize displayWidths to sum to exactly 100% (last segment absorbs remainder) so the track never shows. Prefer (a) since the gap reflects a real counting bug, with (b) as belt-and-suspenders. Plus original pending-segment legibility.

title: Add bulk-triage mode to TriageSplitView split-pane
description: Deferred from v2-05f.11 Files list. The split-pane is a single-comment-focus UX; adding multi-select bulk mode there (likely in the queue sidebar) is a distinct interaction. Reuse BulkTriageBar + submitBulkTriage. Follow-up to v2-05f.11.

title: Bulk triage: select-all across pages (filter-scoped)
description: Today select-all only covers the visible table page (25/comments). With large comment sets users cannot bulk-triage across pages from the table view (the by-rule accordion loads all, so it is unaffected). Add a filter-scoped bulk option (select all N matching the current filter); likely needs a filter-based variant of the bulk_triage endpoint rather than an explicit ID list. Follow-up to v2-05f.11.

title: [EPIC] Investigate Vue 3 compat migration for Vulcan v2.x — @vue/compat spike
description:
Title: [EPIC] Investigate Vue 3 compat migration for Vulcan v2.x — @vue/compat spike

Description:
Bootstrap-Vue 2.23 added @vue/compat support. Vue 3's migration build lets Vue 2 code run under Vue 3 with deprecation warnings, enabling incremental migration. Spike: swap vue → @vue/compat on a branch, test what breaks (vue-turbolinks, vue-multiselect, esbuild alias, 14 Vue packs), document the gap. If viable, migrate component by component. This is the MITRE OSS modernization path — separate from the Aesir Nuxt rewrite.

Files:
- See child cards (spike first, then migration tasks based on findings)

First failing test:
See child cards

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Spike branch created with @vue/compat swap
- [ ] vue-turbolinks compatibility assessed (14 separate Vue instances)
- [ ] esbuild alias configuration tested
- [ ] vue-multiselect 2.x compatibility assessed
- [ ] Vue 2 filter syntax usage audited ({{ | filter }})
- [ ] Deprecation warnings cataloged and categorized by effort
- [ ] Go/no-go recommendation documented
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
yarn build && yarn test:unit (on spike branch)

Decision points:
- If vue-turbolinks is incompatible, is removing Turbolinks viable? (4-8 hours per CLAUDE.md)
- If too many breaking changes, defer to backlog

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT merge spike to main — branch-only investigation
- Do NOT conflate with the Aesir Nuxt rewrite (vulcan-nuxt) — these are separate products
- Do NOT upgrade Bootstrap to v5 in this epic — one thing at a time

NOT in scope:
- Nuxt rewrite (Aesir commercial product)
- Bootstrap 4 → 5 upgrade (separate epic after Vue 3 lands)
- New features — this is infrastructure modernization only

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC checkbox — verify with evidence
- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated
- [ ] Run the exact Verification command — paste output
- [ ] git diff shows ONLY files listed in Files section

Story points: sp:8
Estimate: 45 min Claude-pace (spike only)

title: Add VitePress docs — sidebar collapse for nested requirements
description:
