title: Add progress indicator to split-pane nav (Screen 4)
description:
Title: Add progress indicator to split-pane nav (Screen 4)

Description:
Replace the simple pending count in the split-pane triage navigation with a richer display showing pending of total (e.g. 16 pending of 23 total) plus a compact inline CommentProgressBar. Gives triagers progress awareness while navigating the 2D queue without leaving the split-pane view.
Design doc: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-20-comment-review-stats.md (Screen 4)

Files:
- Modify: app/javascript/components/triage/TriageQueueNav.vue (replace pending-only text with pending/total + inline bar)
- Test: spec/javascript/components/triage/TriageQueueNav.spec.js

First failing test:
mount TriageQueueNav with status_counts { pending: 16, accepted: 5, declined: 2 }; expect text containing '16 pending of 23 total' and a CommentProgressBar instance

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Nav displays 'N pending of M total' where M is sum of all status counts
- [ ] A compact CommentProgressBar renders inline next to the text
- [ ] Bar uses the mini/compact variant appropriate for nav context
- [ ] Falls back gracefully to current behavior when status_counts is unavailable
- [ ] All work via TDD (failing test first)
- [ ] No regressions on existing tests

Verification:
yarn test:unit -- --run TriageQueueNav

Decision points:
- Whether the progress bar should replace the pending badge entirely or appear alongside it
- Text format: 'N pending of M total' vs 'N/M triaged' vs other wording

Anti-patterns:
- Do NOT duplicate progress bar rendering — import CommentProgressBar with compact variant
- Do NOT break the existing 2D queue navigation arrows or keyboard shortcuts
- Do NOT add a separate API call for this data — use status_counts already in the response

NOT in scope:
- Per-rule progress within the nav
- Animated progress updates during triage
- Expandable breakdown tooltip

Before closing:
- [ ] Re-read each AC checkbox — verify with evidence
- [ ] Re-read Anti-patterns — confirm none violated
- [ ] Run the exact Verification command — paste output
- [ ] git diff shows ONLY files listed in Files section

Story points: sp:1
Estimate: 5 min (Claude-pace)
close_reason: Deferred to follow-up. The split-pane sidebar (TriageRuleSidebar) already shows per-rule pending/total counts in the headers. Adding an inline progress bar to the nav would duplicate information that's already visible. Low priority.

title: CommentTriageModal: 'accept and edit' inline edit box for the targeted element
description:
**Surfaced 2026-05-02 by Aaron during PR-717 final review.**

When a triager hits "Accept with changes" (concur_with_comment) on a comment, the canonical action is: accept the comment AND apply the edit to the underlying element. Today this requires a context switch: triage → save → jump to the rule editor → find the right section → make the edit → save. Workflow takes 4-5 clicks across 3 contexts.

## Idea

Open an inline edit box in the CommentTriageModal scoped to the section the comment targets (review.section). Triager edits the actual element + records the triage decision in one combined action.

## Open design questions

1. Which sections are inline-editable? Text fields like `vuln_discussion`, `fixtext`, `check_content` are obvious. What about `status`, `severity`, `disa_metadata` (multi-field)?
2. How does this interact with section locks? If the section is locked, the edit box is disabled with the existing lock-tooltip pattern.
3. What does the audit trail look like? One combined audit_comment? Two separate audits (one rule-update, one review-triage) with shared request_uuid (the .14r work makes this clean)?
4. What's the failure mode? If the rule update fails (validator), does the triage decision still save? Or atomically both?
5. Do we need a "preview" or "diff" view of the proposed change?

## Possible scope phases

- Phase 1: text-only inline edit for the most common sections (check_content, fixtext, vuln_discussion)
- Phase 2: structured fields (status, severity)
- Phase 3: multi-field sections (disa_metadata)

## ACs (placeholder until design pass)

- [ ] Design doc written + reviewed
- [ ] Backend supports atomic triage + rule update
- [ ] Frontend renders inline edit box gated on review.section + lock state
- [ ] Audit trail captures both events with shared request_uuid
- [ ] Tests cover happy path + validator-failure rollback

title: Migrate 9 string-toast endpoints + drop AlertMixin string branch
description:
9 controllers still return bare-string toasts (`render json: { toast: 'X' }`):

- app/controllers/security_requirements_guides_controller.rb:90
- app/controllers/rules_controller.rb:71, :86, :136
- app/controllers/memberships_controller.rb:21, :51, :81
- app/controllers/users_controller.rb:86, :129

The frontend AlertMixin (app/javascript/mixins/AlertMixin.vue:45-53) has a special-case branch for `typeof toast === 'string'`. Migrating the 9 sites to render_toast (object shape) would let the branch be removed → simpler frontend handler.

Sized: 30-45 min. Each site is 1 line + minor format.json wrapper edit. No frontend tests should break; AlertMixin object-branch handles the canonical shape.

PR-717 .15 helper render_toast is already on ApplicationController so this is mechanical.
notes: Surfaced during .18 work, 2026-05-02. Mechanical follow-on; out of .18 scope.
close_reason: 16 sites migrated to render_toast (or inline canonical for multi-key). AlertMixin string branch removed. 2278/2278 backend, 2288/2288 vitest. Playwright+fetch confirm canonical shape end-to-end.

title: Move parked Task 31 migration out of db/ (prevent accidental discovery)
description: `db/migrate.task31-pending/20260430202813_add_inheritance_fields_to_base_rules.rb` is parked Task 31 work. Rails resolves `config.paths['db/migrate']` to `db/migrate/` only — the suffix dir is NOT discovered. But the location is a footgun: if a future engineer renames the dir to anything matching `db/migrate*`, it becomes live. Move under `docs/parked-migrations/` to make accidental discovery impossible.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] File moved from db/migrate.task31-pending/ to docs/parked-migrations/
- [ ] Cross-reference comment added to docs/plans/PR717-public-comment-review/31-inherited-requirements-workflow.md
- [ ] Old directory removed
- [ ] Spec covering db/migrate path discovery confirms only standard dir resolves

title: Add rack_attack throttles for PATCH /reviews/:id/* endpoints
description: `config/initializers/rack_attack.rb:35-62` only throttles `POST /rules/:id/reviews` with `action=comment`. A compromised author/admin credential could mass-update the triage queue (PATCH /reviews/:id/triage, /adjudicate, /admin_*). No rate-limiting on the 9 PR-717 lifecycle endpoints. Also bound `req.body.read` at L49 with length check before JSON parse to limit memory abuse.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] throttle('triage_writes/user', limit: 60, period: 60.seconds) on PATCH /reviews/:id/*
- [ ] throttle on DELETE /reviews/:id/admin_destroy (lower limit, e.g. 10/min)
- [ ] req.body.read bounded by length check before parsing JSON
- [ ] Test: 60 PATCH requests in 60s succeed, 61st returns 429
- [ ] Documented in ENVIRONMENT_VARIABLES.md if env-tunable

title: Unify Review row shape across UsersController/Component/Project #comments
description:
Three endpoints emit Review row hashes with different field sets:
- UsersController#comments (users_controller.rb:280-296): project_id, project_name, component_id, component_name, latest_activity_at — NO duplicate_of_review_id, NO triage_set_at
- ComponentController#comments (component.rb:647-663): triage_set_at, duplicate_of_review_id — NO latest_activity_at
- ProjectController#comments (project.rb:162-177): component_id, component_name, triage_set_at, duplicate_of_review_id — NO project_id, project_name, latest_activity_at
Each consumer is bespoke. Extract a shared `Review.row_for_triage_table(latest_response: nil)` model method that emits the union shape with nil-padding.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `Review.row_for_triage_table(latest_response: nil)` model method emits union shape
- [ ] All 3 endpoints use it
- [ ] Frontend table consumers handle nil-padded fields gracefully
- [ ] Specs cover all 3 endpoints with the same row shape

title: Sanitize Content-Disposition filename in disposition export
description: `app/controllers/components_controller.rb:535` interpolates `project.name` and `component.prefix` into Content-Disposition header with no character sanitization. Project name is length-capped (project.rb:23) but no character constraint — embedded `"` or CRLF in the name would corrupt the header. Rack tends to strip CRLF but defense in depth.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Use `ActionDispatch::Http::ContentDisposition.format(disposition: 'attachment', filename: raw)` OR strip [^\\w.\\-] from filename
- [ ] Test: project name with embedded quote/CRLF/special chars produces valid header
- [ ] Test: legitimate project name unchanged in filename

title: Decide: admin_ prefix consistency for move_to_rule (rename or document)
description: `config/routes.rb:86-89` admin actions: `admin_withdraw`, `admin_restore`, `admin_destroy` use `admin_` prefix. `move_to_rule` is also admin-only but lacks the prefix. Decision: rename to `admin_move_to_rule` for symmetry, OR document the rule (e.g., `admin_` only on actions that have a non-admin sibling — withdraw/restore/destroy exist outside admin context conceptually; move_to_rule does not).
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Decision recorded
- [ ] If rename: route + controller action renamed; frontend axios.patch path updated; specs updated; old route removed
- [ ] If document: comment in routes.rb explains the rule (admin_ prefix only when non-admin sibling exists)

title: Rename TERMINAL_BY_RULE → HIDE_SAVE_AND_CLOSE_FOR (avoid backend collision)
description: `CommentTriageModal.vue:267` JS const `TERMINAL_BY_RULE = ["informational", "duplicate", "needs_clarification", "withdrawn"]` near-name-collides with backend `Review::TERMINAL_AUTO_ADJUDICATE_STATUSES = %w[duplicate informational withdrawn]`. The lists mean different things (backend: auto-adjudicate-on-triage; client: hide "Save & close" button) but the near-mirroring invites future drift. Also export TRIAGE_STATUSES from triageVocabulary.js as canonical list.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] JS const renamed to HIDE_SAVE_AND_CLOSE_FOR (or similar)
- [ ] Comment explains it differs from Review::TERMINAL_AUTO_ADJUDICATE_STATUSES
- [ ] triageVocabulary.js exports TRIAGE_STATUSES = Object.keys(TRIAGE_LABELS)
- [ ] Specs unchanged green

title: Have create endpoint return review payload (eliminate post-create refetch)
description: `reviews_controller.rb#create` (line 74) returns `{toast: 'Successfully added review.'}` with NO `review` key. Every PR-717 lifecycle endpoint returns `{review: hash}`. Inconsistent contract; consumers must refetch the page to get the new review. Adding `review: ReviewBlueprint.render_as_hash(review)` closes the gap and lets callers skip the refetch. Distinct from M5 (which canonicalizes the toast shape) — this is about adding the review payload.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] POST /rules/:id/reviews returns `{review: <ReviewBlueprint hash>, toast: ...}` on success
- [ ] Frontend can skip fetch() after successful comment post
- [ ] Test: response body includes review.id, triage_status='pending', section, etc.
- [ ] Existing 422 path unchanged

title: Remove FormMixin import from 4 non-axios-mutating consumers
description: 4 components import FormMixin but never call axios.patch/post/put/delete: ComponentCard.vue, RuleReviews.vue, NewRuleModalForm.vue, SecurityRequirementsGuidesTable.vue (DRY review surveyed all 32 consumers). 4 unused imports — small cleanup.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] FormMixin import removed from ComponentCard.vue
- [ ] FormMixin import removed from RuleReviews.vue
- [ ] FormMixin import removed from NewRuleModalForm.vue
- [ ] FormMixin import removed from SecurityRequirementsGuidesTable.vue
- [ ] mixins[] array updated in each
- [ ] yarn lint clean
- [ ] Tests pass for each affected component

title: Extract AuditCommentForm sub-component from CommentTriageModal
description: `CommentTriageModal.vue` — section-edit (35 lines, lines 30-64) and admin-actions disclosure (110 lines, 124-233) sub-forms each have an audit-comment textarea + Cancel/Confirm pair. The pair appears 2× verbatim. Extract `<AuditCommentForm v-model="comment" :prompt="..." :confirm-label="..." :confirm-variant="..." :disabled="..." @cancel @confirm>` with slot for action-specific body (typed-id input, RulePicker, etc.). Net −0 LOC but a ~75-line drop in CommentTriageModal cognitive load.

EXISTS query, (2) top-level reviews preload, (3) replies grouped by parent. 3 queries × N components. For a 40-component project export that's 120 queries. Becomes `Rule.where(component_id IN (SELECT id FROM components WHERE project_id IN (SELECT id FROM projects WHERE id IN (...) OR visibility=0)))`. Three nested levels.

A 50-reply thread = 51 audits + 50 + 1 SELECTs. For occasional admin fix-ups it's fine; flagged for production safety if Container SRG sees 50+ reply threads.

title: PR-717 Public Comment Review — post-merge remediation
description:
# PR-717 Public Comment Review — Post-merge review remediation

This epic tracks all 45 findings from the 6-agent specialist review of PR-717 (branch `feat/viewer-comments`, tip `50a01a9`). The review covered Security, Test quality, Migration safety, DRY/architecture, API consistency, and Performance.

## Tier breakdown (45 cards total)

- **Ship blockers (P0, 5 cards: .1–.5)** — must fix before merge to master. Data-correctness regression on legacy reviews, production migration lock-up risk, CSV/Excel formula injection, double-cascade incoherence, broken toast variant.
- **High priority (P1, 8 cards: .6–.13)** — auth gap on withdraw, audit trail gaps, json_archive validator bypass + silent FK loss + audit-laundering chain, 3 TIER-1 test rewrites.
- **Medium (P2, 9 cards: .14–.22)** — DRY refactors (audit_comment filter + toast helper), perf index, response-shape canonicalization, ReviewBlueprint expansion, vocabulary drift detection, audit_comment length cap, stray-param validator.
- **Low (P3, 23 cards: .23–.45)** — broken out by domain:
  - Tests (.23–.30): 3 TIER-2 DOM-binding gaps + 5 TIER-3 missing edge cases
  - Performance (.31–.35): redundant joins, deep-thread audit blowup, nested-IN flatten, counter cache, per-component memoization
  - DRY (.36–.38): Picker.vue (deferred until N=3), AuditCommentForm extraction, 4-file FormMixin import cleanup
  - API (.39–.42): create returns review payload, JS const rename, admin_ prefix decision, row-shape unification across 3 endpoints
  - Security (.43–.44): Content-Disposition filename sanitization, rack_attack throttles on triage/admin endpoints
  - Migration (.45): move parked Task 31 migration out of db/

## Execution order (by priority + within-tier prerequisites)

1. **P0 blockers first** (gate to merge): all 5 parallel; .1 + .4 need design decisions before code work.
2. **P1 high** (post-merge sweep): .6, .7, .8, .9, .11, .12, .13 parallel; .10 depends on .7 + .9 (audit-laundering = associated_with + insert!-validation).
3. **P2 medium** (dedicated cleanup PR): .14, .15, .17–.22 parallel; .16 (length cap) depends on .14 (require_audit_comment filter).
4. **P3 low** (backlog): .39 (create returns review) depends on .20 (expand blueprint); rest parallel.

## Decision points (need Aaron input)

- `.1` — legacy `reviews.triage_status` default: NULL+nullable+scope-fix vs `'legacy'` sentinel vs scope-by-comment-period-start
- `.4` — which side owns the cascade: Rails `dependent: :destroy` (FK becomes `:restrict`) vs Postgres FK (Rails callback removed)
- `.41` — `admin_` prefix consistency: rename move_to_rule or document the rule

## Validation references

- `git log master..feat/viewer-comments --oneline` — 139 commits + delta
- 2124 backend specs / 2276 frontend specs / 0 failures pre-remediation
- See `docs/plans/PR717-public-comment-review/00-SESSION-2026-05-01-roadmap.md` for the original-PR scope
- 6 agent review reports: archived under `.beads/archive/` if needed

## Plan

User intent (Aaron, 2026-05-01): "fix all issues through medium ... then we can see how and if we want to do the lows"
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] All 5 ship-blocker cards (P0) closed
- [ ] All 8 high-priority cards (P1) closed
- [ ] All 9 medium cards (P2) closed
- [ ] PR-717 merged cleanly to master
- [ ] No regression on the 2124+2276 test sweep
- [ ] Container SRG public comment workflow operating in prod
notes: [2026-05-10] PR #717 merged + released as v2.3.6. All P0/P1/P2 children closed. 21 P3 nice-to-haves remain (test branch coverage, post-merge refactors, shared component extractions). Demoting epic to P3 — maintenance mode, no critical work left.

title: Research: OrbStack + GlobalProtect VPN routing conflicts (MITRE dev env)
description:
**Context:** Extended debugging session trying to get local dev PostgreSQL working with OrbStack on a MITRE laptop with GlobalProtect VPN. Documents findings for future developers hitting the same wall.

## Findings

### OrbStack is closed-source
- Cannot inspect or patch the port forwarding proxy or DNS service
- https://github.com/orbstack/orbstack is issue tracker only, not source
- Only free for personal use, paid for commercial

### DNS cache bug (#2267 — open, no fix)
- When a container restarts, OrbStack's mDNS cache holds the OLD container IP
- `.orb.local` hostnames resolve to stale IPs even after container restart
- Workaround: assign static IPs in docker-compose (conflicts with multi-project dev)
- Enterprise users reported this since March 2026 — still open

### macOS 15 Local Network permission (#1452, #1620)
- Terminal apps need "Allow Local Network" in System Settings → Privacy & Security
- Without this, even correct OrbStack DNS fails with "no route to host"
- Common symptom: `nslookup` works but actual connection fails

### GlobalProtect VPN conflict
- GP dynamically adds routes for any 192.168.x.x subnet it sees traffic for
- OrbStack uses 192.168.x.x by default — GP captures and routes to VPN gateway
- Results in traffic to container IP going to MITRE corporate network instead of local bridge
- `netstat -rn | grep 192.168.167` shows the hijacked route via utun0

### Fix applied
- Configure OrbStack to use `198.19.x.x` subnet (not 192.168.x.x)
- OrbStack Settings → Docker → Engine:
  ```json
  {
    "tlscert": "~/.aws/mitre-ca-bundle.pem",
    "bip": "198.19.192.1/23",
    "default-address-pools": [
      {"base": "198.19.192.0/19", "size": 23},
      {"base": "198.19.224.0/20", "size": 23}
    ]
  }
  ```
- GlobalProtect does NOT claim 198.19.x.x (reserved for benchmarks RFC 2544)
- But GP will still claim any subnet OrbStack uses after restart → need to kill DNS cache

### Supplementary fix for scram-sha-256
- OrbStack's port forward proxy mangles PostgreSQL SCRAM-SHA-256 challenge-response
- Set `POSTGRES_HOST_AUTH_METHOD: trust` in docker-compose.dev.yml
- Mastodon, GitLab do the same for dev/CI
- Production uses docker-compose.yml which does NOT set trust
- Documented with link to issue #2267 inline in the compose file

**Why:** Save the next developer 4 hours of debugging when they hit this.
**How to apply:** Reference this card when onboarding v2.x devs on MITRE-issued laptops.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Research notes saved in this card for next dev hitting this
- [ ] OrbStack 198.19.x.x config snippet documented
- [ ] POSTGRES_HOST_AUTH_METHOD trust rationale documented with issue link
close_reason: Done: OrbStack VPN routing documented in docker-compose.dev.yml + port-registry.md.

title: Research: GitHub Actions supply chain hardening post tj-actions attack
description:
**Context:** Original release workflow (git-cliff + tag-triggered) was removed by will (commit 604d808) because GITHUB_TOKEN couldn't push CHANGELOG.md to branch-protected master. We researched the proper industry-standard fix.

## Findings

### tj-actions supply chain attack (March 2025)
- CVE-2025-30066: compromised `tj-actions/changed-files` impacted ~23,000 repos including Coinbase (70K customers)
- Attacker retroactively modified version tags to point to malicious commits
- Tags (`@v1`, `@v2`) are MUTABLE; SHAs are not
- OpenSSF guidance post-attack: pin ALL actions to full commit SHAs

### GitHub App token pattern (industry standard)
- GitLab, Semantic Release, and others use this approach
- Create a GitHub App scoped to the repo with `contents: write`
- Add app to branch protection "Allow specified actors to bypass required pull requests"
- Store `app-id` and `private-key` as secrets
- Use `actions/create-github-app-token@<SHA>` in workflow to mint a short-lived token
- App tokens are NOT tied to a human (PATs are) and are scoped to the specific repo

### Why this is better than PATs
- PATs require a human owner — attacker who compromises the human gets everything
- App tokens are scoped, short-lived, revocable
- GitHub auto-expires app tokens after 1 hour

### What NOT to do
- Never use `pull_request_target` trigger — runs with write permissions on fork PR code
- Never skip hooks (`--no-verify`) in CI
- Never use `@main` or `@master` refs for third-party actions

**Already tracked as:** vulcan-v3.x-8ij (separate card for implementing this)

**Why:** Document the security reasoning behind the planned release workflow restoration.
**How to apply:** Reference when implementing vulcan-v3.x-8ij.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Research notes saved
- [ ] Referenced from release workflow card (vulcan-v3.x-8ij)
close_reason: Done: all 26 GitHub Actions uses: are SHA-pinned across ci.yml, release.yml, docs.yml, dependabot.yml.

title: Research: OmniAuth provider lookup patterns, auto-link, nkf VM bug
description:
**Context:** Before implementing the auto-link feature we researched how major Rails apps handle OmniAuth provider conflicts and multi-provider linking.

## Findings

### GitLab
- Lookup by provider+uid FIRST, then email fallback
- `omniauth_auto_link_user` config setting (true / false / array of provider names)
- GitLab fork of omniauth-ldap was bumped to 2.3.0 (removes kconv dead require)
- Current GitLab: `gem 'gitlab_omniauth-ldap', '~> 2.3.0'`

### Discourse
- Uses separate `omniauth_identity` table (has_many :identities)
- Lookup always by (provider, uid) — never by email for security
- Auto-link is admin-approved, not auto

### Devise wiki
- Recommends the (provider, uid) → email fallback pattern
- Does NOT address the unauthenticated-user-with-matching-local-account case

### Vulcan's pick
- Single provider+uid on User model (not identity table — avoids migration)
- Global `VULCAN_AUTO_LINK_USER` setting (one ring)
- `email_verified` claim check for OIDC safety
- Human-readable error messages, clear UX

## nkf Ruby VM bug (#21967)
- Ruby 3.4+ VM crash in forked processes when nkf.so loads
- `gitlab_omniauth-ldap` 2.2.0 has dead `require 'kconv'` → loads nkf
- Fix: swap to `omniauth-ldap` 2.3.3 (original, no kconv), OR bump gitlab fork to 2.3.0
- We picked `omniauth-ldap` 2.3.3 because it's more actively maintained and has better thread-safety (option :mapping vs @@config)

**Links:**
- https://bugs.ruby-lang.org/issues/21967
- https://github.com/omniauth/omniauth-ldap
- GitLab docs on omniauth_auto_link_user

**Why:** Document the why behind the design decisions for future maintainers.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Research notes saved in this card for future maintainers
- [ ] Referenced from epic description or PR body
close_reason: Done: omniauth-ldap 2.3.3 replaced gitlab_omniauth-ldap in Gemfile. nkf/kconv fix landed.

title: Normalize PROJECT_MEMBER_ADMINS constant to array for consistency with siblings
description:
**Location:** `app/constants/project_member_constants.rb:9`

**Current code:**
```ruby
PROJECT_MEMBER_VIEWERS = %w[viewer reviewer author admin].freeze
PROJECT_MEMBER_AUTHORS = %w[author admin].freeze
PROJECT_MEMBER_REVIEWERS = %w[reviewer admin].freeze
PROJECT_MEMBER_ADMINS = 'admin'   # <-- singular string, plural name
```

**Problem:** Siblings are arrays. `PROJECT_MEMBER_ADMINS` is a scalar string with a plural name. Used in `user.rb`:
```ruby
project.memberships.where(user_id: id, role: PROJECT_MEMBER_ADMINS).any?
```
Works because ActiveRecord accepts a scalar. But any future caller who assumes array semantics (`PROJECT_MEMBER_ADMINS.include?(role)`) gets String#include? which is substring match, not membership check. Footgun.

**Fix:** Normalize to an array: `PROJECT_MEMBER_ADMINS = %w[admin].freeze`. Update any references that assume a scalar.

**Status:** NOT yet fixed. Consider whether in-scope for this PR or follow-up.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Change PROJECT_MEMBER_ADMINS to %w[admin].freeze
- [ ] Audit all references and update if scalar was assumed
- [ ] user.rb can_admin_project? still works
- [ ] Membership specs still pass
close_reason: Fixed in PR #711. PROJECT_MEMBER_ADMINS is now %w[admin].freeze (array, not scalar).

title: Use falsy check in UsersTable typeColumn to handle undefined provider
description:
**Location:** `app/javascript/components/users/UsersTable.vue:162-164`

**Current code:**
```js
typeColumn: function (user) {
  return user.provider === null ? "Local User" : user.provider.toUpperCase() + " User";
}
```

**Problem:** Strict equality with `null`. Rails `as_json` emits `null` for a nil column when the field is in `select`, so existing call sites are safe. But any future caller passing a user object WITHOUT the `provider` key (new endpoint, test fixture, v-bind with partial data) will hit `undefined.toUpperCase()` → TypeError. EditUserModal.vue's `providerLabel` uses `!user.provider ?` — the idiomatic pattern.

**Fix:** Use falsy check:
```js
typeColumn: (user) => !user.provider ? "Local User" : user.provider.toUpperCase() + " User"
```

**Status:** NOT yet fixed.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Vitest: typeColumn({provider: null}) returns 'Local User'
- [ ] Vitest: typeColumn({provider: undefined}) returns 'Local User' (does not throw)
- [ ] Vitest: typeColumn({provider: 'oidc'}) returns 'OIDC User'
- [ ] Vitest: typeColumn({}) returns 'Local User' (no provider key)
- [ ] Replace === null with !user.provider
close_reason: Fixed in PR #711. typeColumn uses falsy check, not strict === null.

title: Harden email_verified OIDC claim check against string 'false' from misconfigured providers
description:
**Location:** `app/models/user.rb:166`

**Current code:**
```ruby
if auth.info.respond_to?(:email_verified) && auth.info.email_verified == false
```

**Problem:** The comment above explicitly says "If the claim is absent, we trust the admin's decision. If explicitly false, refuse." Current code correctly lets `nil` pass (since `nil == false` is `false`). But providers that encode the field as string `"false"` (misconfigured OIDC attribute mappings, some SAML-to-OIDC bridges) would be treated as verified, bypassing the security check.

**Fix:** Use `ActiveModel::Type::Boolean.new.cast(...)` — it returns `nil` for absent/nil, `true` for true/"true"/1, `false` for false/"false"/0.

```ruby
email_verified_claim = auth.info.respond_to?(:email_verified) ? auth.info.email_verified : nil
if ActiveModel::Type::Boolean.new.cast(email_verified_claim) == false
  # refuse
end
```

**Status:** NOT yet fixed.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Test: auto-link with email_verified=true → succeeds
- [ ] Test: auto-link with email_verified=false (boolean) → refused
- [ ] Test: auto-link with email_verified='false' (string) → refused
- [ ] Test: auto-link with email_verified=nil → succeeds (backward compat)
- [ ] Test: auto-link without email_verified field → succeeds (backward compat)
- [ ] Use ActiveModel::Type::Boolean.new.cast for coercion
- [ ] TDD red → green
close_reason: Fixed in PR #711. Uses ActiveModel::Type::Boolean.new.cast for email_verified claim.

title: Log OmniAuth exception backtraces in all environments, not just development
description:
**Locations:**
- `app/models/user.rb:116-117` (from_omniauth rescue)
- `app/controllers/users/omniauth_callbacks_controller.rb` (all omniauth_*_error handlers)

**Buggy pattern:**
```ruby
rescue StandardError => e
  Rails.logger.error "Failed to create/update user from OmniAuth: #{e.message}"
  Rails.logger.debug e.backtrace.join("\n") if Rails.env.development?
  raise
end
```

**Problem:** Production incidents lose the backtrace. Production operators who need to debug an OIDC failure CAN NOT get the stack trace even if they crank log level to debug — the line is gated on `Rails.env.development?`.

**Fix:** Drop the `if Rails.env.development?` gate. `Rails.logger.debug` is already conditionally emitted based on the current log level, so adding the backtrace to debug output is safe — ops can enable debug logging in production when investigating incidents.

**Status:** NOT yet fixed.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Remove Rails.env.development? gate from all OmniAuth rescue handlers
- [ ] Verify backtrace logs at debug level in test env (Rails.logger.debug call happens)
- [ ] Same fix applied to user.rb:117 from_omniauth rescue
- [ ] TDD red → green
close_reason: Fixed: removed Rails.env.development? gate from user.rb:116 backtrace logging. All envs now log backtraces at debug level. Test added.

title: Sync ~/.claude/projects between machines for claude --continue
description: Sync session transcripts between two machines so claude --continue works on either box. Options: (1) rsync over SSH with aliases/hooks, (2) Tailscale + Syncthing for automatic sync. Need SSH access between boxes first. ~1.8GB initial sync, incremental after that.

title: XLSX export: add data validation dropdowns for Status and Severity columns
close_reason: Dropdowns implemented in excel_formatter.rb commit 4ab4fbb (Status, Severity, Source columns)

title: Force logout: admin session invalidation
description: Allow admins to force-logout a user by invalidating their session. Add "Force Logout" button to EditUserModal. Useful when a compromised account needs immediate session termination without waiting for timeout. Devise `sign_out` or token rotation approach.

title: Bulk user actions (lock/unlock/delete multiple)

title: Auto-compact automation: LLM-powered prepare/restore hooks
description:
Research and implement automated prepare-compact/restore-context for auto-compact.

## Problem
When auto-compact triggers, our /prepare-compact and /restore-context skills can't run automatically because hooks only support bash scripts for PreCompact and SessionStart events. Hook types "prompt" and "agent" (which support LLM reasoning) are NOT available for these events.

## Research Findings (Session 2026-02-16)
- GitHub issues: #14258 (29 reactions), #3537 (17 reactions), #17237 (7 reactions) — all open, no Anthropic response
- Hook type "agent" exists but NOT supported for PreCompact/SessionStart
- PostCompact hook does NOT exist (most requested feature)
- SessionStart:compact stdout injection is buggy (#15174)

## Best Workaround: mvara-ai/precompact-hook pattern
- PreCompact bash hook spawns `claude -p` subprocess with transcript
- Fresh Claude instance generates structured recovery brief
- Writes to file, no stdout injection needed
- See: github.com/mvara-ai/precompact-hook

## Other Tools Reviewed
- ContextRecoveryHook (claudefa.st) — script-based, no LLM
- claude-mem (thedotmack) — SQLite memory with LLM compression
- hookshot (CorridorSecurity) — Go typed structs for hooks
- Custom /compact instructions pattern (EmanuelFaria, #13572)

## Implementation Plan
1. Enhance PreCompact hook: capture state + spawn `claude -p` for strategic context
2. Enhance SessionStart:compact hook: inject recovery context + CLAUDE.md instructions
3. Test with auto-compact enabled
4. Consider contributing to #14258 with our findings

## Key Files
- ~/.claude/hooks/pre-compact-save-state.sh (existing)
- ~/.claude/hooks/post-compact-restore-state.sh (existing)
- Skills: /prepare-compact, /restore-context

title: v2.3.0 MIGRATION: Apply SRG hierarchy learnings from v2.2.x
description:
# SRG Hierarchy and ID Field Mapping

## Three-Tier Hierarchy

1. **CORE SRGs** (3 foundational documents)
   - Application Core SRG
   - Operating System Core SRG  
   - Network Core SRG
   
2. **SRGs** (product/platform-specific, derived from Core)
   - Each requirement references a Core SRG requirement via srg_id
   - Example: SRG-APP-000507 (from Core Application SRG)
   
3. **STIGs or Components** (implementation guides)
   - Each rule references an SRG requirement via srg_id

## Data Flow

CORE SRG → SRG → STIG
CORE SRG → SRG → Component

## Field Mapping in RuleOverview

### When viewing a STIG:
- **Rule ID**: SV-12345r1 (STIG rule identifier, version embedded)
- **Satisfies (SRG)**: SRG-OS-000001 (which SRG requirement this implements)

### When viewing an SRG:
- **Requirement ID**: [SRG requirement ID]
- **Core SRG**: SRG-APP-000507 (which Core SRG requirement this derives from)

## Important Notes

- Rule/Requirement "version" (r1, r2) is XCCDF metadata embedded in rule_id
- Separate version field is redundant and removed from UI
- srg_id field means different things in different contexts:
  - In STIG: Points UP to SRG requirement
  - In SRG: Points UP to Core SRG requirement
  
## Future Work

- Core SRGs may be added to Vulcan database for completeness
- Would enable tracing full lineage: Core → SRG → Implementation
- Currently Core SRGs are external reference only

Wire request_uuid into ActiveJob/rake middleware (when ActiveJob lands)
description:
When ActiveJob lands in Vulcan, wire the request_uuid correlation hook:

```ruby
class ApplicationJob < ActiveJob::Base
  around_perform do |_job, block|
    previous = Audited.store[:current_request_uuid]
    Audited.store[:current_request_uuid] = SecureRandom.uuid
    block.call
  ensure
    Audited.store[:current_request_uuid] = previous
  end
end
```

Same pattern for long-running rake tasks in `lib/tasks/`:

```ruby
namespace :stig_and_srg_puller do
  task pull: :environment do
    Audited.store[:current_request_uuid] = SecureRandom.uuid
    # ... work ...
  ensure
    Audited.store.delete(:current_request_uuid)
  end
end
```

The VulcanAudit#ensure_request_uuid callback (commit 193f630) ALREADY consumes Audited.store[:current_request_uuid]. This card is just the producer side: setting the UUID once per job/rake, so all audits in that job/rake share one UUID for forensic correlation.

Sized: 30-45 min once ActiveJob exists. No backfill needed for already-deployed rows — request_uuid only correlates from set-time forward.
notes: Forward-looking from .14r work, 2026-05-02. Consumer (VulcanAudit callback) already in place.

title: Document valid_password? hidden rehash side-effect at unlink call site
description:
**Location:** `app/controllers/users/registrations_controller.rb:95` (in `unlink_identity` action)

**Issue:**
```ruby
unless user.valid_password?(params[:current_password].to_s)
```

**Hidden side effect:** `User#valid_password?` is overridden in user.rb:56 to do bcrypt→PBKDF2 rehashing on successful login:
```ruby
def valid_password?(password)
  if encrypted_password.start_with?('$2a$', '$2b$')
    # ... verify with BCrypt ...
    update_columns(encrypted_password: new_hash, password_salt: new_salt) if result
  else
    super
  end
end
```

So calling `valid_password?` in `unlink_identity` has a hidden write side-effect when the user still has a legacy bcrypt password. Not a bug today — the rehash is idempotent — but:
1. Unlink would fail silently on a read-only DB replica
2. The write happens during what looks like a read/verify step
3. A future refactor might break the assumption

**Fix:** Add a code comment at the unlink call site explicitly referencing `User#valid_password?` override and its migration write. No code change needed today, but document the dependency.

**Status:** NOT yet fixed. Documentation-only card.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Add comment at registrations_controller unlink_identity password check
- [ ] Reference User#valid_password? bcrypt→PBKDF2 migration in the comment
- [ ] No code change required
close_reason: Fixed in PR #711. Comment documenting bcrypt→PBKDF2 rehash side-effect added at unlink call site.
